Origami is an art form that involves folding paper into various shapes and designs. One popular shape to create is a diamond. In this tutorial, we will show you how to make an origami diamond using a 6" by 6" piece of paper.

Step 1: Get out a 6" by 6" piece of paper.

To start, you will need a square piece of paper that measures 6 inches by 6 inches. You can use any type of paper, but origami paper is recommended for its thinness and durability.

Step 2: Fold the paper from left to right or right to left.

Take the paper and fold it in half from left to right or right to left. Make sure the edges are lined up and the crease is sharp.

Step 3: Fold the paper again, but this time from top to bottom, or bottom to top.

Fold the paper in half again, but this time from top to bottom or bottom to top. Once again, make sure the edges are lined up and the crease is sharp.

Step 4: Flip the paper.

Flip the paper over so that the folded side is facing down.

Step 5: Make another diagonal fold, this time bringing the bottom left corner of the paper to the top right corner of the paper.

Take the bottom left corner of the paper and fold it up to the top right corner of the paper. Crease the paper firmly.

Step 6: Make sure your paper is now split into triangles.

If you've done every step up until this point correctly, your paper should now be split into two triangles.

Step 7: Once you have found this line, you should also find a line going up and down, splitting the line in half, so there should be a left part and a right part to the horizontal line.

Locate the two lines on your paper that form a cross. One line should go from left to right, while the other goes from top to bottom. This will divide your paper into four equal squares.

Step 8: Identify the two triangles on your paper.

Notice the two triangles on your paper. One triangle should be on the left half, while the other should be on the right half.

Step 9: Notice that the paper has two flaps.

Take note of the two flaps on the paper. These will be used later on.

Step 10: Flip the paper over, then repeat the previous step.

Flip the paper over and repeat Step 8 and Step 9.

Step 11: Find the two parts of the diamond (not including the flaps).

Locate the top half of the diamond. This should be made up of two triangles.

Step 12: Fold the left part of the first part of this diamond to meet the center of the diamond.

Take the left triangle and fold it towards the center of the diamond. Crease the paper firmly.

Step 13: Flip the paper over, and repeat the last two steps.

Flip the paper over and repeat Step 11 and Step 12.

Step 14: Flip the "diamond" upside down and notice the two pockets on each side of the "diamond".

You should now have what appears to be a diamond shape. Flip the diamond upside down and notice the two small pockets on each side.

Step 15: Flip the paper over, then repeat the previous step again.

Flip the paper over and repeat Step 14.

Step 16: Blow gently into the bottom of the diamond, extending the top.

Carefully blow into the bottom of the diamond to puff it up, extending the top tip of the diamond.

Step 17: Finished.

Congratulations! You have successfully made an origami diamond. Display it proudly or give it as a gift to someone special.

Playing the trumpet, like any musical instrument, requires a certain level of dedication, but it doesn’t have to take years of practice to get started. With this guide, you can learn how to play the trumpet in just one day!

1. Go to your local music dealer and ask about student rentals.

Before you start playing, you’ll need to get your hands on a trumpet. You may be tempted to buy one outright, but it’s better to make sure you’re interested in playing first before investing in an instrument. Head to your local music dealer and ask about student rentals. They should have a variety of options that won’t break the bank.

2. While you're at the music store, you might also want to invest in a beginner's method book.

A method book will help guide you through the basics of trumpet playing, and often includes sheet music and exercises to practice. Ask the music dealer for a recommendation or look up reviews online to find one that works for you.

3. Don't immediately open up the case and start noodling around.

It’s easy to get caught up in the excitement of having a new instrument, but resist the urge just yet. Take some time to familiarize yourself with the parts of the trumpet and how they work.

4. Take out your mouthpiece and buzz into there.

Next, take out the mouthpiece and begin to buzz your lips together into the opening. This exercise will help you get used to the feeling of playing the trumpet and get your lips warmed up.

5. Insert the mouthpiece into the trumpet.

Place the mouthpiece into the trumpet and ensure that it is secure. Make sure to clean the mouthpiece between uses to prevent the buildup of bacteria.

6. Rest the fingers of your right hand on the valves (which are numbered one, two and three, one being closest to you and three closest to the bell), and keep your right pinky either in the air or on top of the hook near the third valve.

The valves of the trumpet are how different notes are produced. Rest your fingers on the valves and position your pinky accordingly.

7. It's time to learn your first scale, the concert Bb (B flat) major scale, also called the C major scale.

The concert Bb major scale is a common first scale to learn and is the foundation for many songs. Your method book will likely have a chart or diagram of the notes and fingerings needed to play the scale.

8. Gradually tighten up your mouth and use more air as you climb up the scale, as you can see that different notes are played with the same finger pattern.

As you practice the scale, focus on tightening your mouth and using more air to produce higher notes. It’s normal to feel tension in your mouth and breathing at first, but with practice, it will become more natural.

9. Start looking in your method book and work on some easier songs, such as Hot Cross Buns and London Bridge.

Once you’ve got the hang of the scale, it’s time to move on to some beginner songs. Practice reading the sheet music provided in your method book, or search online for easy trumpet songs to try.

10. Seek a private instructor if you want extra help.

If you’re struggling with a particular song or just want some extra guidance, consider hiring a private instructor. They can help you improve your technique and provide personalized feedback.

11. Practice for at least 15 minutes a day for at least five days a week.

Consistency is key when it comes to learning any new skill. Try to practice for at least 15 minutes a day, five days a week. With regular practice, you’ll see improvement in your trumpet playing in no time!

Playing the trumpet in one day may seem like a lofty goal, but with dedication, patience, and the right resources, it’s absolutely achievable. So go ahead and give it a try – who knows, you may have just found a hidden talent!

How to Write a Horoscope

Have you ever wanted to write your own horoscope? Perhaps you've always been fascinated by astrology, or maybe you're just looking for a new creative outlet. Whatever your reason for wanting to write horoscopes, there are a few things you'll need to consider. In this article, we'll explore some tips and tricks for crafting compelling horoscopes that are sure to delight and inspire your readers.

1. Build your horoscopes through research and attention to the position of the sun, moon and stars.

The first step in creating a great horoscope is to do your research. You'll want to pay close attention to the position of the sun, moon and stars, as these celestial bodies have a powerful influence on our lives. Consult astronomical charts and almanacs to stay up-to-date on celestial events.

2. Start each horoscope with the major tone of each house.

Each of the twelve astrological houses has its own tone or theme, which can help you establish the overall tone of your horoscope. For example, the first house represents the self, the second house represents finances and material possessions, and so on. Use these themes to set the tone for each horoscope you write.

3. Look to each sign's second and third houses for a deeper understanding of horoscopes.

In addition to the overall themes of each house, you'll want to pay special attention to a given sign's second and third houses. These houses can help you understand more about a person's values, communication style, and relationships.

4. Watch the movement of planets and stars to anticipate big changes in life.

As you're crafting your horoscopes, keep an eye on the movement of planets and stars. Pay special attention to any major changes that are on the horizon, as these can provide clues about significant shifts that might be coming up in your readers' lives.

5. Purchase a set of astrological charts for detailed, day-to-day horoscopes.

If you want to take your horoscope-writing to the next level, consider investing in a set of astrological charts. These can help you create day-to-day horoscopes that are more detailed and nuanced than you might be able to accomplish otherwise.

6. Allow yourself to write freely and poetically when composing horoscopes.

Horoscopes are meant to be poetic and inspiring, so don't be afraid to let your creativity shine through. Use vivid language and evocative images to paint a picture of what's in store for your readers.

7. Look at the major astrological events of the week or month to start writing horoscopes.

If you're struggling to come up with ideas for horoscopes, start by looking at the major astrological events of the week or month. These can serve as a jumping-off point for your horoscope writing.

8. Think about people you know in each sign.

As you're crafting your horoscopes, think about the people you know in each sign. What are their personalities like? What are their strengths and weaknesses? Use these insights to craft horoscopes that will resonate with your readers.

9. Avoid generalizations or very broad proclamations.

When writing horoscopes, it's important to strike a balance between specificity and universality. Avoid making overly broad statements or generalizations that aren't likely to be true for everyone born under a particular sign.

10. Avoid making grand proclamations every day.

While it's important to write inspiring horoscopes, it's also important to avoid making grand proclamations every day. Not every day is going to be a game-changer, so be realistic in your predictions and advice.

11. Read horoscopes for inspiration.

One of the best ways to improve your horoscope writing is to read horoscopes by other writers. Pay attention to what works and what doesn't, and use these insights to improve your own writing.

12. Know the basics of each solar house.

To write effective horoscopes, you'll need to be familiar with the basics of each solar house. Take the time to study these themes so you can create horoscopes that ring true for your readers.

13. Understand the adventurous Aries.

Aries is known for being adventurous, impulsive, and bold. Use these traits to inspire your Aries horoscopes.

14. Understand the warm and persistent Taurus.

Taurus is warm, persistent, and grounded. These traits should be reflected in your Taurus horoscopes.

15. Understand the intelligent Gemini.

Gemini is intelligent, curious, and versatile. Your Gemini horoscopes should reflect these qualities.

16. Understand the emotional Cancer.

Cancer is emotional, intuitive, and nurturing. Use these traits to inspire your Cancer horoscopes.

17. Understand the generous Leo.

Leo is known for being generous, confident, and ambitious. These qualities should be reflected in your Leo horoscopes.

18. Understand the shy, intelligent Virgo.

Virgo is intelligent, analytical, and sometimes shy. Use these traits to craft your Virgo horoscopes.

19. Understand the sociable Libra.

Libra loves beauty, balance, and harmony. Your Libra horoscopes should reflect these values.

20. Understand the passionate Scorpios.

Scorpio is passionate, intense, and sometimes mysterious. Use these traits to craft your Scorpio horoscopes.

21. Understand the good-humored Sagittarius.

Sagittarius is known for being good-humored, adventurous, and optimistic. These qualities should be reflected in your Sagittarius horoscopes.

22. Understand the practical Capricorn.

Capricorn is practical, ambitious, and hard-working. These traits should be reflected in your Capricorn horoscopes.

23. Understand the humanitarian Aquarius.

Aquarius is known for being humanitarian, innovative, and independent. These qualities should inspire your Aquarius horoscopes.

24. Understand the imaginative Pisces.

Pisces is imaginative, dreamy, and intuitive. Use these traits to craft your Pisces horoscopes.

By following these tips and taking the time to do your research, you can become a master of horoscope writing. Whether you're writing for fun or as a professional astrologer, your horoscopes are sure to inspire and delight your readers.

Local politics can have a huge impact on your community and it's important to be involved in the decision-making process. Whether you want to volunteer, support, vote, or even run for office, there are many ways to get involved. Here are some steps to take if you want to start making a difference in local politics:

1. Define your position on the issues and support the legislators who most closely represent your views. It's important to know where you stand on the issues that matter to you, whether it's healthcare, education, the environment, or something else. Research the legislators in your area and find out where they stand on these issues. If you find someone who closely represents your views, support their campaign and volunteer to help them get elected.

2. See what guiding principles are active before deciding which issues to support and what kind of changes are really the right changes. It’s important to understand the values that govern the political environment of your community before making decisions on what issues to support and how to create change.

3. Be a poll watcher which is an election voting observer. One way to get involved in local politics is to become a poll watcher during elections. This involves observing the voting process at polling places to ensure that it’s fair and free from fraudulent activity.

4. Participate in voting-place party meetings. Attend local voting-place party meetings to get a better idea of the issues being discussed and to get to know other people in your community who are interested in local politics.

5. Contribute to the local, county, or state elections in many ways, as you donate, post signs, stickers, talk, etc. Whether you can donate money, post signs, or just spread the word about an election, every little bit helps.

6. Become a better speaker or speech writer and practice on smaller groups before you move to larger responsibilities. If you want to be effective in local politics, it’s important to be a good communicator. Take public speaking and writing courses, and try practicing your skills in smaller groups before taking on larger responsibilities.

7. To run for political office, you must register with authorities for the office that you want to run for, then organize with a chairperson and treasurer and file these papers with the proper authority, learn about financial reporting, keep records and meet with interested people who can help or donate. If you want to run for office, you will need to follow the steps required by your state or local government.

8. Plan for campaign literature and a website: Design and get brochures, handbills, signs and posters. Having a strong campaign strategy is essential for any political candidate. Plan your literature and website design in advance so that you can get your message out effectively.

9. Be involved in local one-on-one by putting a personal face on the issues and talking with others to refine ideas, to guide and strengthen good values in your workplace, school, and home area – knocking on doors, registering new voters, phoning or helping with mailings. Talking to people face-to-face can be a very effective way to get your message out and build support for your cause.

10. Volunteer, support, and vote for the values most important to you. Whether you can volunteer your time, donate money, or simply vote, it’s important to support the values that matter most to you.

11. Don’t fear, even if the opposition is elected. Remember that even if the opposing party is elected into office, you can still fight for your values and create change through grassroots organizing and advocacy.

12. Don’t be disillusioned by politicians who may be romancing you and other members of some organizations and clubs to further their own influence, but not pressing for "real improvements." Be skeptical of politicians who don't seem to be genuinely committed to the values and causes they claim to care about.

13. Avoid breaking promises by not making promises for "changes" that are virtually impossible – things that you actually do not explain very well. Be honest with yourself and others about what you can realistically accomplish in your efforts to create change.

14. Live by what you say you believe, knowing that you will be judged accordingly by the electorate. Always act according to your values and principles, even when it’s difficult.

15. Tell everyone (give information to newspapers) that we need the right ideals. Spread the word about the causes and values that matter to you by contacting local newspapers, online media outlets, and other forums to share your message.

16. Ask for a petition to run for the county committee of your political party. If you are interested in taking on leadership roles within your local political party, ask for a petition to run for county committee. This can be a great way to gain experience and start building relationships in the world of local politics.

Diabetes is a chronic medical condition that occurs when the pancreas is unable to produce enough insulin to regulate blood sugar levels, or when the body becomes resistant to the effects of insulin. If left untreated, diabetes can lead to severe complications such as neuropathy, kidney damage, and blindness. However, with the right treatment plan and lifestyle modifications, it is possible to control diabetes and prevent these complications from occurring. Here are some tips on how to control diabetes:

1. Consult with a doctor to start or adjust your treatment plan.

If you have been diagnosed with diabetes, it is important to consult with a doctor to develop a treatment plan that is tailored to your specific needs. This may include changes to your diet, exercise regimen, and medication regimen. Your doctor can help you understand what steps you need to take to keep your blood sugar levels under control.

2. Act quickly if faced with any of the severe problems in untreated type 1 diabetes.

Type 1 diabetes is a medical emergency that requires immediate treatment. If you experience symptoms such as extreme thirst, dry mouth, confusion, and fatigue, seek medical attention right away.

3. Take steps to understand diabetes.

Education is key to managing diabetes. Learn as much as you can about your condition, including how it affects your body and what steps you can take to keep your blood sugar levels in the target range.

4. Take your medications every day.

If you have been prescribed medication to control your diabetes, it is important to take it as directed by your doctor. Skipping doses or adjusting your dosage without consulting your doctor can lead to complications.

5. Consider an insulin pump.

An insulin pump is a device that delivers a continuous dose of insulin to mimic the effects of the basal rate insulin. This option is best for those with type 1 diabetes, and may be recommended by your doctor if other treatments are not effective.

6. Exercise.

Regular exercise can help improve insulin sensitivity and lower blood sugar levels. Consult with your doctor before starting an exercise regimen and begin slowly if you have not been active recently.

7. Minimize stress.

Stress can cause blood sugar levels to rise, so it is important to find ways to manage stress in your life. Try meditation, deep breathing, or yoga to help reduce stress.

8. Avoid getting sick.

Illness can make it more difficult to control blood sugar levels. Stay up to date on vaccinations and practice good hygiene to avoid getting sick.

9. Modify your diabetes plans to account for menstruation and menopause.

Hormonal changes during menstruation and menopause can affect blood sugar levels. Discuss any changes in your symptoms with your doctor and make adjustments to your treatment plan as needed.

10. Schedule regular check-ups with your doctor.

Regular check-ups with your doctor can help identify any problems early and help you keep your blood sugar levels under control.

11. If you can, manage your diabetes with diet and exercise.

For some people, lifestyle modifications such as a healthy diet and exercise can be enough to control their diabetes. Follow a low-calorie, high nutrient diet and prioritize healthy carbohydrates like whole grains, fiber-rich foods, and lean sources of protein. Avoid foods rich in cholesterol and consume alcohol cautiously.

12. Be prepared to pursue more aggressive treatment options over time.

As diabetes progresses, more aggressive treatment options may be necessary. Discuss these options with your doctor and be prepared to adjust your treatment plan as needed.

13. Consider bariatric surgery if you are obese.

For those who are obese, bariatric surgery may be an option to help control diabetes. Discuss this option with your doctor.

14. Check your blood sugar every day.

Monitoring your blood sugar levels daily can help you identify any problems early and adjust your treatment plan as needed.

15. Take an A1C test several times per year.

The A1C test measures your average blood sugar levels over the past 2-3 months. It is recommended to have this test done several times per year to monitor your diabetes.

16. Test for ketones in your urine if you have ketoacidosis symptoms.

Ketoacidosis is a serious complication of diabetes that can occur when blood sugar levels are too high. If you experience symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, test your urine for ketones and seek medical attention immediately.

17. Receive foot and eye tests regularly.

Diabetes can cause complications such as neuropathy, kidney damage, and blindness. Regular foot and eye tests can help identify any problems early.

18. Always defer to your dietitian's advice.

A registered dietitian can help you develop a healthy meal plan that works for you and your diabetes. Always follow their advice and consult them before making any changes to your diet.

19. Use intelligent portion control.

Monitoring your portion sizes can help you keep your blood sugar levels under control. Use measuring cups and a food scale to ensure you are eating the appropriate amount.

20. Talk to your doctor before taking any medication for your diabetes.

Some over-the-counter medications can affect blood sugar levels and should be avoided if you have diabetes. Always talk to your doctor before taking any new medications.

21. Use insulin to control your blood sugar.

Insulin is a hormone that helps regulate blood sugar levels. If your body is not producing enough insulin on its own, you may need to take insulin injections or use an insulin pump to control your blood sugar levels.

22. Use oral diabetes medications to control your blood sugar.

There are several classes of oral diabetes medications that can help lower blood sugar levels. If your doctor prescribes one of these medications, it is important to take it as directed.

23. Consider supplementing your treatment plan with other medications.

In addition to insulin and oral diabetes medications, there are several other medications that may be used to supplement your treatment plan. Discuss these options with your doctor if you are having trouble controlling your blood sugar levels.

By following these tips and working closely with your doctor and healthcare team, it is possible to control diabetes and prevent complications from occurring.

Ulcers are a common digestive ailment that can cause pain and discomfort. While there are medications available to help treat ulcers, natural remedies can also be effective. In this article, we will explore a range of natural treatments for ulcers.

1. Eat antioxidant-rich foods

Antioxidants can help protect your body from the damage caused by free radicals and may also help reduce inflammation. Foods that are high in antioxidants include berries, nuts, and dark chocolate.

2. Look for foods that contain lots of flavonoids

Flavonoids are compounds found in many fruits and vegetables that may help reduce inflammation and promote healing. Foods that are high in flavonoids include citrus fruits, onions, and broccoli.

3. Drink cabbage juice

Cabbage juice has been shown to have a soothing effect on the digestive tract and may help reduce inflammation. You can buy cabbage juice or make your own by blending fresh cabbage with water.

4. Consume cranberry products

Cranberry is known to fight H. pylori, the bacteria that is often responsible for causing ulcers. Cranberry juice, dried cranberries, and cranberry supplements can all be effective.

5. Eat white sweet potatoes

White sweet potatoes contain compounds that have been shown to help reduce inflammation and promote healing of the digestive tract.

6. Use more honey

Honey has antibacterial properties that may help fight H. pylori. It can be added to tea or used as a natural sweetener.

7. Take licorice supplements

Licorice root has anti-H. pylori properties, so it can help heal your ulcer. Licorice supplements can be found online or in health stores.

8. Eat more bananas

Bananas are a good source of fiber and can help soothe and protect the digestive tract.

9. Switch from butter to oil

Butter can be hard on the digestive tract and may exacerbate ulcer symptoms. Switching to oil can be a good alternative.

10. Follow a bland diet

A bland diet that is low in spices and rich in fiber can help reduce inflammation and promote healing of the digestive tract.

11. Eliminate alcohol from your diet

Alcohol can irritate the digestive tract and may exacerbate ulcer symptoms.

12. Don’t drink milk for relief

While drinking milk can provide some temporary relief from pain caused by ulcers, it will also encourage your stomach to produce more acid, worsening your symptoms in the long run.

13. Avoid spicy foods

Spicy foods can be hard on the digestive tract and may exacerbate ulcer symptoms.

14. Don’t eat fatty foods

Deep fried foods, fast food meals, and other high-fat foods should be avoided.

15. Stay away from garlic

People who have ulcers, or who are prone to them, should avoid garlic because it can irritate existing ulcers and possibly cause new ones.

16. Quit tobacco products

Regular use of tobacco has been shown to slow or prevent the healing of ulcers.

17. Stop taking NSAIDs

Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) include aspirin, naproxen, and ibuprofen. These medications can irritate the digestive tract and may exacerbate ulcer symptoms.

18. Avoid stress

It is still thought that stress can aggravate ulcers and worsen symptoms, even if it has now been shown that stress does not cause most ulcers.

By following these natural treatments, you can help reduce inflammation, promote healing, and alleviate symptoms associated with ulcers. As always, if you are experiencing severe symptoms or have any concerns, be sure to consult with your healthcare provider.

Stickcraft3 is an exciting game that requires creativity and strategic planning. In this game, you will be required to create a stick figure character and battle against other stick figures on a battlefield. Creating the perfect character and equipping them with the right gear is crucial to winning battles. In this article, we'll teach you how to play Stickcraft3, from creating your stick figure to engaging in battles.

Part 1: Creating Your Stick Figure Character

Step 1: Attribute Scores
The first thing you need to do is get 10 points to divide up on four attribute scores: strength, reflexes, wits, and cheek. Decide how you want to best allocate these points based on the type of character you want to create. For example, if you want a strong and hearty character, put more points into strength. If you want a highly skilled character, put more points into wits and reflexes.

Step 2: Special Ability
Next, select one special ability your species has. This will give your character an edge in battles. For example, if you're playing a fantasy game, a spy might be able to move extremely silently.

Step 3: Reasonable Special Ability
You also have the option of creating one reasonable special ability that your stick figure possesses. This ability should make sense within the context of the game. For example, a stick figure might possess the ability to fly or turn invisible.

Step 4: Equipment
Get 20 points to create your character's equipment. You can use these points to purchase weapons, armor, and other items that will help your stick figure in battles.

Step 5: Health Calculation
Calculate your stick figure's health by adding your strength and reflexes scores and multiplying that number times 2. This will give you a number which will determine your character's health points.

Step 6: Drawing Your Character
Now that you have created your stick figure character, it's time to draw them! Be creative and add as many details as you wish.

Part 2: Engaging in Battles

Step 1: Adventure Mode
Perform actions in adventure mode with stick figures. This mode is where you get to explore the game world and interact with other stick figures.

Step 2: Rolling the Dice
Roll 2d6 again and compare the results. This will determine if your stick figure character has successfully done the action they are attempting.

Step 3: Hitting a Target
When rolling to see if you hit a target, roll just as a non-combat action is rolled, except the stick figure can roll to dodge. This adds an element of strategy to battles.

Step 4: War Rules for Battles
Know the War Rules for battles. These rules dictate how battles are conducted, and you must follow them to succeed in the game.

Step 5: Setting Limits
Set limits on what units can be deployed and what cannot be deployed. This helps prevent players from creating overpowered characters.

Step 6: Drawing the Battlefield
Draw the battlefield terrain on paper. Think strategically about where various obstacles and terrain features are placed.

Step 7: Point Budget
Set a point budget of how many points each army gets. The budget can be almost any number but should be at least 10.

Step 8: Buying for Your Army
Buy things for your army. Use your budget to purchase weapons, armor, and other items.

Step 9: Victory Conditions
Set fair victory conditions before the battle begins. Make sure they are reasonable and achievable.

Playing Stickcraft3 requires creativity, strategy, and planning. By following these steps, you'll be well on your way to creating the perfect stick figure character and dominating battles.

Are you looking for a unique and beautiful wall hanging to add to your home decor collection? Look no further than a braided jute wall hanging with dried flowers! Follow these easy steps to create your own stunning wall hanging.

1. Select two suitable handles or hooks from which you'll work the wall hanging. These should be sturdy and able to hold the weight of the finished hanging.

2. Choose three jute rope rolls. The rolls should be of equal thickness and length.

3. Take the first lightly colored jute rope and attach it to the first handle.

4. Take the rope from the first handle to the second one, pulling it tightly as you go.

5. Repeat the procedure for the darker colored jute rope, attaching it to the first handle and pulling it tightly to the second one.

6. Repeat the procedure for the third lightly colored rope.

7. Gather all the jute lengths and hold them tightly at the center.

8. Take your scissors and cut a length of jute from the excess on each side.

9. Arrange and centralize the alignment of the jute lengths.

10. Hold the jute lengths at the center and strengthen the hanger loop by wrapping the excess jute lengths around it.

11. Hang the hanger loop to a nail or a place where it is able to remain firmly in place as you continue to work on braiding the hanging.

12. Divide the rope into three equal portions in readiness for braiding.

13. Braid each of the three divided portions individually, making sure to keep the braids consistent in thickness.

14. Loosely stitch the three braids together from the back side, securing them in place.

15. Using a cutter, cut the jute wire into equal lengths.

16. Shape the wire into a teardrop or petal shape. Repeat this step as many times as you wish to create multiple flowers.

17. Wind colored jute rope around the jute wire (except for the stems) and shape the wire into flower petals.

18. Use the golden stems to form a flower by attaching petals to each stem.

19. Wrap thread tightly around all of the stems of the four or five petals, securing them together.

20. Collect or buy some dried plants of different species to add to your design.

21. Spray the flowers a golden color and let them dry.

22. Arrange the flowers, mirror tile pieces (if using), and dried plants in any sequence you like.

23. Attach and/or glue on all the flowers, mirror tile pieces, and dried plants onto the braided section of the hanging.

24. Your braided jute wall hanging with dried flowers is now complete and ready to grace your home with a touch of natural beauty and style!

If you're planning a trip to Disney World, one of the best ways to save money and have a great experience is to rent a vacation home nearby. However, finding the right rental can be a daunting task. Here are a few tips to help you navigate the process and find the perfect vacation home rental next to Disney World.

1. Search for vacation home rentals which are privately owned and advertised by the owner, AND whose owners (not managers) are residents and personally available to help with any unforeseen need both before and during your trip.

One advantage of renting directly from the owner is that they are often more invested in making sure that you have a great time. They're also more likely to be local residents themselves, which means that they can offer valuable tips and insights about the area.

2. Call and talk directly to the owner.

This is important because it allows you to get a sense of the owner's personality and can help you determine whether they're trustworthy and reliable. It's also a good opportunity to ask any questions that you may have about the property.

3. Obtain a listing of emails so you can contact prior guests.

Talking to prior guests can give you an idea of what to expect and help you avoid any potential issues.

4. Beware of vacation rentals who provide the actual address of the home.

Some vacation rentals may not be entirely truthful about their location. Make sure that the address you're given is accurate before you book.

5. Check on site venues such at TripAdvisor or Flipkey for recommendations.

Reading reviews from other travelers can help you determine which vacation rentals are worth considering.

6. Contact the Better Business Bureau of Central Florida and seek recommendations for vacation rental properties with an A+ rating and NO complaints.

The BBB can provide valuable information about the reputation of vacation rental properties in the area.

7. Do not send any cash or money by wire transfer.

This is a common scam. Never wire money to someone you don't know.

8. Consider a vacation rental property where the owner allows you to pay on arrival after you have toured the home.

This gives you more peace of mind and ensures that you're not risking any money before you've had a chance to see the property.

9. Avoid those management companies who, while they may post great offers, treat you more like one time customers than family.

You want to work with companies that value your business and will go above and beyond to make sure that you have a great experience.

10. Have another person contact the vacation home rental owners and ask the same questions.

This can help you avoid any potential discrepancies or misunderstandings.

11. Ask if the photos you've seen are of the actual home you are booking.

Make sure that the photos accurately reflect the property you'll be staying in.

12. Find out if pets or smoking are allowed.

If you have specific needs or preferences, make sure you clarify them upfront to avoid any surprises. With these tips in mind, you'll be well on your way to finding the perfect vacation home rental next to Disney World.

A brick driveway is a classic addition to any home or property. Not only does it add curb appeal, but it is also durable and long-lasting. Installing a brick driveway can seem like a daunting task, but with the right tools and knowledge, it can be done easily and efficiently. Follow these steps to learn how to install a brick driveway.

1. Lay out the driveway in the shape you want it.

The first step in installing a brick driveway is to lay out the shape of the driveway. Use stakes and string to create a border for the driveway. The shape should be square or rectangular, with straight edges.

2. Excavate the area for the driveway to a depth of at least 12 to 14 inches (30 to 36 cm) and compact the raw sub-grade.

Excavate the area inside the border to a depth of at least 12 to 14 inches. Remove any large rocks or debris from the area. Compact the raw sub-grade using a plate vibrator.

3. Bring in the stone base that will double as support for the brick and drainage.

Bring in the stone base that will double as support for the brick and drainage. Dump the stone in small manageable piles on the sub-grade.

4. Install the stone in at least 2 or, better, 3 lifts using a plate vibrator to compact it together.

Install the stone in at least 2 or, better, 3 lifts using a plate vibrator to compact it together. This ensures that the stone is level and compacted properly.

5. Hold the last lift of stone to a grade that allows for 2 inches (5.1 cm) of sand and the 3 inch (7.6 cm) brick paver.

Hold the last lift of stone to a grade that allows for 2 inches (5.1 cm) of sand and the 3 inch (7.6 cm) brick paver. This will ensure that the bricks are level and flush with the ground.

6. Cover the stone with landscape cloth to stop weeds from coming up between the pavers.

Cover the stone with landscape cloth to stop weeds from coming up between the pavers. This helps to keep the driveway looking neat and tidy.

7. Place 2 inches (5.1 cm) of sand over the cloth to bed the paver block in.

Place 2 inches (5.1 cm) of sand over the cloth to bed the paver block in. This will ensure that the bricks are level and stable.

8. Set the plastic brick edge retainer in place following manufacturer's instructions.

Set the plastic brick edge retainer in place following manufacturer's instructions. This will hold the bricks in place and prevent them from moving or shifting.

9. Begin at the center of the end of the driveway and work out toward the edges using brick spacers to keep even spacing.

Begin at the center of the end of the driveway and work out toward the edges using brick spacers to keep even spacing. Make sure to use a level to ensure that the bricks are level and even.

10. Use a 3 foot (1 m) length of 2 x 4-inch (5.1 X 10.2 cm) wood to lay across the bricks every couple of feet.

Use a 3 foot (1 m) length of 2 x 4-inch (5.1 X 10.2 cm) wood to lay across the bricks every couple of feet. This will help to ensure that the bricks are level and even.

11. Start on the second row, centering the brick over the joint of the 2 bricks in the middle.

Start on the second row, centering the brick over the joint of the 2 bricks in the middle. This will ensure that the bricks line up properly and look neat and tidy.

12. Continue the previous 3 steps to the completion of the driveway.

Continue the previous 3 steps to the completion of the driveway. This will ensure that the driveway is even and level and looks beautiful.

13. Use masonry sand to fill the joints between the brick by spreading it out over the brick sweeping it into the joints.

Use masonry sand to fill the joints between the brick by spreading it out over the brick sweeping it into the joints. This will ensure that the bricks are held in place and look neat and tidy.

14. Spray water from a hose over the brick to settle the sand into the joints.

Spray water from a hose over the brick to settle the sand into the joints. This will ensure that the bricks are held in place and the driveway looks beautiful. Your new brick driveway is now complete!

Hernias are a common medical condition that affect millions of people every year. They can occur in different parts of the body, such as the abdomen, groin, or upper thigh, and can cause discomfort, pain, and other symptoms. Knowing if you have a hernia can be difficult, but there are several risk factors and signs to watch out for. In this article, we will guide you through the steps to assess your risk factors, recognize the symptoms, and understand the potential outcomes of this condition.

Part 1: Assess your risk factors

1. Take note of any bulges: One of the most common signs of a hernia is a bulging or swelling in the affected area. This can occur when an organ protrudes through a weak or torn muscle wall. You may notice a lump or mass that grows larger when you cough, sneeze, or strain.

2. Pay attention to vomiting: If you experience persistent vomiting, this may be a sign of a more serious type of hernia, such as a paraesophageal hernia. This can occur when the stomach pushes through the diaphragm and causes severe symptoms, such as chest pain and difficulty breathing.

3. Watch for constipation: Chronic constipation can put a strain on your abdominal muscles and increase your risk of hernias. It can also cause pain and discomfort in the lower abdomen, which may lead to a hernia.

4. Don't ignore abnormal feelings of fullness: If you feel bloated or full after eating, this may be a sign of a hernia. You may also experience indigestion, heartburn, or regurgitation.

5. Keep track of your pain levels: Hernias can cause different types of pain, such as a dull ache, sharp stabbing pain, or a burning sensation. Pay attention to the location of the pain and how it changes over time.

6. Know when to see a doctor: If you suspect you have a hernia, it's important to see a doctor for proper diagnosis and treatment. Some hernias can lead to serious complications if left untreated, such as bowel obstruction or strangulation.

7. Take your gender into consideration: Men are more likely to develop hernias than women, especially older men over the age of 50. This is due to the natural weakening of the abdominal muscles over time, as well as lifestyle factors such as smoking and obesity.

8. Know your family's history: If you have a family member with a history of having hernias, you are at a greater risk yourself. This may be due to genetic factors that make you more susceptible to developing weak abdominal muscles.

9. Take your lung condition into account: Chronic coughing or bronchitis can put a strain on your abdominal muscles and increase your risk of hernias. If you have a chronic lung condition, it's important to manage it properly to prevent further complications.

10. Pay attention to chronic constipation: If you suffer from chronic constipation, you may be more likely to develop a hernia due to the prolonged straining.

11. Know that you're at risk if you're pregnant: Women who are pregnant may experience hernias due to the increased pressure on their abdominal muscles. This is especially common during the third trimester when the baby is growing rapidly.

12. Try to keep your weight at a healthy level: Obesity can put a strain on your abdominal muscles and increase your risk of hernias. Maintaining a healthy weight through diet and exercise can help reduce your risk.

13. Consider whether your job could be the culprit: Certain occupations that require heavy lifting or prolonged standing can increase your risk of hernias. If you work in a job that puts strain on your abdominal muscles, be sure to take proper precautions and avoid lifting heavy objects if possible.

Part 2: Recognize the symptoms

1. Understand how doctors diagnose hernias: Your doctor will perform a physical examination to check for signs of a hernia, such as a bulge or lump in the affected area. They may also use imaging tests such as ultrasound or MRI to get a better picture of the hernia.

2. Recognize an inguinal hernia: This is the most common type of hernia, and happens when the intestines or bladder push the lower abdominal wall into the groin and inguinal canal. Symptoms may include a visible bulge in the groin area, pain or discomfort in the affected area, and a heavy or dragging sensation.

3. Suspect a hiatal hernia in people over 50: This type of hernia occurs when the stomach pushes up through the diaphragm and into the chest cavity. Symptoms may include heartburn, chest pain, difficulty swallowing, and regurgitation of food or liquids.

4. Look for umbilical hernias in babies: This type of hernia occurs in infants when the abdominal muscles don't fully close around the belly button. Symptoms may include a visible bulge around the navel, which may become more noticeable when the baby cries or strains.

5. Be careful of incisional hernia after surgery: This type of hernia can occur after a surgical incision has weakened the abdominal muscles. Symptoms may include a visible bulge around the incision site, pain or discomfort in the affected area, and difficulty moving or bending.

6. Recognize a femoral hernia in women: This type of hernia occurs when the intestines push through the femoral canal, which is located just below the groin crease. Symptoms may include a visible bulge in the upper thigh, pain or discomfort in the affected area, and a heavy or dragging sensation.

7. Report acute pain immediately: If the hernia's symptoms come on suddenly, the first thing the doctor will do is try to manage your pain. Acute pain may be a sign of a more serious complication, such as bowel obstruction or strangulation.

Part 3: Understand the potential outcomes

1. Consider getting elective surgery: If your hernia is causing significant pain or discomfort, your doctor may recommend surgery to repair the weakened muscle wall. This can help prevent further complications and improve your quality of life.

2. Be aware of potential outcomes: While surgery is often successful in repairing hernias, there is always a risk of complications such as infection, bleeding, and nerve damage. It's important to discuss the potential risks and benefits of surgery with your doctor before making a decision.

In conclusion, knowing if you have a hernia can be difficult, but there are several risk factors and signs to watch out for. By assessing your risk factors, recognizing the symptoms, and understanding the potential outcomes of this condition, you can take steps to manage your health and prevent further complications. If you suspect you have a hernia, be sure to see a doctor for proper diagnosis and treatment.

Making gift bags for baby showers can be a fun and creative way to personalize your gifts. One of the first steps in making a gift bag is to determine the width, height, and depth of your bag based on the gifts you have chosen. This will help ensure that your bag is the proper size and shape to accommodate all of your presents. Once you have this information, you can follow these steps to create a beautiful and functional gift bag.

Materials:
- Two pieces of paper
- Scissors
- Chipboard or cardstock
- Glue
- Small piece of cardboard
- Eyelets
- Cord
- Tissue paper

Instructions:

1. Lay two pieces of paper on top of each other. Make sure they are both the same size.

2. Cut out the paper in a rectangle shape. This will be the base of your gift bag.

3. Place the side of the paper that should be on the exterior of the bag down on your work surface.

4. Fold the paper as follows:
- Measure the width from the left edge and fold it over.
- Fold the top of the paper down 1.25” along the width of the rectangle.
- Fold the bottom of the paper up 2” along the width of the rectangle.

5. Cut a piece of chipboard or cardstock so that the width of the piece equals the width of your bag. Glue the chipboard or cardstock to the width pieces of your rectangle.

6. Glue the top flap down over the cardboard. Then glue the left end of the paper to the 0.5” seam on the right of the paper.

7. Position the bag so that the 2” flap is at the top.

8. Cut out a small piece of cardboard and push it into the bottom of the bag. This will create a sturdy base.

9. Punch holes through the top rim of each side of the bag. Then add eyelets to these holes.

10. Cut two matching lengths of cord to form the handles. Thread each end of the cord through the eyelets.

11. Tie knots in the cords behind the eyelets so that the cord is held in place.

12. Place the gift materials in the bag.

13. Stuff a few pieces of tissue paper over the gift materials to complete the bag.

With these step-by-step instructions, you can create a beautiful and functional gift bag for any baby shower. Customize your bag with different colors and patterns to make it truly unique.

Performing a Cardiac Auscultation: A Comprehensive Guide

A cardiac auscultation is a medical examination often conducted by healthcare professionals such as doctors and nurses to determine the state of a patient’s cardiovascular health. It is a non-invasive technique that involves listening to the sounds generated by the patient’s heart, providing essential diagnostic information that could help in identifying various cardiovascular diseases. This article will give you a step-by-step guide on how to conduct a cardiac auscultation appropriately.

Find an adequately lit, quiet room.

Before conducting a cardiac auscultation, ensure that you are in a quiet, well-lit room. This helps to reduce background noise and improve your ability to listen to the patient's heart sounds.

Introduce yourself and give an overview of what will take place during auscultation.

As a healthcare professional, it is essential to establish rapport with your patient before conducting a cardiac auscultation. Introduce yourself to the patient and give an overview of what the procedure entails.

Courteously ask the patient to remove his/her upper body clothing.

Ask the patient to remove his/her upper body clothing and lie down on the examination bed once they have completed the task. You may need to sanitize your hands before touching the patient.

Stand at the right side of the patient.

Standing at the right side of the patient gives you access to the patient's heart, which is located on the left side of the chest.

Feel the patient's heart.

Before using a stethoscope, touch the patient's chest gently to feel his/her heartbeat; this can provide you with a rough estimate of the heart rate.

Start auscultation with the diaphragm of your stethoscope placed at the heart's apex.

Position the stethoscope's diaphragm at the heart's apex, located about two finger-widths below the left nipple. Listen for at least one full cardiac cycle and note any abnormal heart sounds such as murmurs or rubs.

Listen in the other 3 auscultation spots.

Subsequently, move the stethoscope to other three auscultation spots (the pulmonic, tricuspid, and aortic areas) to listen for any heart abnormalities. Repeat the same process of listening in each spot for at least one full cardiac cycle.

Repeat steps 2 and 3, this time using the bell of your stethoscope.

Repeat steps 2 and 3, but this time, use the stethoscope's bell (a smaller disc-shaped piece placed on the opposite side of the diaphragm).

Ask the patient to lie on his/her left side and ensure proper draping.

Ask the patient to roll over to his/her left side, exposing the left side of the chest to examine the heart's rhythm and rate. During this process, ensure that the patient is adequately draped to protect privacy.

Leave the examination room and allow the patient to get dressed.

Once you are done with the auscultation, give the patient privacy to dress up and exit the examination room to fill in the findings in the patient's medical record.

Identify whether the cardiac rhythm is regular or irregular.

Listen to the patient's heartbeat and determine whether the cardiac rhythm is regular or irregular.

Figure out the heart rate.

Count how many S1 sounds you hear in ten seconds while listening to the heartbeat. Multiply that number by six to get the patient’s heart rate.

Listen for the presence of murmurs.

Listen for any murmurs that could be present during the auscultation.

Listen for gallops.

Double-check by listening to any gallops that the patient’s heart may produce during the auscultation.

Conclusion

Conducting a cardiac auscultation may appear daunting, but it is vital for a healthcare professional when determining the cardiovascular health of a patient. By following the outlined steps, you can perform a comprehensive cardiac auscultation. Remember to document the findings precisely and report any relevant data to physicians.

"How to Be a Good Chick Fil A Customer"

Chick Fil A has made a name for itself in the fast food industry by serving up tasty chicken sandwiches, nuggets, and waffle fries. But being a good customer goes beyond just enjoying the food. Follow these tips to ensure a pleasant and efficient drive-thru experience at Chick Fil A.

1. Don't pull up to the speaker box with your window up.

It may seem like a no-brainer, but make sure your car window is rolled down when you pull up to the speaker box. This will allow you to clearly hear the employee taking your order and for them to hear you as well.

2. Finish your telephone conversation before you think about entering the line.

Talking on the phone can be distracting and delay the ordering process for everyone else in line. Be courteous to other customers and wait until you're off the phone before entering the drive-thru line.

3. If you complain about time, the employee will complain about you.

Remember that the employees working at Chick Fil A are doing their best to serve each customer quickly and efficiently. If you complain about the wait time or rush them to finish your order, it's unlikely to speed things up and may even cause unnecessary tension.

4. Pull as close as possible to the speaker box, and at the same time speak loudly without shouting.

Make it easier for the employee to hear your order by pulling up as close as possible to the speaker box. However, be mindful not to shout, as this can come across as rude or aggressive.

5. Know what you want before you get there.

The Chick Fil A menu is not overly complicated, so take a moment to decide what you want before you get to the speaker box. This will save time for both you and the employee.

6. Speak up while you order.

When placing your order, speak clearly and confidently. This will help the employee understand your order and avoid any mistakes.

7. Do not ask for sauce until you get to the window.

Chick Fil A employees are trained to provide the correct amount of sauces with each order. Wait until you get to the window to request any additional sauces.

8. Pull as close as possible to the window-the employee doesn't have a stretchable back.

Just like at the speaker box, it's important to pull up as close as possible to the window at the pickup window. This will make it easier for the employee to hand you your food without having to reach too far.

9. Ask for a specific number of sauces.

If you need additional sauces with your order, be specific about how many you need. Don't ask for an excessive amount of sauce for your order, as this can delay the drive-thru process for other customers.

10. You do not need 16 sauces for your 12 nuggets.

On a related note, it's important to remember that you don't need an excessive amount of sauce for your order. Generally, one or two packets of sauce per item is enough.

11. Do not order party trays through the drive-thru.

Party trays can be complicated to order and may take up too much time in the drive-thru. Instead, call ahead to place your party tray order and pick it up inside the restaurant.

12. Be specific.

If you have special requests or modifications to your order, be as specific as possible to avoid confusion or mistakes.

13. Listen when the employees repeat your order at the window.

Before you drive away from the pickup window, listen to the employee as they repeat your order back to you. This will ensure that your order is accurate before you leave.

14. Realize that the employee you are speaking with has probably been working in the same position for a few hours.

Working the drive-thru can be a repetitive and tiring job. Be kind to the employees taking your order, as they are likely working in the same position for a few hours.

15. If you see an employee changing one of the trash cans in one of the drive-thru lanes DO NOT be in such a hurry you run over the trash bag full of trash that the employee just took out of the trash can.

Finally, be patient and mindful of employees working in the drive-thru. If you see an employee changing a trash can or performing another task, give them space and avoid rushing past them. Running over a trash bag full of trash is not only rude, but it can also be dangerous. Exercise caution and courtesy at all times.

A good attitude is important for your and everybody else's learning benefit. You don’t want to get in trouble with the teachers or principal in your first year for breaking all sorts of rules you didn't even know about. Make sure you learn your school’s dress code as well as the other rules and then follow them! Making the teachers dislike you or making yourself seem like a troublemaker is only going to make things harder for you. Even if your friends are breaking the rules, don't follow their example. Make an effort to stay in your teachers' good books.;
, There will be lots of gossip and some really malicious (mean) rumors floating around. Ignore rumors, even when they’re about you, and if anyone talks or asks you about a rumor, tell them to ignore it and not spread it around. Never start rumors: they only break friendships, create enemies, hurt feelings, and make things worse for everyone. Gossip may seem juicy at first, but it bites at the end. Do your best to stay away from it.


Help to stop rumors and save lives by standing up for people and not participating by spreading the rumor.
Protect other people's privacy and mistakes. You wouldn't want someone spreading your personal secrets around, would you?
Drama is unnecessary. Some people think that a life has to be filled with melodrama to be important and appreciated by others. It does not. Focusing on the things, people, and ideas that are really important is better than worrying about who is going to ask who to the dance.

, This is very important. Be friends with people who don’t start or participate in drama and you’ll avoid the worst things that happen in middle school. Establish a small group of good friends. Every group will face a few problems, but if you suddenly feel like your life could be a Disney Channel show, you might want to consider a more sensible group of friends.


Understand that in middle school, rather intentionally or, in most cases, unintentionally, you will make enemies. The only way to survive is to have a close group of friends who you can trust. You don't need too many close friends; three or four good friends, and then several casual friends is definitely enough. Be nice and friendly to everyone, but don't worry about making everyone love you.

, Similar to the step above, you shouldn't be friends with people who get you in serious trouble. If someone asks you to lie about something important, do something illegal, or do something to hurt someone else, then don't do it. Don’t do anything that makes you feel uncomfortable or what you think is wrong. This is called peer pressure and it can lead to all sorts of problems.


Don’t be afraid to tell an adult if someone asks or tells you to do something really bad. This doesn't make you a snitch: it makes you a good person doing the right thing. If you make a bad decision, talk to an adult you can trust. Talking to friends about it is the fastest way to start gossip. But a warning: They may want revenge if you do that, and if they are willing enough, that may be a bad can of worms to open.

, Just like you don’t want to do something to hurt other people, you shouldn't do anything to hurt yourself. Don’t do drugs, play choking games (or anything else that someone tells you is safe/legal, but you know it's not), or any kind of self-harm like cutting yourself. If you need help, there are always people who will help you.

, Now that you’re in middle school, you’ll start feeling a lot more grown up and you might find yourself wanting a boyfriend or a girlfriend. You’ll definitely have some pretty serious crushes! But relationships are hard, stressful, and usually cause more problems than they solve. You might enjoy having crushes and maybe flirt a little, but stay single and focus on having fun, making friends, and learning things.

, Everyone worries about gym. You've probably heard that you’ll have to change your clothes in front of other girls or boys or maybe you've never been very good at gym and you feel embarrassed. What you have to remember is that everyone is worried and embarrassed, so you're not alone.


You might feel like everyone is watching you while you change, but they are really focusing on getting themselves changed. Nobody will look at you, because they are too busy thinking YOU are watching them. Everyone wants to just keep to themselves and change as quickly as possible!
If you’re a girl and you feel self-conscious about being on your period while you’re having to change, just wear black or brown underwear. No one will notice at all. Middle school is all about those little changes going on your body; if you feel nervous about them, talk to your mom, another trusted adult or a mentor/counselor.

, This is a really important skill, not just for getting through middle school but for getting through your whole life. If you learn good ways to solve problems, you’ll be able to handle anything that comes your way.


For example, you should learn to ask for help when you need it. Sometimes you’ll feel silly asking for help or you don’t want to admit that you’re having problems, but you don't have to. Everyone has problems, and whoever you ask for help will totally understand. They’ve had to ask for help at some point too.
Apologize and accept consequences when you've done something wrong. After making a mistake, refusing to acknowledge that you did something bad (even if you didn't mean to) will just make things harder for you. You’ll feel guilty or you’ll have to deal with people being angry with you, and you don’t want that. If you spread a rumor, apologize. If you lie to a teacher, confess.

Communicate clearly. By doing this, you are avoiding most of the problems people face. A lot of the time, rumors get started because someone misunderstands what you say or you misunderstand what they say. You might also accidentally offend someone if you say something you don’t mean. Be careful, clear and always make sure you know what you’re saying.

, Remember: We won't try to make middle school seem like it's the worst thing that will ever happen to you, it might be really great! But, we also won't try to tell you that it's all awesome and like living in a TV show either. It can be really hard. Just believe that there will be happy times, and there will be sad times, but no matter how bad it gets, things will always get better.

, This will give you a few friends to start off with. You can ask your elementary friends what school they’ll be going to at the end of your final year, just make sure to get phone numbers so that you can make plans to meet up with them at your new school.

, Once you start school, you can also try to make friends with people you see getting on and off at the same bus stop. Friends that live in your neighborhood can be handy, since you’ll have an easier time hanging out and you’ll have someone nearby that you can call for homework help or advice.

, Even if you have a whole bunch of your elementary friends coming with you to middle school, you should still try to make some new friends. If you don’t try to meet new people, you’ll never know what you’re missing. Maybe someone you meet will be your best friend for a while.

, A great way to meet new friends is to join clubs at your new school. Most schools will have at least a few clubs and some have lots! Some schools will let you start your own too, if you don’t find one that sounds good to you. There might be book clubs, a bible study, movie clubs, theater clubs, environmental clubs, cooking club, robotics clubs, or yearbook club (and these are just a few examples).


Don’t forget about sports! There are sports teams if you want to join a team, but there might also be clubs that are just about watching or playing friendly matches if you aren't really good enough or don’t want to join the official school teams.
Volunteering is also a sort of club that will help you meet new people to be friends with. Your school might have a group that volunteers to raise money for events, make cards for elderly people or people in the hospital, clean up local parks, or other great activities.

, You should show what you’re interested in, in subtle ways so that people that like the same thing will know they can come up and talk to you about it. This is a great way to make friends because you’ll know you already have something in common.


For example, if you like Adventure Time, you can wear a Lumpy Space Princess pin on your backpack. If you like video games, get a class binder with a picture from your favorite game on it. If you like a sports team, wear a bracelet for that team.

, If you show people that you think you’re a great person to be friends with, and that you think you have a lot to offer to people, then they’ll be much more likely to want to be friends with you. Don’t constantly apologize and don’t back down if people don’t immediately like you. Speak up for yourself, stand up straight, and celebrate the things that make you unique.

, This is the most important part of making new friends! You’ll never make friends if you don’t talk to people. Join conversations that sound interesting and introduce yourself to people you think you’d like to be friends with.


Don’t forget to speak up so that people can hear you! Talk like you mean it!

, If other people see you enjoying yourself, they’ll want to join in and be friends with you so that they can have fun too. You can do fun things by joining clubs, drawing in between classes, or putting together parties or other activities for after school.

, If you want people to be friends with you, it’s important to be a nice person. Who wants to be friends with a jerk? Nobody! Be nice to everyone you meet, even if they aren't nice back. People will notice that you are a great person and they will be much more likely to be nice to you.


It’s important to be actively nice, not just polite. Help people that have trouble in class, stand up for others when they’re getting bullied, and do nice things for people when you can. Also, give people honest compliments when they seem like they need it!
You never know when someone is going through some really tough times. They could feel like they’re dying inside and they might not show it. Your kind words or deeds could make a world of difference to them.
Remember that sometimes, when people are acting really jerky, they do that because they feel bad about themselves or something that’s happening in their life. They’re mean because they don’t know what kindness looks like! Try to be nice to them even if they’re mean to you. It might help make them a better person.

, If you want to do well in class, the best place to start is to pay attention! It’s amazing how much your grades will go up if you just pay attention and try to absorb as much information from the lesson as possible. Don’t mess with your phone, try not to daydream, and don’t pass notes with friends. There’s always time for fun stuff later!

, Take notes in class. You don’t have to write down everything that the teacher says: just write down the really important or hard to remember information. Write the sort of stuff you’d say if you were explaining the lesson to someone who wasn’t there. This will help you study for tests later and also do your homework.

, This is really important for getting decent grades. If you don’t do your homework, you’re almost guaranteed to get bad grades, even if you ace tests and stuff like that. Find some quiet time each evening and just work to get your homework done. Get help if you need it too! Your homework shouldn't take so much time that you don’t have time to relax too.

, Don’t just shove everything in your backpack. This will make you forget about assignments or lose important papers. Instead, have a binder for homework assignments and organize them by when they’re due. Have another binder for class notes, organized by subject.


Consider getting a planner. You also want to keep your life organized! Get a planner and carefully organize your day. Set aside time for homework, time for hanging out, time for getting ready and eating breakfast in the morning, and everything else you need to do during your day.

, Lots of people develop the very bad habit of procrastinating. This means that they don’t do things when they should. Instead, they wait until the last minute! This is bad, because it means that when you do things, you’ll do a bad job because you’ll be rushed. It will also make you really stressed. Develop a good habit of doing things at the appropriate time and you’ll save yourself a lot of trouble.

, This is a great way to make sure you improve your grades. When you don’t understand something, ask! This way you know you’re doing something right. Even if you do understand something, it’s good to ask questions if you’re curious about something else. Always ask questions and you’ll find yourself getting smarter and smarter.

, If you really want good grades, you’ll need to study. Read all of the books you are assigned and set aside plenty of time to study. Middle school is an important time to develop good school habits, so getting used to studying now will really help you later.

, Don’t worry about getting straight A’s right now. Just focus on learning as much as you can, developing good school habits, and get the best grades you can. You shouldn’t settle for a C- but you shouldn’t worry about a B or B+.

, Middle school is a great time to explore the things you enjoy and figure out what is important to you. You should take electives that you think you might like, learn to do things that you’ve always wanted to learn how to do, and read about things you’d like to do in the future.


Read books about people who inspire you. Figure out what they did to get to where they got and figure out if you want to do the same.
Clubs are a great way to explore things that make you happy! Try joining one at your school.
The internet can also be a great place to explore things you like, especially if you like nerdy stuff! You’ll have a much easier time finding people who like the same stuff. Just be careful because just like the real world, there are lots of bad people out there on the internet.

, Make sure you’re washing your body, keeping your face clean, wearing clean clothes and other things to keep yourself looking nice. This will help you develop confidence and make you feel more comfortable in your body, even if it is changing.

, While it’s important to devote time to learning while you’re in middle school, it’s also important for you to learn how to balance responsibility with fun and relaxation. You’ll drive yourself crazy if you spend too much time studying, but you’ll also have a really hard time in life if you don’t learn how to be responsible.

, You may not realize it now, because many people don’t, but helping others can be the most rewarding thing you ever do. Making a positive difference in your community and the world can make you feel like a superhero because you’ll be one! Volunteer, help people that you see struggling, and find out how you can improve the world around you.

, School is all about getting your mind fit and healthy, but you need to make sure your body stays healthy too. Make sure you eat right, and get plenty of good exercise to keep your body in good condition. Getting healthy now means you’ll be able to look forward to a lifetime of good habits!

, If you’re good at something, you should find ways to do those things! Get better and better at things you enjoy and that you’re good at. Your talents can often be turned into a great job or hobby when you’re older (or even now). Talk to your parents about what you can do, and if they can’t help, talk to a teacher.


For example, if you’re good at drawing, take an art class. If you have a talent for learning songs, join the band. If you’re good at math, offer to tutor other students (for extra credit or money!) the possibilities are endless!

, You’ll be a much happier person and it will be much easier to deal with problems and the stress of middle school if you learn to worry only about the problems that really matter. This can be hard to do, and will probably take a long time to learn, but it’s worth keeping in mind.


For example, don’t worry about stuff like losing a game (it’s just a game!), feeling left out (you’ll find your crowd someday and you’re probably not as alone as you feel), people making accusations about you (their drama is theirs, not yours, ignore them), or other kids teasing you (you’ll be the one laughing at them when they work at 7-11 in twenty years and you have a college degree).
Instead, worry about things like injustice, current events, and the world around you. These are things that matter and that you should always worry about: because if you don’t worry, you won’t do anything about it, and if people don’t do anything then the problems will never get better.

, There will be many times where you will feel different and alone. You might be afraid because you find yourself having a crush on the "wrong" person. You might feel like no one understands you because you like the "wrong" things. You might feel left out because you and your parents don't look like everyone else. But it is very important for you to understand that no matter how alone you feel, no matter how "wrong" or weird you think you are, there are so many people just like you. Someday you're going to meet them and you're going to find better friends and family that you never thought you would...and you will be more happy than you ever thought you could be.


You might notice, for example, that while all the other girls are giggling over boys, you don't feel the same way. You might find yourself wanting a very close relationship with another girl. This shouldn't make you feel like there is something wrong with you because there is nothing wrong with you. Give things time and take things slow. You never know how you'll feel in a few months or even a few years.
You might think that you're weird because your family doesn't look like or doesn't talk like everyone else's. Maybe your parents don't speak English. Maybe you have two dads. Maybe your dad is black and your mom is Asian. The important thing to understand is that families come in all shapes and sizes, and as long as you love each other, that's all that matters. You are just like everyone else. No matter how your family looks.

, This can be a source of lots of embarrassment and nervousness, but it shouldn’t be. All the girls have to deal with the same problems! Be prepared and you won’t have anything to worry about.

, Pretty much all boys will have to deal with this problem at some point. Don’t worry: it’s totally normal! Know how to solve the problem and you won’t have anything to worry about. Try carrying a textbook in front.

, A lot of embarrassing moments in middle school come from tripping, falling, or running into someone or something at the worst times. Work on your coordination and pay attention to your surroundings and avoid your own tale of falling down the bleachers in the middle of an assembly.

, You’re probably worried about having to wear a uniform, since most middle schools these days require them, but still looking cool and being yourself. You can totally do this! With a little creativity, your uniform will be no problem.

, Girls are going to need bras and this can be a kind of scary thing. Don’t worry, though: it’s totally normal. Don’t be embarrassed to ask your mom or dad for help and get help at the store to find the right size.

, Nobody wants to be stinky! Since you’re going through puberty, your body will be a little more likely to get extra stinky and sweaty. Don’t worry: it’s totally normal! With a little effort, you can stay clean and ready for the day.

, As you get your grown up body, you’ll probably have to deal with embarrassing pimples. This is normal, but there’s no reason you should have to put up with it! With a little help, you can keep your skin pretty clear and nice looking.


If you have constant, painful break-outs it might be a good idea to consult your doctor. Many times they can prescribe medicines that will help your skin look and feel better!

, You don’t want to bully, be bullied, or let anyone else be bullied. Be brave enough to stop bullying in its tracks and make your school better for everybody!

, This will be very important, not just in middle school but for the rest of your school career. Get good at studying now and this will lead you to better grades and better opportunities for the rest of your life.

, Many students coming to middle school struggle with opening their locker. Combination locks are hard to work sometimes, even for adults. Learn how to work one, and things will be much easier for you.

How to Apply Lip Liner

If you're looking to perfect your lip game, applying lip liner is a must. Lip liner helps define the natural shape of your lips, prevents bleeding of your lipstick, and can even make your lips look fuller. Here are some steps to follow to apply lip liner like a pro:

1. Exfoliate your lips (optional).
Using a homemade scrub or purchasing a lip exfoliant can help remove any dead skin on your lips, making them smoother for application. Gently rub the exfoliant onto your lips using circular motions and then rinse off with warm water.

2. Moisturize your lips.
Follow up with a good lip balm to help hydrate your lips and provide a smooth base for application. Wait for the balm to dry before moving onto the next step.

3. Prime your lips (optional).
