To create an even base and help your lipstick and lip liner last longer, you can choose to prime your lips with a lip primer.

4. Choose your lip liner color.
Choose a lip liner color that closely matches your lipstick or your natural lip color. You can also use a clear lip liner for a more versatile option.

5. Sharpen your lip liner.
Make sure your lip liner is sharpened to a point for precise application.

6. Warm up the lip liner.
Warm up the tip of your lip liner by rubbing it between your fingers. This will help make the liner more pliable and easier to apply.

7. Part your lips slightly.
Part your lips slightly so that you can easily see and follow your natural lip line.

8. Trace your natural lip line.
Starting at the cupid's bow, lightly trace your natural lip line with short strokes. Be sure to stay close to the natural shape of your lips and avoid overlining.

9. Move in light, short strokes.
Moving in light, short strokes will give you a more precise line than trying to trace over your line in one smooth movement.

10. Complete your look.
Fill in your lips with liner to help your lipstick last longer and prevent bleeding. You can also choose to blend a nude liner into your lips for a natural look.

11. Apply your lipstick.
Choose a lipstick color that matches your liner and apply it to your lips.

12. Refine your line.
Using a concealer or foundation around your lips (optional), refine your lip line and blend it in with your skin tone for a polished look.

13. Blot between applications (optional).
If you want your lipstick to last longer, blot your lips with a tissue or cotton swab between applications.

14. Set your lips (optional).
Finish off your lip look with a setting spray or powder (optional) to help your lipstick last all day.

15. Choose your colors.
When choosing your lip liner colors, consider the occasion and your skin tone.

16. Draw slightly inside of your natural lip line.
If you want to create the illusion of fuller lips, try drawing slightly inside of your natural lip line. Be careful not to overdo it, as overlining your lips can appear unnatural.

17. Clean up around your lips.
Clean up any mistakes with a cotton swab dipped in either concealer or foundation.

18. Buy a good-quality lip liner.
When purchasing a lip liner, invest in a good-quality one that will last and not smudge.

19. Know what colors to buy.
Choose lip liner colors that complement your lipstick or natural lip color. You can also opt for a clear lip liner that works with any color.

20. Get a good sharpener.
Investing in a good sharpener will help sharpen your lip liner to a precise point.

21. Have some tissue paper or cotton swabs available.
In case of any smudging or mistakes, have some tissue paper or cotton swabs readily available.

22. Get a good lip balm.
A good lip balm not only moisturizes your lips but also provides a smooth base for application.

23. Get a lip primer (optional).
If you want your lipstick and lip liner to last longer and have a smoother application, consider using a lip primer.

24. Keep it natural (optional).
If you prefer a more natural look, skip the lipstick and only use lip liner to define and shape your lips.

25. Go big (optional).
If you want to give the illusion of fuller lips, try overlining your lips slightly.

26. Return to your natural lip line at the corners of your mouth.
To create a balanced and natural look, return to your natural lip line at the corners of your mouth.

27. Heavily apply lipstick to the center of your lips.
For a fuller lip look, heavily apply your lipstick to the center of your lips and lightly blend outwards.

28. Blend the liner and lipstick together.
To avoid a harsh contrast between your liner and lipstick, blend them together with a lip brush or your finger.

29. Run your ring finger or pinky finger over your lips.
This will help blend the liner and lipstick even further for a polished finish.

30. You're done!
Admire your perfectly lined lips and enjoy your flawless lip look!

The easiest way to create an installation USB drive is to use an ISO, or disc image, of your Windows 7 installation disc. If you have your disc, you can create your own ISO in just a few minutes. If you don't have your Windows 7 installation disc, see the next step.


Insert the Windows 7 installation disc into your DVD drive.
Download and install ImgBurn. You can download it for free from imgburn.com. Select "Custom installation" during the setup process and uncheck the boxes for any additional software. You'll have to do this twice.
Launch ImgBurn and select "Create image file from disc."
Select your DVD drive and click the folder button to create a file name and choose the location. The ISO file will be several GB in size. Give it a name you can easily recognize, such as "Windows7Install."
Click the large "Read" button to begin creating the file. This will take a few minutes to complete. You'll be able to find your new ISO file in the location you specified.;
, If you don't have a Windows 7 installation disc, or you don't want to install ImgBurn, you can download the Windows 7 ISO file directly from Microsoft. You'll need your Windows 7 product key in order to get the download link, which you can find affixed to your computer, with your computer's documentation, or in your purchase confirmation email message.


You can use NirSoft's free ProduKey to find your product key. You can get it from nirsoft.net/utils/product_cd_key_viewer.html. Running this small program will display your Windows 7 product key.
Once you have your product key, visit microsoft.com/en-us/software-download/windows7. Enter your product key and download the ISO file to your computer. This may take a while, depending on your internet speed.

, Make sure that you've backed up any important files, as anything on the drive will be deleted when you add the ISO.

, This may not be necessary, but some people have reported that this helps with errors with the creation process.


Open your Computer/This PC window. You can find this in the Start menu, or by pressing ⊞ Win+E.
Right-click on your USB drive and select "Format."
Select "NTFS" from the "File system" menu and format the drive.

, This utility allows you to easily turn your USB drive into a Windows installation drive using the ISO file. You can download the program from wudt.codeplex.com. It will need to be installed before you can use it.

, You'll be asked to select this file on the first screen of WUDT. Browse for the ISO file you created or downloaded from Microsoft.

, You can also use this tool to create a bootable DVD disc, but this tutorial is for a USB pen drive.

, If you have more than one USB drive inserted, they will all be listed here. Ensure that your USB drive displays at least 4 GB of storage.

, It will take several minutes to copy the ISO file onto the USB drive. Do not remove the USB drive until the process has completed.

, You can use the pen drive you created to install Windows 7 on any PC, but each installation will require its own product key. You will only be able to install the same version (Home, Professional, Ultimate) that you created your ISO for.

, This key varies by manufacturer, and needs to be pressed when the computer is first booting. This will allow you to change the boot order for your devices so that your computer boots from your pen drive instead of the hard drive. The correct key will be displayed on the same screen as the manufacturer logo when you first boot up the computer. Repeatedly press the key until the menu opens.


Common keys include F2, F10, F11, or Del.

, If you entered the BOOT menu directly, skip to the next step. Otherwise, use your arrow keys in the BIOS menu to open the BOOT section.

, Rearrange the boot order so that your pen drive comes first. This will ensure that your computer boots from the pen drive before booting from any other drive.

, You will be prompted to press a key to start Windows Setup.

, You'll be asked to select your language and region options. Once you've selected these, click "Install now" to begin the installation.

, This will allow you to format the drive and install a fresh copy of Windows 7. This will delete all of the data on the partition that you choose to install Windows on.

, Everything on this selected partition will be deleted during the installation. You can delete additional partitions that you don't use anymore and combine their free space by clicking the "Drive options" link. Select the partition you want to delete to turn it into unallocated space.

, This will take around 20-30 minutes, and you won't be prompted to do anything until it's finished.

, You'll be asked to create a user account and give the computer a name. Create your user account to proceed.


If you are setting up the computer for someone else or prepping it for sale, press Ctrl+⇧ Shift+F3 at this screen. This will start Windows in Audit mode. You can then install any necessary drivers and run the System Preparation Tool. Select "Enter System OOBE" and click "OK" to finish. This will prompt the next person who starts the computer to create a user., You'll be prompted to enter your product key so that Windows can be activated. You'll have up to 30 days to do this, but it's recommended that you enter the product key now.

, This will ensure that you always have the latest updates available.

, You'll be prompted to set the current time and date. Many computers will have this automatically correct thanks to the BIOS.

, This will affect how your computer appears on the network and what other devices can access. Make sure to select "Public network" if you aren't at home or work to help protect your computer.

, Windows will begin preparing your desktop, which may take a few minutes. Once this is complete, you can begin using Windows 7.

Starting a Relationship from Dating a Stranger

Meeting new people can be challenging, especially when trying to find someone special. However, love can happen anywhere, even to strangers. If you are interested in someone you’ve just met and want to start a relationship, here are some steps to follow.

1. You can meet someone anywhere.

Before you start, remember that perfect matches can happen anywhere at any time. Be open to meeting new people regardless of the situation you’re in.

2. Walk up to the person you are interested in and talk about random things.

To get the conversation started, approach the stranger and start chatting. Keep the conversation light, ask questions about their interests, or talk about a topic that you think you both will enjoy.

3. Build a rapport by flirting a bit...don't go too far.

As you talk, add a flirty tone to the conversation. Compliment them on something that catches your eye, or tease them in a playful manner. But make sure not to go too far and make them uncomfortable.

4. Sometime during the conversation, exchange names.

To take the conversation to the next level, exchange names, and remember to use their name while you talk to them. It will help you to build a more personal connection.

5. Continue flirting.

If the chemistry is good between you two, keep flirting. Make them feel attractive and noticed. If the conversation is flowing, keep going.

6. Assess whether closing in for their phone number would be ideal.

If you feel like the conversation is going well, take the next step and ask for their phone number. But make sure they are receptive to that idea. 

7. If ideal, go ahead and see if you can get their phone number; ex: Is she flirting back?

If you feel like they are interested in you too, ask for their phone number. Create a natural and easy going way of getting the number, and don’t pressure them if they are hesitant.

8. Ask for the number by saying something like: "It was nice meeting you, unfortunately, I have to go, but I would really like to continue this conversation."

Before leaving, let them know that you enjoyed the conversation and would like to continue it. Then ask for their phone number.

9. Proceed to pull out your cellphone, enter her phone number.

Save their number with a name and text them so that they can have your number too. Say something like “Hey, it’s me. Let’s keep in touch.”

10. Call it so she has yours.

Call your new friend's phone so that they can see your number and save it with your name.

11. Say good-bye and leave.

After you’ve exchanged numbers, say goodbye but keep it casual. 

12. Wait for a day.

Wait for a day before contacting them again. Give them time to get to know you.

13. Call.

Call them after a day or two and show interest in them. Ask about their day or talk about something you previously discussed with them.

14. Dress accordingly and flirt by talking less and asking more questions.

If you ask them out, make sure you dress presentably. Listen more and speak less. Ask questions about them to show that you are interested in them.

15. Call again at your discretion.

Wait a few days and call them again. Continue to get to know one another.

16. Suggest a private meeting next time, such as your place for a movie.

If you feel comfortable and like the conversation is flowing, invite them to spend time together. Suggest a movie night or dinner at your place.

17. Build more rapport by asking about topics such as her dreams and childhood, talk about how she is unique, look mesmerized and then slowly work into a kiss.

As you spend more time together and get to know each other, ask about their dreams and childhood. Show interest and compliment them. Slowly move towards a kiss if the vibe feels right.

18. Repeat steps 13 through 15, and then after a few weeks, ask her out on an "official" date.

Keep repeating the earlier steps and continue to build a relationship with them. Once you are comfortable enough, ask them out on an official date.

19. Congratulations!

If you and your friend have decided to start a relationship, congratulations! Remember to take things slow and think about what is best for you both in your relationship.

20. At this time, you may also want to hang out apart from just organized dates (for the sake of hanging out, like with your guy buddies).

Make sure to spend time together apart from your organized dates. Hang out as friends and get to know each other’s interests outside of dates.

21. Have a serious discussion on whether you guys should be in a relationship.

If you’ve been together for a while and things are going well, have a serious talk about whether or not you should be in a relationship.

22. The relationship has either started, or you're still casually dating.

If you agree to be in a relationship, congratulations again! If not, continue to enjoy your time together and explore your options. Keep these steps in mind, and you never know where your next relationship may come from.

How to Make a Butterfly Costume

A butterfly costume is a classic costume that is perfect for Halloween or any dress-up event. Follow these steps to make your own butterfly costume.

1. Look for types of butterflies in a book at the library or on an Internet search engine.

By doing this, you can get inspiration for the type of butterfly and the colors you want to use for your costume.

2. Choose a type of butterfly with colors that you want to emulate.

This is crucial because it will determine the colors you use for your costume.

3. Look at the main color in the wings.

This will be the color you use for the main fabric of the wings.

4. Assess what the weather will be like when you wear the costume.

This will help you determine the type of fabric you should use for your costume.

5. Purchase one yard (0.9m) of polyurethane foam.

This will be used to give the wings structure.

6. Buy additional colors of felt in the colors you would like to use for wing accents.

These will be used to create the detailing on the wings.

7. Print out the wing template. You can also hand draw your wings.

This template will be used to create the shape of the wings.

8. Trace the wing template onto your main felt color with a fabric pen or chalk.

This will be the base of the wings.

9. Trace two wing shapes into the polyurethane foam.

These will be used to give the wings structure.

10. Cut out the wing shapes in the felt and the foam.

11. Cut out smaller butterfly markings from the template.

These will be used to create the detailing on the wings.

12. Pin one set of markings on one large piece of felt.

This will be used to create the detailing on one half of the wings.

13. Stack the wings as follows: whole polyurethane wing, whole decorated felt wing (face up), cut decorated felt wing, face down, cut polyurethane wing.

This will create a sandwich of the materials used for the wings.

14. Sew around the perimeter with needle and thread or a sewing machine.

This will secure all the layers together.

15. Attach elastic straps vertically near the center section of the wing.

This will allow you to attach the wings to your costume.

16. Buy a headband and pipe cleaners in colors matching your costume.

These will be used to create the antennae.

17. Wrap pipe cleaners around the top of the headband.

This will create the base of the antennae.

18. Attach pom poms to the ends of the pipe cleaners with craft glue.

This will create the tips of the antennae.

Your butterfly costume is ready to go! Wear it with pride and enjoy your Halloween or dress-up event.

How to Choose a Summer Beach Wedding Dress

Summer beach weddings are a popular choice among couples who want to have a fun and memorable wedding celebration. If you're planning to have a beach wedding, it's essential to choose the right dress that will make you feel comfortable and beautiful under the sun. Here are some tips to help you choose the perfect summer beach wedding dress!

1. Choose light fabrics.

When selecting a dress for a summer beach wedding, opt for light and breathable fabrics that will keep you cool and comfortable. Some of the most popular fabrics for beach wedding dresses are chiffon, organza, and lace. These fabrics will keep you looking stylish and comfortable throughout the wedding day.

2. Consider a designer dress.

If you have a high budget for your wedding dress, consider investing in a designer beach wedding dress. These dresses are usually made of high-quality materials and designed to perfection to provide a luxurious look. Choosing a designer dress may also provide you with more options to customize your dress to fit your style and preferences.

3. Combine light fabrics.

It isn't necessary to have the entire wedding dress made of something as sheer as organza. You can also have a dress made of a combination of light fabrics to achieve your desired look. For instance, you can opt for a bodice made of lace and a skirt made of chiffon.

4. Avoid full skirt dresses and trains.

As the beach is often breezy, a full skirt dress or a dress with a flowing train may not be the best option. Your dress may get blown around, making it uncomfortable for you. Instead, choose dresses with tea-length or hemlines that stop just above the ankle.

5. Consider adding some colors.

Don't be afraid to add some colors to your summer beach wedding dress! You can add pops of color to your dress through embellishments, beadwork, or embroidery. Pastel shades or bright tropical colors can add a touch of elegance to your dress.

6. Choose a column style dress.

A column-style dress can be both cool and simple but elegant with the right fabric. Choose a well-fitted dress that accentuates your body shape and flatters your figure.

7. Try a tropical or Hawaiian print dress.

For a lighthearted, fun look, consider wearing a tropical or Hawaiian print dress. These dresses are perfect for a beach wedding and add a bit of personality to your wedding day look.

8. Add a flower wreath to complete your look.

Finally, add a flower wreath to your hair to complete your beach wedding look. Flower wreaths are simple yet beautiful accessories that will add a touch of glamour and sparkle to your overall look.

In conclusion, choosing the right dress for a summer beach wedding can be a daunting task. However, with these tips, you can select the perfect dress that will make you feel comfortable and beautiful throughout your special day.

This has the arc-shaped scales visible through the window and a pointer which will indicate the values read from the scale.The arc-shaped marks on the meter dial face may be different colors that indicate each scale, so they will have different values. These determine the ranges of magnitude.
A wider mirror-like surface shaped like the scales might also be present. The mirror is used to help reduce what's called "parallax viewing error," by lining up the pointer with its reflection before reading the value the pointer is indicating. In the image, it appears as a wide gray strip between the red and black scales.
Many newer multimeters have digital readouts, rather than the analog scale. The function is basically the same, you'll just get a numerical readout.;
, This allows you to change the function between volts, ohms, and amps, and to change the scale (x1, x10, etc.) of the meter. Many functions have multiple ranges, so it's important to have both set correctly, otherwise serious damage to the meter or harm to the operator may result.


Some meters have an "Off" position on this selector switch while others have a separate switch to turn the meter off. The meter should be set to "Off" when stored and not in use.

, Most multimeters have several jacks used for this purpose.


One is usually labeled "COM" or (-), which stands for for common. This is where the black test lead will be connected. It will be used for nearly every measurement taken.
The other jack or jacks should be labeled "V" (+) and the Omega symbol (an upside down horseshoe) for Volts and Ohms, respectively.
The + and - symbols represent the polarity of probes when set for and testing DC volts. If the test leads were installed as suggested, the red lead would be positive as compared to the black test lead. This is nice to know when the circuit under test isn't labeled + or -, as is usually the case.
Many meters have additional jacks that are required for current or high-voltage tests. It is equally important to have the test leads connected to the proper jacks as it is to have the selector switch range and test type (volts, amps, ohms) set. All must be correct. Consult the meter manual if you're unsure which jacks should be used.

, There should be two test leads or probes. Generally, one is black and the other red. These are used to connect to whatever device you're planning on testing and measuring.

, This is usually found on the back, but is also sometimes on the side of some models. This holds the fuse (and possibly a spare), and the battery that supplies power to the meter for resistance tests.


The meter may have more than one battery and they may be of different sizes. A fuse is provided to help protect the meter movement. Likewise, there is often more than one fuse. A good fuse is required for the meter to function, and fully charged batteries will be required for resistance/continuity tests.

, This is a small knob usually located near the dial that is labeled "Ohms Adjust," "0 Adj," or something similar. This is used only in the ohms or resistance range, while the probes are shorted together (touching each other).


Rotate the knob slowly to move the needle as close to the 0 position on the Ohms scale as possible. If new batteries are installed, this should be easy to do - a needle that will not go to zero indicates weak batteries that should be replaced., Turn the meter on if it has a separate power switch. When multimeter measures resistance in ohms, it can not measure continuity because resistance and continuity are opposites. When there is little resistance, there will be a great deal of continuity, and vice versa. With this in mind, you can make assumptions about continuity based on the resistance values measured.


Find the Ohm scale on the dial. It is usually the top-most scale and has values that are highest on the left of the dial ("∞" or a sideways "8" for infinity), gradually reducing to 0 on the right. This is opposite of the other scales, which have the lowest values on the left and increase going right., If the test leads are not in contact with anything, the needle or pointer of an analog meter will rest at the left-most position. This represents an infinite amount of resistance, or an "open circuit." It's safe to say there is the no continuity, or path between the black and red probes.

, Connect the black test lead to the jack marked "Common" or "-". Then, connect the red test lead to the jack marked with the Omega (Ohm symbol) or letter "R" near it.


Set the range (if provided) to R x 100.

, The meter pointer should move fully to the right. Locate the "Zero Adjust" knob and rotate it so that the the meter indicates "0" (or as close to "0" as possible).


Note that this position is the "short circuit" or "zero ohms" indication for this R x 1 range of this meter.
Always remember to "zero" the meter immediately after changing resistance ranges or you'll get a faulty reading.
If you're unable to obtain a zero ohm indication, this may mean the batteries are weak and should be replaced. Retry the zeroing step above again with fresh batteries.

, Locate the two electrical contact points of the bulb. They will be the threaded base and the center of the bottom of the base.


Have a helper hold the bulb by the glass only.
Press the black probe against the threaded base and the red probe against the center tab on the bottom of the base.
Watch the needle move from resting at the left and move quickly to 0 on the right.

, Change the range of the meter to R x 1. Zero the meter again for this range and repeat the step above. Observe how the meter did not go as far to the right as before. The scale of resistance has been changed so that each number on the R scale can be read directly.


In the previous step, each number represented a value that was 100 times greater. Thus, 150 really was 15,000 before. Now, 150 is just 150. Had the R x 10 scale been selected, 150 would have been 1,500. The scale selected is very important for accurate measurements.
With this understanding, study the R scale. It is not linear like the other scales. Values at the left side are harder to accurately read than those on the right. Trying to read 5 ohms on the meter while in the R x 100 range would look like 0. It would be much easier at the R x 1 scale instead. This is why when testing resistance, adjust the range so that the readings may be taken from the middle rather than the extreme left or right sides.

, Set the meter to the highest R x value possible and zero the meter.


Loosely hold a probe in each hand and read the meter. Squeeze both probes tightly. Notice the resistance is reduced.
Let go of the probes and wet your hands. Hold the probes again. Notice that the resistance is lower still.

, It's very important that the probes not touch anything other than the device being tested. A device that has burned out will not show "open" on the meter when testing if your fingers provide an alternate path around the device, like when they are touching the probes.


Testing round cartridge type and older style glass automotive fuses will indicate low values of resistance if the fuse is lying on a metal surface when under test. The meter indicates the resistance of the metal surface that the fuse is resting upon (providing an alternate path between the red and black probe around the fuse) instead of trying to determine resistance through the fuse. Every fuse in this case, good or bad, will indicate "good," giving you a faulty analysis.

, Many times, the voltage to be measured has a value that is unknown. For this reason, the highest range possible is selected so that the meter circuitry and movement will not be damaged by voltage greater than expected.


If the meter were set to the 50 volt range and a common U.S. electrical outlet were to be tested, the 120 volts present could irreparably damage the meter. Start high and work downward to the lowest range that can be safely displayed.

, Insert the black probe in the "COM" or "-" jack. Next, insert the red probe in the "V" or "+" jack.

, There may be several Volt scales with different maximum values. The range chosen by the selector knob determines which voltage scale to read.


The maximum value scale should coincide with selector knob ranges. The voltage scales, unlike the Ohm scales, are linear. The scale is accurate anywhere along its length. It will of course be much easier accurately reading 24 volts on a 50 volt scale than on a 250 volt scale, where it might look like it is anywhere between 20 and 30 volts.

, In the US, you might expect 120 volts or even 240 volts. In other places, 240 or 380 volts might be expected.


Press the black probe into one of the straight slots. It should be possible to let go of the black probe, as the contacts behind the face of the outlet should grip the probe, much like it does when a plug is inserted.
Insert the red probe into the other straight slot. The meter should indicate a voltage very close to 120 or 240 volts (depending on type outlet tested).

, Rotate the selector knob to the lowest range offered that is greater than the voltage indicated (120 or 240).

, The meter may indicate between 110 and as much as 125 volts this time. The range of the meter is important to obtain accurate measurements.


If the pointer did not move, it is likely that DC was chosen instead of AC. The AC and DC modes are not compatible. The correct mode must be set. If not set correctly, the user would mistakenly believe there was no voltage present, which could be a dangerous mistake.
Be sure to try both modes if the pointer does not move. Set meter to AC volts mode, and try again.

, Whenever possible, try to connect at least one probe in such a way that it will not be required to hold both while making tests. Some meters have accessories that include alligator clips or other types of clamps that will assist doing this. Minimizing your contact with electrical circuits drastically reduces that chances of sustaining burns or injury.

, You need to determine whether or not the circuit is AC or DC by measuring the voltage of the circuit as described in previous steps.

, If the circuit to be tested is AC but the meter will only measure DC amps (or vice versa), stop. The meter must be able to measure the same mode (AC or DC) amps as the voltage in the circuit, otherwise it will indicate 0.


Be aware that most multimeters will only measure extremely small amounts of current, in the uA and mA ranges. 1 uA is .000001 amp and 1 mA is .001 amp. These are values of current that flow only in the most delicate electronic circuits, and are literally thousands (and even millions) of times smaller than values seen in the home and automotive circuits that most homeowners would be interested testing.
Just for reference, a typical 100W / 120V light bulb will draw .833 Amps. This amount of current would likely damage the meter beyond repair.

, Ideal for the homeowner, this meter were to be used to measure current through a 4700 ohm resistor across 9 Volts DC.


To do this, insert the black probe into the "COM" or "-" jack and insert the red probe into the "A" jack.
Shut off power to the circuit.
Open the portion of the circuit that is to be tested (one lead or the other of the resistor). Insert the meter in series with the circuit such that it completes the circuit. An ammeter is placed in series with the circuit to measure current. It cannot be placed "across" the circuit the way a voltmeter is used (otherwise the meter will probably be damaged).
Observe the polarity. Current flows from the positive side to the negative side. Set the range of current to the highest value.
Apply power and adjust range of meter downward to allow accurate reading of pointer on the dial. Do not exceed the range of the meter, otherwise it may be damaged. A reading of about 2 milliamps should be indicated since from Ohm's law I = V / R = (9 volts)/(4700 Ω) = .00191 amps = 1.91 mA.

, Even if the operating current is low and within the range of the meter fuse, the surge can be many times higher than the operating current, because the empty filter capacitors are almost like a short circuit. Blowing the meter fuse is almost certain if the DUT's (device under test) inrush current is many times higher than the fuses rating. In any case, always use the higher range measurement protected by the higher fuse rating and be careful.

How to Make Your Bedroom an Apartment

Are you a teenager who's tired of sharing a room with a sibling or just looking to add some independence to your living space? Why not turn your bedroom into an apartment? Of course, you'll need to ask your parent's permission before making any changes. Here's how to make your bedroom an apartment:

1. Clear out the room.

Before you can start decorating and making changes, you need to clear out your bedroom. This includes removing any furniture or clutter that you don't want or need anymore. Take the time to go through everything and decide what belongings you want to keep and what you want to donate or sell.

2. Make a plan.

Now that you have an empty room, it's time to start planning. Think about what you want in your apartment: a living room, kitchen, bathroom, and bedroom? Take measurements of the room so you can plan out where everything will go. This will help you make the most of your space.

3. Get a bedroom doorbell.

Living on your own means you won't have anyone to keep track of where you are or if you're asleep. To avoid any unwanted disturbances, install a bedroom doorbell to alert visitors when you're busy or need privacy.

4. Put a potted plant outside your door as well or a chair if you prefer.

Adding something outside of your door, such as a potted plant or a chair, can make your space feel more inviting and cozy. If you don't have room for a plant, a chair may be a better option. You can use it to put on shoes or just relax.

5. Add a little mailbox for letters, even if it's just a painted cardboard box.

If you want to add a touch of whimsy to your apartment, install a little mailbox outside your door. This can be used for mail or notes from friends and family.

6. Get a coat rack and a shoe rack.

Having a coat rack and shoe rack will help you keep your space tidy and organized. You don't want piles of coats and shoes cluttering up your living area.

7. Get a mat for the door.

A mat for your front door is always a good idea to help keep dirt and debris outside.

8. Get a small table and put some flowers and a phone with a cord on the table.

A small table with a phone and some flowers can add a charming touch to your apartment. You can also use the table for storage or other decorative items.

9. Put a mirror on the wall if there is space for a mirror.

Legoland Florida is a popular amusement park that brings to life the world of Lego bricks. If you are planning to visit Legoland Florida, it is essential to plan your visit in advance to avoid long lines and make the most of your time at the park. Here are some tips to help you make the most of your visit to Legoland Florida:

1. Purchase your "LEGOLAND Florida" tickets online (or over the phone) before visiting the park, particularly if you are visiting from outside the US.

Before visiting Legoland Florida, you can save time and money by purchasing your tickets in advance either online or over the phone. You can find information on the various types of tickets, admission prices, seasonal deals, and promotions available on the park's website.

2. Preview the attractions in the park before your visit.

Before visiting Legoland Florida, take some time to preview the park's attractions so that you can plan your visit accordingly. You can find a virtual map of the park, complete with a list of attractions, restaurants, and shops on the park's website.

3. Drive and park to the parking lot for Legoland.

When you arrive at the park, drive and park your vehicle in the designated parking lot. You can take a shuttle or walk to the entrance of the park.

4. Look at the setup of the park as you enter through the turnstiles.

Take the time to look at the park's layout as you enter through the turnstiles. Familiarizing yourself with the park's layout will help you plan your visit and avoid getting lost in the park.

5. Enter into the park through the section known as The Beginning.

As you enter the park, make your way to the section known as The Beginning. Here, you will find some of the park's most popular attractions, including the Island in the Sky ride, which provides an overview of the park's layout, and the Mia's Riding Adventure ride.

6. Ride the slow-moving ride called Island in the Sky to get a good idea what the park and the nearby landscape looks like.

One of the first rides you should consider when you enter the park is the Island in the Sky ride. It takes you on a slow-moving tour and provides an overview of the park's layout and the surrounding landscape.

7. Continue on into the next section in an almost clockwise manner.

After leaving The Beginning section, make your way around the park in an almost clockwise manner. Follow the crowd and enjoy the various attractions at each section.

8. Ride the spinning disc circular forward and back ride called Mia's Riding Adventure.

Mia's Riding Adventure is a popular ride in The Beginning section. It is a spinning disc circular forward and back ride. You can enjoy this ride with your friends or family.

9. Wait on taking in Stephanies Newsroom, as it's not been unveiled yet but is on the park map as being open.

The park is always updating its attractions, so make sure you check the park map for any newly opened or updated attractions. For instance, Stephanies Newsroom may not yet be open when you visit, so wait until it is open or bypass it entirely.

10. Watch the Friends to the Rescue’ Live Stage Show instead, if you'd like to watch Andrea's big premier at a park and see a little more about her life and her musical style.

One of the attractions that you don't want to miss is the Friends to the Rescue Live Stage Show. This show features Andrea, one of the characters of Lego Friends, and provides insight into her life and musical style.

11. Continue into this section in the clockwise pattern as you recognize that this section is the one section that makes LEGOLAND parks so famous to anyone and everyone who visits.

The Lego Miniland USA section is one of the park's most famous attractions. It features miniature Lego constructions of major landmarks all over the USA. Visit this section in a clockwise pattern, starting with the Kennedy Space Center area.

12. Inspire yourself for science and look at some of the LEGO figures made to look like the Kennedy Space Center.

The Kennedy Space Center area of Miniland features some of the most remarkable Lego figurines that resemble various spacecraft and constructions associated with space exploration. It is an excellent spot for anyone interested in science and space exploration.

13. Look at the Daytona International Speedway section for racing-inspired Lego creations to this famous nearby track.

The Daytona International Speedway section of Miniland features amazing Lego creations inspired by various racing events that took place at the track.

14. Visit the LEGO stylings of Las Vegas' famous landmark Strip in the Las Vegas section of Miniland.

The Las Vegas section of Miniland features a replication of the city's famous landmarks, including the Strip, various casino-themed buildings, and other popular landmarks.

15. Get a view of the Californian coastline in the "California" section of Miniland.

The California section of Miniland features replicas of famous Californian landmarks, beaches, and even the Golden Gate Bridge.

16. Look at the New York section for quite a few minutes.

The New York section of Miniland features landmarks from the Big Apple, including the Statue of Liberty, the Manhattan skyline, and other iconic landmarks.

17. Look at all the Star Wars inspired figurines sections in the Star Wars section of Miniland.

The Star Wars section of Miniland features various models of the iconic spacecraft, droids, and characters from the legendary movie franchise.

18. Walk through the LEGO figurines collection of pirate-inspired motif in the Pirate Shores section of LEGOLAND.

The Pirate Shores section of the park features pirate-themed attractions that are perfect for any pirate lover. Walk through the Lego figurines collection to get a feel of the unique pirate-inspired motif.

19. Look at the Florida section of Miniland.

The Florida section of Miniland features replicas of famous Florida landmarks and attractions, including the Miami skyline, Universal Studios, and the Kennedy Space Center.

20. Have your tots play in the DUPLO Tot Spot area of the Lego Technic area of the park.

The DUPLO Tot Spot area is a great spot for parents with young children. The Lego Technic area features various fun, age-appropriate activities that your tots will love.

21. Ride Project X, a full-blown roller coaster.

Project X is one of the park's most popular rides, with a full-blown roller coaster that takes you on a thrilling ride with lots of twists and turns.

22. Ride the "AQUAZONE Wave Racer".

The AQUAZONE Wave Racer is a water-based ride that is perfect for a hot day. The ride takes you on a thrilling journey through the water with lots of twists and turns.

23. Ride the Technicycle attraction.

The Technicycle ride is a spinning ride that takes you on a unique journey of motion.

24. Walk through Pirates Cove.

Pirates Cove is a themed area of the park that features pirate-themed entertainment, including live shows and attractions.

25. Get used to exhilaration of freefall in "Kid Power Towers".

The Kid Power Towers ride takes you on a thrilling freefall experience, with lots of twists and turns.

26. Learn a little about the Hero Factory line of products that LEGO makes and sells in the LEGO Hero Factory building.

The Lego Hero Factory building features a collection of Lego creations inspired by the Hero Factory product line.

27. Get your family together and play in the WB (Warner Brothers) Games Zone.

The WB Games Zone is a fun spot for families to play arcade games, board games, and other fun activities.

28. Try to build and program a LEGO Mindstorm robot in the LEGO MINDSTORMS attraction.

The LEGO MINDSTORMS attraction is perfect for anyone interested in robotics and AI. Try your hand at building and programming a Lego Mindstorm robot.

29. Build a LEGO car and test it out in the "Build and Test" exhibit.

The Build and Test exhibit allows you to build and test your Lego car creations on various tracks and obstacles.

30. Learn to drive a boat in the Boating School ride around a watery non-tracked area.

Visit the Boating School ride and learn to drive a boat in a watery non-tracked area.

31. Get healthy with the NFPA Rescue Academy as you help pump levers to help put out fake fires and help stop bad guys with water guns that can shoot far distances.

The NFPA Rescue Academy is a fun and educational spot for firefighters in training. You can help pump levers to put out fake fires and stop bad guys with water guns.

32. Obtain a LEGOLAND Driver's License after you've driven in an official real electric LEGOLAND car at Ford Driving School.

The Ford Driving School is one of the park's most popular attractions. You can drive an official electric LEGOLAND car and obtain a LEGOLAND Driver's License.

33. Have your tot's play in the DUPLO Tot Spot area of the Lego City area of the park.

The DUPLO Tot Spot area at Lego City is perfect for young children to play and have fun.

34. Get your thrill on when you ride the Flying School roller coaster.

The Flying School roller coaster is a thrilling ride that takes you on a fun and exciting journey.

35. Watch your kids laugh on Safari Trek.

Safari Trek is a fun and entertaining ride that takes you on a journey through the wild, with lots of laughs and screams.

36. Ride on the Coastersaurus roller coaster.

The Coastersaurus is another popular roller coaster at the park, with lots of twists and turns.

37. Ride Beetle Bounce with your kids.

Beetle Bounce is a fun and interactive ride where you can bounce up and down with your kids.

38. Explorer a Lego-themed medieval Egyptian castle in the Lost Kingdom Adventure ride.

The Lost Kingdom Adventure ride is a fun and interactive ride that takes you on a journey through a Lego-themed medieval Egyptian castle.

39. Let your kids join in on "Pharaohs Revenge".

Pharaohs Revenge is a fun and interactive activity that allows kids to participate in a mock Pharaoh's treasure hunt.

40. Let your kids draw out some energy on "The Forestmen's Hideout", but keep an eye on them, as some kids will lose sight of their security and there is a slight possibility of injury on unstable bridges and stuff that kids understand the most about.

The Forestmen's Hideout is a great spot for children to expend some energy and have fun. However, be sure to keep an eye on your children, as some of the structures may be unstable, and there is a slight possibility of injury.

41. Let your younger riders take part in their medieval joust on Royal Joust.

Royal Joust is a medieval-themed ride that is perfect for younger riders. Children can take part in their own medieval joust on this family-friendly ride.

42. Ride the ups and downs on hills on the Merlin's Challenge ride.

The Merlin's Challenge ride is a fun and interactive ride that takes you on a journey through hills and valleys.

43. Ride The Dragon.

The Dragon is a legendary roller coaster ride at the park that takes you on an exciting journey through the air.

44. Let your smaller children be creative and draw out excess energy in the DUPLO Tot Spot.

The DUPLO Tot Spot is a perfect spot for your smaller children to be creative and expend some excess energy.

45. Have your kids play in the fun park areas of "DUPLO Splash & Play".

The DUPLO Splash & Play area is a fun and interactive spot where kids can play in the water and cool off on a hot day.

46. Make your youngest children believe they are mowing some corn fields on the "DUPLO Tractor" (These corn fields will be next to the tractor, and the tractor will be moving on open land, but it will appear to some that they are doing something rather helpful with their parents).

The DUPLO Tractor is a fun and interactive attraction that allows children to imagine that they are mowing corn fields.

47. Get your children to ride on the "DUPLO Train".

The DUPLO Train is a fun and interactive ride where children can ride a Lego-themed train.

48. Let your kids frolic (while keeping an eye on them) in the "DUPLO Farm".

The DUPLO Farm is a fun spot where kids can frolic and play.

49. Let your toddlers play in the Toddler Play Room in DUPLO Valley.

The Toddler Play Room in DUPLO Valley is a spot where toddlers can play and have fun.

50. Continue through to the last land in the park called "Fun Town".

After visiting all the other sections of the park, make your way to Fun Town, the last land in the park.

51. Look into the Lego Factory here.

The Lego Factory in Fun Town is a spot where you can learn about the process of creating Lego bricks.

52. Try the Carousel.

The Carousel is a classic amusement park ride that is perfect for families with young children.

53. Learn from the stageshow called "Wells Fargo Fun Town 4D Theater.

The Wells Fargo Fun Town 4D Theater is a spot where you can learn through an educational stage show.

54. Visit the "World of Chima" land here next to and further away than the Duplo Valley area from The Beginning.

The World of Chima land in Fun Town features various Lego creations inspired by the popular Chima line of products.

55. Play in the water park area at the very back of the park.

The water park area at the back of the park is a great spot to cool off on a hot day.

56. Take a step back in time and lull around in the Cypress Gardens area of the park.

The Cypress Gardens area of the park is a spot where you can relax and take in the beauty of nature.

Visiting Legoland Florida is an exciting adventure for the whole family. With these tips and insights, you are sure to have a fun-filled and memorable experience in the world of Lego bricks.

How to Enjoy Your San Diego Trip

San Diego, also known as "America's Finest City", is a great vacation destination to explore and enjoy. With its beautiful beaches, perfect weather, rich history, and numerous attractions, San Diego offers something for everyone. If you are planning a trip to this fabulous city, here's how to enjoy your San Diego trip:

1. Decide the time period when you would want to visit San Diego

San Diego is a year-round destination, but the best time to visit is from March to May and from September to November. During these months, the temperature is moderate, and the crowds are relatively low.

2. Select the medium of transportation for traveling to San Diego and book your tickets in advance

Depending on your location, you can travel to San Diego by car, airplane, or train. Book your tickets in advance to save money and avoid last-minute hassles.

3. Choose a hotel accommodation or affordable lodging in San Diego which is centrally located

There are plenty of accommodation options in San Diego, ranging from luxury hotels to budget-friendly lodges. Choose a hotel that is centrally located so that it's easy to explore the city's attractions.

4. Plan out what you want to see in San Diego

San Diego offers endless opportunities for sightseeing and adventure. Make a list of the places you want to visit, such as beaches, museums, theme parks, or historic landmarks.

5. Rent a car in advance so that you can travel around in San Diego as per your convenience

Renting a car is the best way to explore San Diego as it offers flexibility and convenience. Book a car rental in advance to save money and avoid the hassle of searching for a rental car upon arrival.

6. After reaching San Diego, dress properly for the day-long trip in San Diego

San Diego's weather is usually warm and sunny. Dress appropriately for your day-long trip, wear comfortable shoes and bring sunscreen.

7. Start your trip by visiting the Old Town State Historic Park which is the most visited state park in California

The Old Town State Historic Park showcases San Diego's early history and culture. Explore its museums, historic buildings, shops, and restaurants.

8. Drop in at the world-renowned San Diego Zoo for some time

The San Diego Zoo is one of the most famous zoos in the world, showcasing over 3,700 animals. It's a must-visit attraction in San Diego.

9. Spend the afternoon at the Balboa Park, the largest urban cultural park in the USA with plenty of museums, theaters, gardens, old architectural buildings, shops, and restaurants

Balboa Park offers a wide range of activities, including museums, theaters, gardens, and eateries. It's a perfect place to spend your afternoon in San Diego.

10. Your next stop is the Coronado Beach

Coronado Beach is one of the most beautiful beaches in San Diego, known for its white sand and blue water. Relax and enjoy the scenic view, or try out some beach activities.

11. Finish your trip in the historic Gaslamp Quarter, which is known for its lively and energetic nightlife

The Gaslamp Quarter is a historic neighborhood offering a vibrant nightlife, excellent dining options, and shopping. Take a stroll down the streets and soak up the lively atmosphere.

By following these tips, you can ensure that you have an enjoyable vacation in San Diego. From scenic beaches to historic landmarks and stunning attractions, San Diego offers an unforgettable experience.

Printing a lengthy document or a web page in its entirety can be wasteful both in terms of paper and ink. Fortunately, almost all printing software allows you to print selected or specific pages from any document, web page, or email, which reduces waste and saves printing costs. In this article, we’ll show you how to print only a section of a document or a web page, selected text, nonconsecutive pages, consecutive pages, and images.

Method 1: Print Selected Text and/or Images
1. Open the document or web page you want to print in your browser.
2. With your cursor, select the text and/or images you want to print.
3. Choose “File” then “Print” from the toolbar at the top of your screen.
4. In the print dialogue box, select “Selection” under “Page Range”.
5. Click “Print” to print only the selected items.

Method 2: Print a Single Page
1. Open the document you want to print.
2. Scroll to the page you want to print.
3. Choose “File” then “Print” from the toolbar at the top of your screen.
4. In the print dialogue box, select “Current Page” under “Page Range”.
5. Click “Print” to print only the current page.

Method 3: Print Nonconsecutive Pages in a Document
1. Open the document you want to print.
2. Scroll through the document and identify the pages you want to print.
3. Choose “File” then “Print” from the toolbar at the top of your screen.
4. If you are using Microsoft Word, select “Page Range” (Mac) or “Pages” (Windows).
5. Enter the page numbers you want to print in the text box.
6. Click through the preview to make sure all of the pages you want to print are included.
7. Select “Print” to print the nonconsecutive pages.

Method 4: Print a Series of Consecutive Pages
1. Open the document you want to print.
2. Scroll through the document and identify a set of consecutive pages you want to print.
3. Choose “File” then “Print” from the toolbar at the top of your screen.
4. If you are using Microsoft Word for Windows, select “Pages”.
5. Enter the first and last page numbers of the series of pages you want to print.
6. Click through the preview to make sure all the pages you want to print are included.
7. Select “Print” to print the series of consecutive pages.

Method 5: Print Selected Images
1. Open the document or web page you want to print in your browser.
2. Right-click on each image separately that you want to print.
3. Click on “Save Image As” and choose where to save it.
4. Open the saved images in an image viewer or editor application.
5. Choose “File” then “Print” from the toolbar at the top of your screen.
6. In the print dialogue box, select the printer, paper size, and quality according to your preferences.
7. Click “Print” to print the selected images.

In conclusion, this guide has demonstrated five different methods for printing selected text, images, and pages from a document, web page, or email. These methods allow you to save on printing costs and reduce waste by selecting only the important information you need to print. Try them out and see how much paper, ink, and valuable time you can save.

Are you planning an international backpacking trip? Packing can be a daunting task, especially when you're trying to pack light. In this article, you will learn how to pack for an international backpacking trip with ease. 

1. Select the right kind of backpack from a reputable company, such as Lowe Alpine or North Face. Make sure your backpack has comfortable straps and is durable enough to handle the wear and tear of travel. 

2. Create a list of what you think you'll need for an international backpacking trip. This will help you stay organized and focused while packing. 

3. Pack a backpack with the following clothing items: two pairs of lightweight pants made of cotton or another fabric that dries quickly, two pairs of shoes (walking shoes and relaxing shoes), and weather-appropriate gear based on your location. 

4. Use see-through packing cubes for packing clothing. This will help you save space and easily find what you're looking for. 

5. Choose a sturdy freezer zip-lock type of bag or a PVC mesh bag to pack toiletries in. Remember to pack a good fast-drying towel. 

6. Save space when you pack a backpack with selected travel information such as guidebooks and maps. 

7. Bring a digital camera, charger and a high-capacity memory card. Don't forget to carry mosquito repellent. 

8. Opt for a small first aid kit with pain relievers, band-aids, tweezers, antibiotic cream, something for upset stomachs and hydrocortisone cream for bug bites. 

9. Set aside camping type equipment if a backpacking trip will often take you away from towns. 

10. Think about small extras that might come in handy, such as a travel mug or a portable phone charger. 

11. Keep money and important papers in a money belt strapped to your waist and worn under your shirt. This will help keep your valuables safe and secure. 

By following these tips, you'll be able to pack for an international backpacking trip like a pro. Remember, the key is to pack light and only bring what you truly need. Bon voyage!

Starting a car during freezing cold winter weather can be a challenge, especially if you live in an area with particularly harsh winters. The first step is to minimize electrical drain on the battery, as you don't want to waste any of the battery's power before you try to start the car. From there, we'll walk you through several steps to ensure your car starts and runs smoothly during cold winter weather.

Method 1: Starting a car with a charged battery

1. Minimize electrical drain on the battery. Turn off all unnecessary accessories, such as the radio, defroster, and heater fan.

2. Turn the key to start and hold it for up to 10 seconds. If the engine starts, great! Let it warm up for a few minutes before driving.

3. Let the battery recover if the car fails to start. If the engine doesn't start right away, let the battery rest for a few minutes before trying again. You don't want to drain the battery completely by continually trying to start the engine.

4. Consult the owner's manual. If your car doesn't start after a few tries, consult the owner's manual for specific instructions and troubleshooting tips.

5. For cars older than about 1985, with engines with a carburetor, gently push on the gas pedal while still parked. This can help pump fuel into the engine and make it easier to start.

Method 2: Jump-starting a dead battery

1. Position the running car as close to the car with the dead battery as possible. You want the jumper cables to easily reach both cars' batteries.

2. Hook up the jumper cables to the appropriate terminals. Make sure you attach the cables in the correct order to avoid damaging the cars or injuring yourself.

3. Allow the dead battery to charge from the running car for a few minutes. You don't need to charge the battery fully; just enough to give the engine a boost.

4. Try to start the car with the dead battery. If it starts, great! Let it run for a few minutes before driving.

5. Disconnect the cables promptly, but continue to run the engine of both cars for several minutes to ensure both batteries have charged enough for another start.

6. Replace the battery if necessary. If you find that your car's battery is frequently dead, it may be time to replace it.

Method 3: Keeping your car running smoothly during winter weather

1. Warm the engine with a block heater. If you live in an area with extremely cold temperatures, consider installing a block heater, which warms the engine before you start it.

2. Keeping your car's battery warm. If your battery is exposed to the cold, either park in a garage or use a battery blanket or heater to keep it warm.

3. Park indoors. If possible, park your car in a garage or other sheltered area to protect it from the elements.

4. Use thinner oil. Thicker oil can make it harder for the engine to turn over in cold weather. Switch to a thinner oil during winter months.

5. Use gas line antifreeze with fuel stabilizer. Gas line antifreeze can help keep your fuel lines from freezing.

6. For diesel engines, consider using fuel conditioner. Diesel engines can have trouble starting in cold weather, so using a fuel conditioner can help.

7. Keep your fuel tank full. A full tank can help prevent condensation and water in the fuel lines, which can cause problems in cold weather.

8. Replace your windshield wipers and wiper fluid. Make sure your wiper blades are in good condition, and use wiper fluid that's designed for cold weather.

9. Check your tire pressure and consider snow tires. Make sure your tires are properly inflated, and consider switching to snow tires for better traction in snow and ice.

10. Maintain the battery. Check your battery regularly for corrosion or damage, and make sure the cables are securely attached. Consider having your battery tested before winter weather hits.

In summary, starting a car in freezing cold winter weather requires several steps to ensure a smooth start. By minimizing electrical drain on the battery, jump-starting the battery if necessary, keeping the battery warm, using thinner oil, and maintaining the battery, you can increase your chances of starting your car in even the coldest of weather.

How to Feel More Confident Without Makeup

Makeup has been used for centuries to enhance and cover up certain features of the face. However, relying on makeup to feel confident can lead to dependance and low self-esteem. Here are some steps you can take to feel more confident without makeup.

1. Prepare by lightening makeup for a few days: Choose natural appearing makeup plus lip gloss, with natural or no eye makeup -- as a sort of dress rehearsal.

2. Take off makeup. This is the first step towards feeling confident without makeup. Wash your face and take advantage of the natural beauty you were born with.

3. Dress and do your hair like every day or better. Dressing nicely and doing your hair can boost your confidence without relying on makeup.

4. Get buy-in secretly; produce a kind of "reality-show": Try to get your friends and family to show their real faces without makeup for a week! The support of loved ones can help to make this process easier.

5. Expect and accept criticism. Not everyone will like your new look. However, it’s important to accept their criticism with grace and not get defensive.

6. Don't be "Angryman" (that's not heroic) or belligerent: Do not pressure anyone to say what they may or may not think, when you are sporting and shining your way through your naked-face-project in public. This is a personal journey and should not involve pressuring others to follow suit.

7. Fake it (courage) until you make it: Act open and unguarded. Emulating confidence can help you feel more confidence, even if it’s not genuine at first.

8. Go full-speed on after work or school extra-curricular activities as usual. Just because you’re not wearing makeup doesn’t mean you can’t still be social and make the most of your free time.

9. Go big-time, if you are called out, so be a "cheerleader": Courage is when you are scared but still go and do things -- even if you feel yourself breathing differently, shaking and trembling. If someone calls you out on your lack of makeup, embrace it and use it as an opportunity to show your confidence.

10. Go to events without unnecessary makeup. Challenge yourself to attend social events, like parties or concerts, without relying on makeup to feel confident.

11. After a week without makeup, admire your face in the mirror. Take time to appreciate and admire your natural beauty.

12. Get your school to have a makeup-free-day. Encourage others to embrace their natural beauty by working to organize a no-makeup day at school or work.

13. Praise the true-face of that popular girl that made fun of you the first day you went bare faced. Emphasizing positive reinforcement and showing love to those who may have once made fun of you can help promote a more positive environment.

14. Celebrate real life. Embrace the natural environment and everything that comes with it.

15. Check the workplace dress code, if any. While embracing your natural beauty is important, it’s important to ensure that your appearance is still appropriate for your workplace.

16. Dress for a promotion. Dressing nicely and professionally can help you feel more confident in your abilities.

17. Be humble in your approach to "facing" your real skin. Remember that confidence takes time and practice, and it’s important to be patient and kind to yourself throughout the process.

18. Be seen "as highly motivated". By showing strength and motivation, you can inspire others to embrace their natural beauty as well.

19. Be proud to show your face. Embrace your natural beauty and show pride in your appearance, without relying on makeup. 

20. Tone down the makeup for a few days. If you’re not ready to go completely makeup-free, start by toning down your makeup for a few days at a time, gradually working towards being comfortable without it.

Taking the PSSA (Pennsylvania System of School Assessment) can be a daunting task for any student. However, with a little bit of preparation, you can increase your chances of performing well on the exam. In this article, we will guide you through a step-by-step process of how to take the PSSA.

1. About two months earlier, start reviewing your basic concepts

Preparation is key, and the earlier you start, the better. Start reviewing your basic concepts about two months before the PSSA exam. This will help refresh your memory and ensure that you don't forget anything important on the day of the test.

2. Have a good night's sleep before the exam (9 hours). This will keep your mind fresh

A good night's sleep is essential for your mind to function at its best. Get at least 9 hours of sleep before the exam to ensure that your mind is fresh and alert.

3. The night before the test, set your alarm 10 minutes before you absolutely need to get up, then 8 minutes before, then 6, then 4, then 2, and the exact time you usually wake up

This step is crucial in ensuring that you wake up on time and are ready to tackle the exam. Set your alarm for 10 minutes before you absolutely need to get up, then 8 minutes before, then 6, then 4, then 2, and the exact time you usually wake up. This gradual increase in alarm times will help you wake up without feeling groggy or disoriented.

4. Eat a healthy breakfast

A healthy breakfast is important for providing your body and mind with the energy they need to perform well on the exam. Avoid sugary and fatty foods, and opt for foods that are high in protein and other nutrients.

5. Go to the bus stop 5 minutes earlier than you would before, to ensure being on time

Arrive at the bus stop five minutes earlier than usual to ensure that you don't miss the bus and arrive late for the exam.

6. When you get there, relax and stop badgering the teacher with questions

Stressing out and asking too many questions can only cause more anxiety and make it harder for you to focus. Instead, take a deep breath and relax. Trust that you have prepared well and that you are ready for the exam.

7. When she is handing things out, make sure she doesn't forget to hand something to you

Make sure that you receive all of the necessary materials for the exam, such as a pencil and eraser. If you notice that you are missing something, politely remind the teacher.

8. Listen carefully to every single thing the teacher is telling you

Listen carefully to every piece of instruction and guidance that your teacher is providing. This information can be helpful in understanding the exam questions and performing well on the test.

9. When you are starting the test, if there is a short passage with questions, read the questions first and as you go along reading the story, highlight the answers to the questions you remember reading, and if you didn't remember some, always look back in the passage

This strategy can be helpful in ensuring that you don't miss anything important and that you answer all of the questions effectively. Reading the questions first can help you understand what to look for in the passage, and highlighting your answers can help you keep track of what you have already found.

10. Do your best

Remember that you have prepared well, and that you are capable of performing well on the exam. Give it your best effort, answer every question to the best of your ability, and don't stress too much about the result.

11. When you are done, check your answers

After completing the exam, take some time to review your answers and check for any mistakes. This can help you identify areas where you need to improve and help you avoid making similar mistakes in the future.

12. When you get home, do not concentrate on mistakes that you made during the exam, but shift your focus on the next paper

It's natural to feel a little disappointed if you didn't perform as well as you had hoped. However, it's important to shift your focus to the remaining exams and moving forward. Concentrating too much on mistakes can only damage your confidence and make it harder to perform well in the future.

13. Do not meditate on exam day, just keep your mind alert and active

Meditation can be a helpful tool in reducing stress and promoting relaxation. However, on exam day, it's important to keep your mind alert and active. Instead of meditating, focus on staying positive, confident, and alert for the exam.

Administering a coffee enema can have adverse health risks, including colitis,septicemia,, and rectal burns due to the temperature of the coffee. There have even been reports of coffee-enema-related deaths., Despite holistic rumors a coffee enema is unlikely to have significant effects. The caffeine absorption from a coffee enema is far less than drinking coffee, and it has a negligible effect on blood pressure and heart rate.While limited use may not be harmful to every user, the benefits appear to be minimal.

, If you are considering a coffee enema, talk to your doctor about it first. It is likely that your physician will advise against the treatment, but she may be able to offer alternatives or to give you additional information about your prognosis. Other health and lifestyle changes may have a greater effect on your wellbeing with fewer risks.

, Since the theory behind the coffee enema has to do with the body’s response to caffeine, you should try drinking coffee instead of using an enema. You will absorb more caffeine by drinking the coffee without the other health risks of the enema., If you choose do do a coffee enema despite the fact that there are few likely health benefits and many possible risks, then do what you can to minimize the risks:


Cool the temperature to an appropriate temperature to avoid burns.
Keep equipment as sterile as possible.
Watch for signs of infection and seek medical attention immediately if you experience a fever after administering a coffee enema.
Do not discontinue other types of medical treatment in favor of this unproven alternative therapy.
Do not share the enema equipment with anyone.
Always wear vinyl or latex gloves when administering enemas.
