Dwarf and low-growing varieties work best, but nearly any type can be grown if you have a large enough pot.;
, A good container to start with should be approximately 12 inches (30.5 cm) deep by 12 inches (30.5 cm) in diameter. Larger varieties may need even larger containers, however, especially if the flowers exceed 3 feet (0.91 m) in height.

, A light pot may not be sturdy enough, and the dahlia could become top heavy as a result.

, This step may not be necessary if the container you use already has several large drainage holes that allow excess moisture to drain quickly. If your pot has small holes or only has one center hole, you may need to drill a few extra holes to improve drainage.

, A dirty pot could spread disease, and hidden insect eggs may be lying in wait at the bottom of the pot. Remove these threats by cleaning the container before you plant. Simple soap and water will usually do the trick.

, Standard potting soil alone is too porous and may lead to pour bud formation. A good potting mix includes both potting soil and garden soil, or potting soil and compost. Alternatively, non-soil mixes made of fine bark, peat, and various minerals may also work.

, Long roots will become entangled, and separating the roots will damage them. If the tubers need to spend time forming new roots, the flower's upward growth and bloom will be delayed and may be minimized for the season.

,


If starting the dahlias in pots that sit outdoors, wait until mid-April through all of May.
If starting the flowers indoors, you can plant the tubers in early April.

, Filters wick away moisture, preventing the roots from drowning. Placing gravel at the bottom of your pot will accomplish the same task, but a dahlia's roots need all the space they can get to grow, and the coffee filter method takes up much less room than gravel would. Placing filters over the holes may also prevent bugs from getting in.

, Do not pack it tightly; allow the soil to remain loose.


If using a pot deeper than 12 inches, you may need to fill it with more soil. When all is said and done, dahlias need to be planted about 6 inches deep, and there should be an inch between the surface of the soil and the rim of the pot at the end of the potting process.

, Make it damp, but do not soak.

, Dahlias require a lot of nutrients to thrive. Fish or seaweed fertilizer can provide additional micro nutrients, as well.


You can also purchase a copolymer slurry from a gardening store instead of using bone meal and fertilizer. Follow the package for usage directions.
If mixing the slurry, fertilizer, or bone meal into the soil causes the coffee filters to move from the bottom of the pot, remove the soil from the pot and mix the elements together that way. Simply add it back in, over the repositioned filters, once everything is combined.

, Lay it down horizontally and leave at least 1/4 inch space between the root end of the tuber and the side of the pot. If an eye has already developed, center the eye in the middle of the pot and allow it to face upward. The sprout will come through from this eye.

, Do not bury the tuber at this time, however. Instead, cover it with a minimal amount of soil so that you may monitor its growth.

, Lightly spritz the exposed surface of the tuber with just enough warm water to moisten the surface.

, Do so carefully to avoid damaging the stalk, since the stalk is still fairly fragile at this stage. Never cover the top set of leaves. Continue adding soil until only an inch of empty space remains between the top of the soil and the rim of the pot.

, Large dahlia varieties need extra support to prevent the stalks from breaking off. Use a four foot stake with most varieties. Metal works best due to its sturdiness. The base of the stake should touch the bottom of the pot, and the stake itself should be firmly held in place by the soil and/or wires tied to holes drilled into the sides of the pot.

, Give them a thorough watering two or three times a week. Dahlias growing in hot, dry climates may need daily watering. Never allow the soil to get soggy, however.

, Dahlias grow best with six to eight hours of direct sunlight.

, Many indoor dahlias do not receive enough light from the sun, and a plant light may be necessary to spur proper growth. Set the light about six inches above the top rim of the pot for newly planted tubers and raise the light as the plants grow.

, Use a low nitrogen fertilizer, and avoiding over-feeding.

, The removal of these leaves improves air circulation, which reduces the risk of mildew.

, Dahlias are subject to attacks from mold, earwigs, slugs, spider mites, aphids, and cucumber beetles.

If you're looking to rent movies, iTunes is a great platform to do so. With a large library of movies, from classics to the latest releases, iTunes provides an easy and convenient way to rent movies right from your computer or iOS device. In this article, we'll walk you through the steps to rent movies on iTunes.

Part 1: Accessing the iTunes Store
1. Launch iTunes on your computer or iOS device.
2. Navigate to the iTunes Store and select “Movies” from the dropdown menu.

Part 2: Browsing and Selecting a Movie to Rent
1. Browse the newest movie releases displayed on-screen, or select a movie category from the dropdown menu to view movies by genre.
2. Click on the movie you want to rent, then look for the “Rent” button displayed below “Buy.” Only select movies will be available to rent from the iTunes Store; not all movies are available for rent.
3. Click on the “Rent” button, then sign into the iTunes Store using your Apple ID and password.
4. Follow the on-screen prompts to complete your purchase using the credit card on file with iTunes.

Part 3: Downloading and Watching the Movie
1. Select the option to play the movie when the download is complete.
2. Try watching any HD movies you download on a device that supports HD playback if the movie fails to play on your current device.
3. Restart your iOS device or iTunes if the movie download fails to complete due to network interruptions or another cause.
4. Try updating iTunes on your computer or install the latest version of iOS if you experience difficulties with renting movies from iTunes Store.
5. Try using another iOS device or network if you have problems renting and downloading movies from iTunes Store.
6. Verify that the time, date, and time zone are accurate on the computer or device you’re using to rent movies from iTunes.
7. Disable or remove any firewall settings enabled on your computer or device if iTunes fails to download movies.

With these steps, you should be able to successfully rent movies from the iTunes Store. Happy watching!

Writing an article for your school newspaper can be a great way to hone your writing skills and get your voice heard on important topics. However, it's important to be aware of the two types of newspaper articles before you begin. In this article, we'll teach you the steps to follow to write an article for your school newspaper.

1. Be Aware of the Two Types of Newspaper Articles

The two types of newspaper articles are news articles and feature articles. News articles provide information about current events and typically follow a strict structure. Feature articles are more creative and provide in-depth coverage of a topic.

2. Understand the Structure of an Article

Articles typically have a five-part structure: the headline (or "hed"), dek (also known as the subheadline), lede (the first sentence or paragraph), body, and conclusion.

3. Learn What a “Dek” and a “Lede” Are

The dek is the subheadline that provides additional context and information about the article. The lede is the opening sentence or paragraph that grabs the reader’s attention and provides the most important information.

4. Be Aware of the Tone and Point of View of a Newspaper Article

Newspaper articles should be written in an objective tone and from a third-person point of view. Avoid using personal opinions or biases in your writing.

5. Read Example Articles

Reading example articles can give you a better idea of the format and tone of newspaper articles. Note if the example articles follow the five-part structure and identify the hed, dek, and lede of the examples.

6. Use Writing Prompts

Writing prompts can help you come up with topic ideas and develop a unique take on a current or controversial subject.

7. Brainstorm

Take some time to brainstorm ideas for your article and think about what will be most interesting to your readers.

8. Ask Your Editor for Story Assignments

Your school newspaper editor may have story assignments that need to be covered. Ask for these assignments, as they can be a great opportunity to get your work published.

9. Do Basic Research Before You Pitch the Article

Do basic research on your topic before pitching your article. This will help you come up with a unique angle and ensure that your article is accurate.

10. Set Up Interviews Early

If you plan to include interviews in your article, set them up early to give yourself plenty of time to prepare.

11. Prepare Interview Questions

Create a list of interview questions to ensure that you cover all of the topics you want to address in your article.

12. Use a Digital Recorder or a Recording App During the Interview

Using a recording app or digital recorder during an interview can help you capture accurate quotes and ensure that you don’t miss any important information.

13. Create a Pitch Letter

When pitching your article to your editor, create a pitch letter that highlights the most important points you plan to cover and your unique angle on the topic.

14. Get Feedback and a Word Limit from Your Editor

Before you begin writing, get feedback from your editor and ask for a word limit. This will help you stay focused and ensure that your article is a good fit for the publication.

15. Create a Strong, Unique Lead

Your lede should grab the reader’s attention and provide essential information about the topic in a unique way.

16. Avoid Flowery Language or a Casual Tone

Stick to a formal tone and avoid using flowery language or informal slang in your writing.

17. Write to Your Audience

Consider your audience and write your article in a way that will be interesting and accessible to them.

18. Use the Active Voice

Using the active voice will make your writing more engaging and easier to understand.

19. Back Up Your Story with Strong Quotes

Including quotes from sources can help support your article and provide additional information for readers.

20. Follow the Five Part Structure

Make sure that your article follows the five-part structure, including the hed, dek, lede, body, and conclusion.

21. Revise and Format the Article

Before submitting your article, revise and format it to ensure that it is well-written and meets the publication’s requirements. Once you’ve submitted your article, you can sit back and wait for your work to be published in your school newspaper!

How to Make Torrone

Torrone is a traditional Italian nougat candy that can be enjoyed all year round. Making torrone at home may seem intimidating, but with the right tools and ingredients, it's actually quite simple. Here's how to make torrone.

Ingredients:

- 3 cups of whole blanched almonds (680.4 g)
- 3 cups sugar (680.4 g)
- 1 cup clover honey (237 ml)
- 1/4 tsp. kosher salt
- 2 egg whites
- 1/2 cup confectioner's sugar (113.4 g)
- 1 tsp. vanilla extract
- Edible wafer paper

Instructions:

1. Prepare a large, clean working surface to use for making the torrone.

2. Line the bottom of a 9 by 13 inch (228.6 mm by 330.2 mm) baking dish with edible wafer paper.

3. Place 3 cups of whole blanched almonds on a large rimmed baking sheet.

4. Toast the almonds in the oven at 350 F (176 C) for 10 to 12 minutes or until golden.

5. Put 3 cups sugar and 1 cup clover honey in a heavy 4 qt. saucepan and stir to combine.

6. Heat the sugar and honey mixture over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until it reaches 315 F (157 C) on a candy thermometer.

7. While the sugar and honey mixture is cooking, beat the mixture of egg whites and kosher salt within the mixing bowl attached to the electric mixer until firm peaks form.

8. Add 1/2 cup confectioner's sugar to the mixing bowl and continue to beat the mixture for about 1 minute.

9. Continue to monitor the sugar and honey mixture that is cooking. When it reaches 315 F (157 C), remove it from the heat and let it cool for 2 minutes.

10. Pour the sugar and honey mixture slowly into the mixing bowl, combining everything with the electric mixer.

11. Add 1 tsp. vanilla extract and continue to beat the mixture until it becomes thick and glossy.

12. Stir in the toasted almonds with a wooden spoon.

13. Use your prepared working surface to form the finished torrone candy.

14. Place the torrone candy on the work surface and knead it for 5 to 6 turns.

15. Lay the 2nd layer of edible wafer paper on top of the torrone candy, pressing down lightly.

16. Cut the torrone into individual pieces.

Enjoy your homemade torrone! Store it in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 1 week.

On the other hand, if she starts the conversation too often, she might not be interested, either. When a girl likes a guy, she will likely try to show her interest without being too obvious or looking too needy. As a result, she will start at least half of your conversations if not most, but probably won't start all of them. If a girl does start all of them, it could be a sign that she is not worried about seeming needy because she only sees you as a friend.;
, Even if the girl is tempted to hang on your every word, most girls will resist the urge. If anything, a girl who likes you is more likely to end a text conversation first in an effort to show that she is interesting and has an engaging social life even without you. When a girl ends most texting conversations with something like, "Gotta go, I'm heading to movie with my bff," she could be interested. If, however, she just stops texting instead of saying good-bye, she is probably not interested.

, A formal count is not necessary, of course, but make a mental note of how often she uses emoticons and which ones she uses most. The most often used emoticon a girl will flirt with is the winking face. The more often she uses an emoticon wink, the more likely it is that she has a thing for you. Standard smiles may or may not mean the same thing, but large smiles are another good sign. A blushing emoticon and a "kiss face" emoticon may also frequent her texts if she likes you

, If she quickly turns the conversation to herself, she might not be interested. Similarly, if she gives super quick answers like "K" or frequently leaves the conversation with little more than a "brb," she probably is not very interested. If she regularly jokes back with you when you say something funny, though, or asks you questions to keep the conversation, it demonstrates that she wants to keep talking to you for as long as possible.

,, If most of her texts are practical, such as question about assignments, then she may not be interested. On the other hand, if her texts are personal and ask about your personal life, she might be interested. This is especially true if she recalls specific details you previously mentioned to her about your plans. Another promising type of text is the random question. Look for something of no significance, like, "Would you rather live on nothing but your least favorite food for a year or give up your favorite food for the rest of your life?" These questions are fairly unimportant, and if she asks a bunch of them, it could be a sign that she is looking for an excuse to talk to you because you are on her mind.

, Specifically, look at how often she uses exclamation points. More exclamation points indicates more excitement at the opportunity to talk to you, which could signify that she likes you. A "Hey you!" is infinitely more promising than a simple, "Hey."

, Not every girl does it, but many girls add extra vowels and consonants onto words. For instance, she might text words like "heyyyy," "riiiiiight," "pleeeeeaaassseee," "hiiiiii" or "byyyyeee." The idea is to mimic the elongation of words often used in real-life flirtatious banter.

, Any text laughter is a good sign, but there are some that are better signs than others. Standard abbreviations like "LOL," "ROTFL," or "LMAO" can be sent to just about anyone, friend or crush alike. A "haha" is a better sign, because she wants you to actually imagine her laughing. A "hehe" or "teehee" is even better. These two forms of text laughing are the equivalent of a real-life giggle, rather than a loud laugh, and a girl is more likely to text you a "giggle" if she wants to sound cute.

, A common hint dropped via text is the invitation to meet in person. An actual invite to a party is good, but an even better invitation is the random “on the fly” sort, in which she casually mentions something like, “I think I’ll head to the pool. Meet me there?” These messages may seem casual, but in actuality, they usually indicate that she wants to see you and only you, and now. A strong desire to see you is a pretty good hint that she is interested.

If you have a loved one with Alzheimer's disease or dementia, finding a nursing home that can meet their unique needs is important. There are several steps you can take to find the best nursing home for your loved one. In this article, we'll explore the different factors to consider when choosing a nursing home.

1. Determine your ability to pay for nursing home care.

Before you start looking for a nursing home, it's important to determine your budget and what you can afford. Nursing home care can be expensive, so you'll want to factor costs into your decision-making process. Consider whether your loved one has long-term care insurance or qualifies for Medicaid.

2. Research available nursing homes online.

The internet is a great resource for finding nursing homes in your area. Many nursing homes have websites that you can visit to learn more about their services and amenities. You can also use review sites like Yelp and Google Reviews to read about the experiences of other families who have used the nursing home's services.

3. Contact individual nursing homes through their websites.

Once you've identified a few nursing homes that seem like a good fit, reach out to them directly. You can typically do this through their websites or by phone. Ask about the availability of beds and whether they have experience working with Alzheimer's and dementia patients.

4. Consult with experts in nursing home care for recommendations.

There are a variety of professionals who can provide recommendations for nursing homes. This includes doctors, nurses, and social workers who work with older adults. You may also want to consult with your state's long-term care ombudsman, who can provide insight into the rankings for a given nursing home as well as a record of complaints filed against that home and their resolution.

5. Balance your loved one's needs with your own.

When choosing a nursing home, you'll need to balance your loved one's needs with your own. This includes considering things like location, visiting hours, and the availability of specialized care. It's important to find a nursing home that meets both your loved one's needs and your own needs as their caregiver.

6. Visit prospective nursing homes.

Once you've narrowed down your list of potential nursing homes, visit each one in person. Take along copies of any nursing home checklists you may have found online. During your visit, pay attention to cleanliness, the friendliness of staff members, and the availability of activities and amenities.

7. Listen as well as look.

During your visit, listen to your gut instincts as well as your observations. Take note of any red flags or warning signs, such as a strong antiseptic smell that may indicate poor handling of incontinent residents.

8. Interview the nursing home's administrator and staff.

Talk to the nursing home's administrator and staff members to get a better sense of their approach to care. Ask about how the home handles Alzheimer's and dementia patients as well as how it handles general issues such as falls and incontinence. Ask the staff about their workload, employee morale, and turnover rates.

9. Ask both the administrators and staff about any upcoming changes in ownership.

Sometimes nursing homes may be in a state of transition and undergoing ownership changes. It's important to know about any upcoming changes that could impact your loved one's care.

10. Ask residents' families about the quality of care the home delivers.

Lastly, ask other families who have loved ones in the nursing home about their experiences. This can provide valuable insights into the quality of care your loved one can expect to receive.

Finding the right nursing home for someone with Alzheimer's disease or dementia can take time and effort. By taking these steps, you can increase your chances of finding a home that meets your loved one's needs and gives you peace of mind.

Building a wooden louvered gable vent can be a great way to enhance the ventilation in your attic and keep out pests like birds and insects. In this article, we will walk you through the steps to create your own wooden gable vent.

1. Measure the gable to decide how large a vent you can put into it and to determine the pitch of the roof if you want your vent to match it.

Take accurate measurements of the gable, including its height and width, to determine the size of vent you will need. You should also consider the pitch of the roof and how it will affect the installation of your gable vent.

2. Figure out how much lumber you will need and what type you will use.

Choose the type of lumber you want to use for your vent, such as cedar or pine. Determine the amount of lumber needed for your project based on the measurements you took earlier.

3. Assemble other tools and materials you will use for the project.

In addition to lumber, you will need tools like saws, drills, nails, screws, and a waterproof sealant. Organize these materials before beginning your project.

4. Lay out the shape and size of your vent on a flat work table or sheet of plywood.

Take a plywood sheet and lay it out flat. Draw the shape and size of your vent on the sheet using a pencil and ruler.

5. Rip the louver boards on a bevel.

Using a saw, cut the boards on a bevel so that they form the louvers needed for your gable vent.

6. Cut the face frame to the sizes and angles established from your layout.

Using treated southern yellow pine, cut the face frame, ensuring it is the correct size and angle established from your layout.

7. Assemble the face frame using finish nails or screws, then test fit it on the gable where it will be installed.

Use finish nails or screws to assemble the face frame according to your layout. Once completed, place the face frame onto the gable to ensure it fits correctly.

8. Cut the louver vane frame according to the layout.

Cut the louver vane frame to the lengths and angles established from your layout. Apply a waterproof sealant to the seam and use rust-resistant wood screws to attach it to the face frame.

9. Cut a compound angle on one end of a piece of the vane stock ripped earlier.

Using a saw, cut a compound angle on one end of a piece of the vane stock that has already been cut into louvers.

10. Mark the approximate length of the vane and cut it an inch or so longer than needed to allow for angle adjustments when it is fit into position.

Determine the approximate length of the vane and cut it a little longer than needed to allow for adjustments in angle when it is fit into position.

11. Begin installing the vanes, working from the shortest (top) to the longest.

Start with the shortest vane by nailing each end and keeping it flush with the bottom of the face frame so that insect screening can be stapled to it later. Repeat this step for each vane, working from shortest to longest.

12. Turn the louver over to complete nailing from the back side where the ends are more easily accessed.

Once all of the vanes have been nailed to the face frame, turn the louver over and complete nailing from the back side for easier access.

13. Caulk, then paint or stain the louver if desired.

Apply a waterproof sealant like caulk to the seams and paint or stain your louver if you desire.

14. Place the louver on the table with the back up, and stretch the aluminum insect screen on it.

Flip the louver upside down and stretch aluminum insect screening tightly over the back.

15. Cut the hole in the gable siding and install your new louver.

Choose an appropriate location for your gable vent and cut a hole in the gable siding. Install your new louver using rust-resistant screws and seal around the edges with a waterproof sealant.

By following these steps, you can successfully build a wooden louvered gable vent for your attic.

How to Do the 12 Steps

The 12 steps are a set of guidelines that can help those struggling with addiction to achieve and maintain sobriety. These steps are often used in recovery programs such as Alcoholics Anonymous (AA) and Narcotics Anonymous (NA). Here's how to do the 12 steps:

1. Admit that you are powerless over your addiction—that your life has become unmanageable.

The first step requires you to acknowledge that you have a problem with addiction and that your life is unmanageable because of it. This can be difficult to do, but it is essential to get started on your journey towards recovery.

2. Believe that a power greater than yourself could restore you to sanity.

The second step involves believing that a power greater than yourself, such as God or a higher power, can restore you to sanity. This belief can be a great source of strength and hope as you work towards recovery.

3. Make a decision to turn your will and your life over to the care of God or your higher power.

The third step involves making a conscious decision to turn your will and your life over to the care of God or your higher power. This can involve letting go of control and trusting in a power greater than yourself.

4. Make a searching and fearless moral inventory of yourself.

The fourth step is all about taking an honest look at yourself and identifying the behaviors and thought patterns that have contributed to your addiction. This step can be challenging but is crucial to moving forward.

5. Admit to yourself, others, and to God or your higher power the exact nature of your wrongs.

The fifth step involves admitting your wrongdoings to yourself, others, and your higher power. This step can be difficult but is essential to achieving healing and peace.

6. Be entirely ready to have God remove all these defects of character.

The sixth step involves being entirely ready to let go of your negative character traits and behaviors. This step requires a willingness to change and grow.

7. Humbly ask God or your higher power to remove your shortcomings.

The seventh step involves asking God or your higher power to remove your shortcomings and character defects. This step can be a powerful act of surrender and faith.

8. Make a list of all people you have harmed, and become willing to make amends to them all.

The eighth step involves making a list of all the people you have harmed because of your addiction. This step requires a willingness to seek forgiveness and make amends.

9. Make direct amends to those you have harmed wherever possible, except when doing so would injure them or others.

The ninth step involves making direct amends to those you have harmed, wherever possible. This step can be difficult but is necessary for rebuilding relationships and healing.

10. Continue to take personal inventory and promptly admit when you are wrong.

The tenth step involves ongoing self-reflection and accountability. You should continue to take inventory of your thoughts and behaviors and promptly admit when you are wrong.

11. Seek through prayer and meditation to improve your conscious contact with God or the higher power.

The eleventh step involves seeking to improve your conscious contact with God or your higher power through prayer and meditation. This step can help you stay connected to your faith and maintain a sense of peace and clarity.

12. Carry this message to addicts and/or alcoholics to practice these principles in all their affairs.

The twelfth step involves passing on the message of hope and recovery to others who are struggling with addiction. By sharing your experiences and practicing these principles in all of your affairs, you can help others find healing and hope.

By following the 12 steps, you can take meaningful steps towards recovery and achieve lasting sobriety. Remember to take things one day at a time and ask for help when you need it.

A head-to-toe exam is an important aspect of providing first aid to a victim. By conducting this assessment, you can identify any injuries or conditions that may require immediate attention. Here's how to conduct a head-to-toe exam during first aid:

1. Write down the acronym "DR ABC".

When providing first aid, it is important to follow the DR ABC protocol. This acronym stands for Danger, Response, Airway, Breathing, and Circulation. By following this protocol, you can ensure the safety of both yourself and the victim.

2. Evaluate the situation to see if you put yourself in immediate physical danger by assisting the victim.

Before you begin your assessment, it is important to check if the environment is safe for you to assist the victim. This will involve checking for any hazards or dangers that could pose a risk to your safety.

3. Check the victim's response to see if the victim is conscious.

To assess the victim's response, you can gently shake them and ask if they are okay. If there is no response, call for emergency medical assistance immediately.

4. Examine the victim's airway to determine if he is breathing normally.

Check the victim's airway by tilting their head back and lifting their chin. This will open up the airway, allowing you to check for any obstructions or blockages.

5. Analyze the victim's breathing.

Once the airway has been opened, you can check the victim's breathing by looking for chest movements and listening for any sounds of breathing.

6. Check the victim's pulse (circulation) and examine their body for signs of bleeding.

By checking the victim's pulse, you can assess their circulation. You should also check for any signs of bleeding, such as blood pooling under the victim or any visible wounds.

7. Ask the victim about the history of the incident.

To gain a better understanding of the situation, ask the victim about what happened and if they are experiencing any pain or discomfort.

8. Ask the victim to tell you their personal medical history.

It is important to know if the victim has any pre-existing medical conditions, allergies, or is taking any medications that could affect their treatment.

9. Have the individual describe their symptoms to you.

By having the victim describe their symptoms, you can identify any potential injuries or conditions that require immediate attention.

10. Examine the victim's body.

Put on a pair of gloves before performing your head-to-toe assessment. This will help to prevent the spread of any infectious diseases. Then, examine the victim's body from head to toe, checking for any injuries, swelling, or abnormal growths.

11. Observe the injured person's head for proper shape and symmetry.

Check the victim's head for any signs of injury, such as bumps, bruises, or lacerations. You should also check for proper shape and symmetry of the head.

12. Look at the condition of the person's skin.

Check the victim's skin for any signs of injury or discoloration. You should also look for any rashes, abrasions, or other skin conditions.

13. Examine the person's chest.

Check the victim's chest for any signs of injury or trauma. You should also listen for any abnormal sounds, such as wheezing or crackling.

14. Palpate the 4 quadrants of the person's abdomen: upper left and upper right (including the shoulders and ribs, down to their waist) and lower left and lower right (including the lower ribs and stomach).

By palpating each quadrant of the abdomen, you can identify any areas of tenderness or discomfort. This will help you to identify any injuries or conditions in the abdominal area.

15. Examine the pelvis.

Check the victim's pelvis for any signs of injury or trauma. You should also check for any tenderness or pain in the pelvic area.

16. Assess the person's capillary refill by pressing on their fingernail.

By assessing the capillary refill, you can identify any issues with the circulation. Press on the victim's fingernail and release it. The nail bed should return to its normal color within 2-3 seconds.

17. Exam the bones of the arms and legs to check for breakage.

Check the victim's arms and legs for any signs of injury or fracture. You should look for any deformities, swelling, or discoloration in the affected area.

By following these steps, you can conduct a thorough head-to-toe exam during first aid. This will help you identify any injuries or conditions that require immediate attention, allowing you to provide the best possible care to the victim.

When your Samsung A737 cell phone screen breaks, getting it fixed can be expensive. Instead, you can replace the screen yourself by following these simple steps.

1. Turn the phone off, remove the battery cover, battery, and SIM card.

2. Remove 6 Phillips screws from the back of the phone, two of which are under little rubber caps, which you can pry up with your flathead screwdriver.

3. Remove the two silver side buttons.

4. Disconnect the number pad connector from the main circuit board (it pops off by pulling gently up).

5. Slide the number pad out from under the main circuit board (gently!).

6. Disconnect the main wiring harness (marked in red) from the main circuit board (marked in blue, but the connector is on the opposite side of the board as it sits in the picture).

7. Pop the rubber keypad face (the thing with the actual numbers on it) out of the lower housing, and remove the white Samsung sticker and the skinny black sticker in the battery compartment.

8. See the two metal tabs marked in red above?

9. Remove the six Phillips screws from the back of the upper half of the phone.

10. The phone should be in three pieces now, the upper housing still holding the screen, the middle part with the spring-loaded section, and the bottom housing.

11. Flip the upper housing over, peel off the green piece of tape, and disconnect the main wiring harness from the back of the screen.

12. Disconnect the upper keypad wiring harness from the back of the screen in the same manner.

13. Gently pry the screen up, starting at the bottom, with a flathead screwdriver.

14. Peel the protective plastic off of your new screen.

15. Reattach the speaker and camera wiring.

16. Pop the upper keypad face into the housing (again, the holes line up, and it's pretty obvious where it goes), reattach the upper keypad circuit board harness, and press the circuit board in behind the keypad face.

17. Reattach the main wiring harness, and replace the green tape.

18. Reinstall the two siderails onto the upper housing.

19. Place the spring-loaded section of phone onto the upper housing with the screen, making sure you don't smash the main wiring harness.

20. Place the lower housing onto the upper housing.

21. Press the number pad face into the housing.

22. Reconnect the main wiring harness (red) to the main circuit board (blue) by pressing gently down, it should snap on.

23. Slide the number pad circuit board under the main circuit board...

24. Reattach its connector by pressing down gently.

25. Replace the side buttons (the tabs go down) and place the back cover of the phone onto the housing.

26. Put your SIM card back in, replace the battery, and put the battery cover on.

27. Hopefully, everything works well.

Now you have successfully replaced the screen of your Samsung A737 cell phone. Enjoy!

How to Buy a Lawn Tractor

Lawn tractors are handy machines that make lawn maintenance a lot easier. However, with so many options available, choosing the right one can be quite overwhelming. Here's how to buy a lawn tractor:

1. Determine what your needs are.

Make a list of the tasks you'll need your lawn tractor to perform. This could include mowing, hauling heavy loads, or plowing snow. You'll need to choose a lawn tractor that can handle the tasks you require from it.

2. Assess the type of terrain that you will be using a lawn tractor for.

Consider whether you'll be using your lawn tractor on hilly or flat terrain. For hilly terrain, choose a lawn tractor that has good traction and stability. For flat terrain, a lawn tractor with a smaller engine might be sufficient.

3. Consider a lawn tractor with a more powerful engine to efficiently tackle bigger loads.

If you plan on doing heavy-duty tasks like hauling or plowing snow, you'll need a lawn tractor with a powerful engine. Otherwise, a smaller engine might suffice for basic needs like mowing.

4. Decide what features you want for your lawn tractor.

Do you want a model that has built-in headlights or one with a cup holder? Make a list of features that you want in your lawn tractor to help narrow down your options.

5. Contemplate including some comfort and support features such as adjustable seats, steering wheels, and foot pedals with padding, and control levers that can be adjusted.

Ensure that the lawn tractor you choose has features to make it comfortable and easy to use for extended periods.

6. Think about purchasing a bagging attachment to collect grass clippings to make cleanup easier while you are mowing the lawn.

A bagging attachment is a useful feature for the collection of grass clippings. It's essential for easy cleanup of your yard.

7. Consider getting a sun canopy for additional comfort to keep cool if you live in an area where the climate tends to be hot, and you expect to be spending a lot of time on yard maintenance chores.

If you live in a location where the temperatures tend to be high, you might want to consider getting a sun canopy for additional comfort.

8. Educate yourself on lawn tractor safety precautions to safeguard yourself when you are using it.

It's essential to ensure that you take all the necessary safety precautions before using your lawn tractor.

9. Determine how much money you want to invest in a lawn tractor.

Set a budget for your lawn tractor purchase as they come in different price ranges. This will help you narrow down your search.

10. Read reviews for lawn tractors to be informed on the best and most reliable models.

Consult reviews of different lawn tractor models to make informed decisions on the best models in the market.

11. Do some comparison shopping and visit your local garden centers to look at some models that you are interested in.

Visit your local garden center to find out the different models that they stock. This will provide you with an opportunity to compare the different models and choose the best. 

In conclusion, purchasing the right lawn tractor is a process that requires careful considerations. This guide will help you make an informed decision on the model to choose to suit your specific needs.

How to Eat More Vitamin C

Vitamin C is an important micronutrient that our body needs to function properly. It supports the immune system and helps prevent cell mutations and premature aging. There are several ways to eat more vitamin C in your diet, which can help keep you healthy and feeling your best.

1. Learn about vitamin C.

Before you start incorporating more vitamin C into your diet, it's important to understand what it does and how much you need. Vitamin C has a role in sustaining memory, preventing oxidation of fatty foods, and supporting the immune system. Some people believe that vitamin C cures or stops the common cold, but there is no strong scientific evidence proving that this is true.

2. Understand the role between diet and vitamin C intake.

The best way to get your daily recommended dose of vitamin C is through your diet. It's important to eat foods rich in vitamin C to ensure that you're getting enough of this important micronutrient.

3. Be aware of the relationship between vitamin C and stress.

Stress can deplete vitamin C levels in your body. If you're going through a particularly stressful time, it's even more important to make sure you're getting enough vitamin C in your diet.

4. Notice the signs of possible vitamin C deficiency.

If you're not getting enough vitamin C, you may experience symptoms such as fatigue, muscle weakness, joint and muscle aches, and easy bruising.

5. Be aware that you need to ingest vitamin C daily.

Unlike some other micronutrients, your body does not store vitamin C. This means that you need to ingest it daily to maintain your levels.

6. Get enough vitamin C in your diet.

To get enough vitamin C in your diet, you should be eating a variety of fruits and vegetables every day. The recommended daily intake of vitamin C for adults is 75 milligrams for women and 90 milligrams for men.

7. Eat fruits and vegetables rich in vitamin C.

Some fruits and vegetables that are especially high in vitamin C include raw pineapple, asparagus, raw broccoli, sun-dried tomatoes in oil, raw parsley, and citrus fruits like oranges and grapefruits.

8. Be aware of recommended vitamin C intake.

It's important to know how much vitamin C you should be getting each day and to make sure you're getting it from a variety of sources. Too much vitamin C can also be harmful, so don't overdo it.

9. Be aware of the shelf life of your vitamin C sources.

Vitamin C can degrade over time, especially when exposed to air, heat, or light. Make sure to store fruits and vegetables properly and use them before they go bad.

10. Know which sources of vitamin C are the best.

There are many different sources of vitamin C, but some are better than others. Fruits and vegetables are the best sources, but you can also get vitamin C from supplements and fortified foods.

11. Eat plenty of green, leafy vegetables.

Green, leafy vegetables like broccoli, cabbage, Brussels sprouts, and collard greens are also great sources of vitamin C.

12. Add potatoes to your diet.

Potatoes are another good source of vitamin C. Just one medium-sized potato contains about 30 milligrams of vitamin C.

13. Take care when counting juice as part of your vitamin C intake.

Even though fruit juice can be a good source of vitamin C, it's important to be mindful of the sugar content and to drink it in moderation.

14. Consume fresh juice.

If you're going to drink juice, try to stick to fresh-squeezed juice to get the most nutrients and avoid added sugars.

15. Take vitamin C supplements in pill form.

If you're not able to get enough vitamin C from your diet alone, you can take a vitamin C supplement in pill form.

16. Use a topical supplement.

Some vitamin C supplements come in a topical cream or serum form. These can be applied directly to your skin to help improve its appearance.

17. Eat chewable vitamin C tablets.

Chewable vitamin C tablets can be a fun and tasty way to get your daily dose of vitamin C.

18. Eat vitamin C lozenges.

Vitamin C lozenges can be a great way to soothe a sore throat while also getting your daily dose of vitamin C.

In conclusion, vitamin C is an important micronutrient that our body needs to function properly. By incorporating more fruits, vegetables, and other sources of vitamin C into your diet, you can help support your immune system and keep yourself healthy.

A quiet room allows instant amplification of the heart sounds. This eliminates the chance of missing out on an abnormal heartbeat.


If you are a male medical professional, it is advisable to find a female companion before proceeding on physical examination of a female patient. The rationale behind this set-up is that a female companion will side with you if the female patient alleges that sexual harassment took place.
This ensures the safety and professionalism of the medical professional and gives peace of mind and protection to the female patient.;
, Auscultation of the heart can cause anxiety for patients, especially first timers. Therefore, taking the time to inform them about what you're going to do lets the patient know what to expect during the examination and helps them to remain calm.


This brief chat before the exam also helps to create a rapport between the patient and the medical professional and provides a sense of trust.
Also take this as an opportunity to notify the patient that the examination will be done with no clothing and or undergarments on the upper body to ensure proper auscultation.

, Leave the room while they undress to maintain privacy.


Warm the stethoscope with your hands as you wait. A cold stethoscope causes tightening of the skin. Tight skin will impede smooth traveling of heart sounds to the stethoscope.
Knock before re-entering the examination room to ensure that the patient is already prepared for the exam.
Be sure to offer the patient a drape to cover their upper body with. These are usually present in the examination room. Ask the physician you are working with where to find them if you are unsure.
A patient with his or her bare chest exposed can feel uneasy, so it is important to provide the patient with a proper drape for their comfort and as an indication of professionalism. The drape can be removed as needed during the cardiac exam., Standing at the right side makes auscultation easier., Also known as palpation, place your right hand over the patient's left pectoral. The heel of your hand should be against the sternal border and your fingers should lie just below the nipple. You hand should lay flat against the chest, fingers extended.Make sure you tell the patient what you are going to do before you begin, and explain the purpose. While palpating, be aware of the following:Can you feel a point of maximum impulse (PMI), which indicates the location of the left ventricle? Try to pinpoint its exact location, which is usually near the mid-clavicular line.If the ventricle is normal-sized and functioning, it should be about the size of a penny. If it is enlarged, it may be located closer to the armpit.
What is the duration of the impulse? If the patient suffers from hypertension, the impulse will last longer. This is subtle and largely a subjective assessment, however.
How strong is the impulse?
Do you feel a vibration (or thrill)? If a valve is partially obstructed, you may detect this. If you notice a murmur during auscultation, then check again for a thrill.

, Gentle upward displacement of the left breast must be done in women to hear the heart sounds. Once the diaphragm of your stethoscope is in place, listen intently.


The diaphragm is the auscultatory part of the stethoscope with the wider circumference and flat surface.The diaphragm helps in auscultation of high-pitched normal heart sounds.
There are two normal heart sounds, S1 and S2.S1 corresponds to mitral and tricuspid heart valve closure during heart contraction. S2 corresponds to aortic and pulmonic valve closure during heart relaxation. S1 is louder than S2 at the apex since it is closer to the mitral valve.

, After auscultating the apex, it is important to listen to the following areas with your stethoscope:

The left side of the patient's sternum, at the bottom (in the 5th intercostal space). This is the best place to auscultate the tricuspid valve.The left side of the patient's sternum, near the top (in the 2nd intercostal space). This is the best place to auscultate the pulmonic valve.The right side of the patient's sternum, near the top (in the 2nd intercostal space). This is the best place to auscultate the aortic valve.Note that listening to the heart's apex is the best spot to assess the mitral valve., The bell is the auscultatory part of the stethoscope with the smaller circumference and concave surface. It is sensitive to abnormal heart sounds called murmurs.The bell should be applied lightly over the skin to increase sensitivity to murmurs. Pinch the sides of the bell with your thumb and index finger. Rest the heel of your hand against the patient’s chest to ensure that the bell is placed lightly.
The bell should create an air-tight seal with the skin to easily hear abnormal heart sounds.

, This position amplifies the apex’s heart sounds.Place the bell lightly on the apex and listen for murmurs.


Ask the patient to sit up, lean forward, exhale completely and suspend breathing. This maneuver accentuates murmurs.Both of these maneuvers are "specialty techniques" used to better assess for any heart pathology or murmurs.

, Do not discuss your physical examination findings with the patient still undressed.

, While palpating the pulse, establish which sound is the first one (S1). The S1 sound is the one that is synchronized with the pulse. Then you need to establish whether the rhythm is a regular one or an irregular one, by following the S1 sound.If the rhythm is irregular, an electrocardiogram must be taken immediately.

, If his resting heart rate is below 60 bpm or above 100 bpm, an electrocardiogram should also be taken and additional medication may follow.


You need to keep in mind that sometimes a patient’s pulse may not be identical with his heartbeat, like in atrial fibrillation. For this reason, it is preferable to listen to the patient’s heart without taking his pulse when assessing his cardiac rhythm and rate.
By counting how many sounds you hear between the S1 sounds, you can establish if it is a gallop rhythm (when you hear an extra two or even three sounds between the S1 sounds).A gallop rhythm often indicates heart problems, but it is normal in children and athletes.

, Murmurs are pathological heart sounds with long duration, usually lasting between either S1 to S2 or S2 to S1. "Systolic murmurs" are the ones that can be heard between S1 to S2, while "diastolic murmurs" are the ones that can be heard between S2 and S1.Mitral insufficiency is characterized by a systolic murmur heard at the mitral area.Mitral stenosis is characterized by a diastolic murmur heard at the mitral area.Aortic insufficiency is characterized by a diastolic murmur heard at the aortic area.Aortic stenosis is characterized by a systolic murmur heard at the aortic area.
Ventricular and atrial septal defects are characterized by both systolic and diastolic murmurs., A gallop is an extra heart sound that occurs following S2 (S3) or just before S1 (S4). The S3 and S4 heart sounds are best heard with the bell of the stethoscope.

An S3 is normal in patients under 40, but in older patients it may indicate left ventricular failure. It occurs during ventricular filling and is usually due to an enlargement of the ventricular chamber.
The presence of an S3 indicates decreased contractility, myocardial failure, or volume overload of the ventricle.
An S4 is due to decreased ventricular compliance, increased ventricular stiffness, and increased resistance. This can be heard in trained athletes or in older adults.
Causes of S4 include hypertensive heart disease, coronary artery disease, aortic stenosis, and cardiomyopathy.

Growing flowers, ornamentals, or vegetables all follow basic principles, and the choice of what you grow is up to you.;
, Even if they are not close friends, most hobby gardeners love to "show their stuff".

, In the USA, the Dept. of Agriculture has the Agricultural Extension Service, and these folks are there to help answer specific questions about lawn and garden issues. Often, they can give up to date advice on local conditions, growing seasons, and successful plant varieties.

, Watermelons and tomatoes come in more than the standard red colors, so if you want yellow, go yellow. The same applies to beans and peas, with "bush" type, vining, or climbing varieties available.

, Many plant species have "dwarf" cultivars which take up much less space.

, Plants vary greatly in the amount of sun they can tolerate or require, and this information will help you decide what to buy. Tomatoes and many leafy vegetables simply won't thrive without enough sun.

, Garden supply stores and nurseries are good places to go, often offering better selection and more knowledgeable advice, but usually at a higher price.

, Basically, they will have information about the plant including its name, the temperature zone it is appropriate for, and the amount of sun it prefers.

, Large plants in small containers are likely to be "root bound", a condition where the roots have been confined in the container so long they are simply a tangled mass which will not flourish when transplanted to the soil of your garden.

, The individual packaged variety of seeds will have information included on the label as to planting season, spacing the seeds, and depth to plant, as well as sun, soil type, and drainage requirements.

,, Here, it should be said, some guesswork will be needed unless you consult someone familiar with your particular growing conditions. Adding a composted organic soil conditioner is more beneficial in sandy or clay soil, where rich loamy soil wouldn't require much, if any, but nonetheless, adding it shouldn't hurt. Fertilizer, on the other hand, can have more dramatic effects, and not always in a positive way. Too much nitrogen on legumes (beans and peas) for example, can cause a tremendous foliage growth with little fruit, so unless you are pretty confident, leave the fertilizer until later.

, Corners, particularly those with little shade are a fairly safe bet, and mowing around the garden later will be easier.

, Repeating this until a large enough area for your purposes is all the preparation needed, but a rotary tiller or other implement will give better results.

,,, It is better to space these out a bit wide, rather than having them crowd each other later as they grow. For peat cup grown plants, you can tear the "cup" loose around the roots of the plant, and stick the whole mess in the ground, but for plastic containers, you will need to remove the container before planting.

, If there is excessive branching or foliage, some plants benefit from "pruning", or removing some of this growth, which leaves the roots less mass to support until they are fully established.

, This can be one with a shovel handle, a hoe, or other tool, by dragging it through the soil creating a small "furrow", or grove in it.

, Many common garden seeds have a suggested planting depth of 1⁄2 inch (1.3 cm), and if the soil is kept moist until the plant sprouts and puts down a root, it should work. Take care when covering the seeds in the furrow not to pack the soil too tightly, but it should be packed down some to set the soil firmly around the seed.

, When the plants are established or the seeds are up and growing, cut back watering frequency and increase water depth to encourage the roots to grow deep enough to support the plant as it matures.

, Yellowing or streaking of the foliage may indicate the need for fertilizer if the soil is poor. For beginning gardeners, a mixable soluble fertilizer may be better than calculating or trying to figure out proportions of a commercial type bulk material. Miracle Grow, Scott's, and many other brands, including store brands, are easily mixed and come with fairly comprehensive instructions on the packaging. One thing to note is that the product you use contains micronutrients and trace elements, like copper, zinc, and iron, all essential for healthy plants.

, These include anything from caterpillars to rabbits and even deer, all of which may have a taste for your new crop. Unfortunately, this article cannot cover all the possibilities, but if you see leaves that have been eaten or become spotted, you will have to address this problem.

, Mulch will help, although some plants may experience disease problems if too much mulch is used or the humidity is too high in your location.

, If you find one particular variety of beans or tomatoes is more successful for you, you will want to go back to it again next year.

How to Make a Carnation Corsage

If you're attending a wedding or prom, a beautiful carnation corsage can add a touch of elegance to your outfit. With a few materials and some basic floral arrangement skills, you can create your own stunning corsage in no time. In this article, we'll guide you through the steps for creating a carnation corsage that will make you the belle of the ball.

Materials:
- Carnations (spray or single, depending on preference)
- Filler flowers (ferns, leaves, or additional flowers)
- 22-gauge wire
- Florist's tape
- Wired silk ribbon
- Shears
- Wire cutter
- Pins
- Plastic container (for storage)

Instructions:

1. Snip each carnation head off with shears. Remove any leaves from the stem that will be below the corsage.

2. Snip some filler ferns, leaves, or flowers. Trim them to a length that complements the carnations.

3. Cut several 6 inch (15.2 cm) pieces of 22-gauge wire using a wire cutter.

4. Thread a piece of wire through the upper part of the stem, just below the base. Twist the wire tightly around the stem to secure it.

5. Cut a 6 inch (15.2 cm) piece of green florist's tape. Affix the top to the point just above where the wire is inserted into the stem.

6. Wrap the tape around the wire at a downward angle until you reach the ends. This will cover the wire and provide a polished look.

7. Arrange the stems of your filler flowers or leaves around your carnation. You should aim for a balanced look with equal proportions on both sides.

8. Cut another 6 inch (15.2 cm) piece of florist's tape and wrap the stems around the artificial stem. This will help hold everything in place.

9. Repeat this process for each of the spray or single carnations you are using.

10. Arrange each of the carnations in the order you would like them to appear. You can experiment with different arrangements until you find one that looks best to you.

11. Cut a long piece of florist's tape. Wrap the tape around the base of the corsage several times to create a sturdy stem.

12. Cut a very long piece of wired silk ribbon. Create a loop in the ribbon and place it between your thumb and forefinger. Continue looping and stacking the ribbon in 4 to 5 figure 8 loops.

13. Take a 6 inch (15.2 cm) piece of wire and thread it through the center of your loops. Twist the wire tightly to secure the loops together.

14. Align the ribbon with the base of the carnations. Wrap a long piece of florist's tape around the bow wire and the larger corsage stem to secure the ribbon in place.

15. Place a pencil or pen near the bottom of the corsage stem. This will give it an elegantly curved shape.

16. Place 2 pins in the top of the spiral, for pinning on a lapel or dress. Make sure the pins are securely in place.

17. Trim any spray that extends too far from the sides of the corsage. You want it to be compact and neat.

18. Keep the corsage in a plastic container in the refrigerator until it is ready to be given. This will help it stay fresh for longer.

With these steps, you'll have a stunning carnation corsage that will look beautiful and hold up for hours. Whether you're attending a wedding or prom, your corsage is sure to turn heads and make you feel confident and beautiful.

If you're interested in communicating with spirits or entities beyond the physical realm, creating your own Ouija board is a great way to do it. Follow these steps to create your own Ouija board:

Materials:
- Large piece of paper
- Letters of the alphabet
- Numbers 0-9
- Words "Yes", "No", "Hello", and "Goodbye"
- Glue
- Smooth surface for the pointer
- Soldering iron
- Varnish
- Embellishments

Instructions:
1. Begin by covering your large piece of paper with the letters of the alphabet, the numbers 0-9, and the words "Yes", "No", "Hello", and "Goodbye". You can print these out or handwrite them, as long as they are clear and easy to read.
2. Lay the letters in a circle, putting a sun in the top left hand corner and next to that the word "Yes".
3. Sit around the table with a couple of friends, who will be your partners in using the board.
4. Start by writing out all of the letters in one or two rows, making sure that the characters are large enough to read. In the row below that, write the numbers 0-9.
5. Write out the symbols for "Yes", "No", and "Goodbye", which will make communicating with the spirits easier. Cut out the characters, numbers, words, and phrases.
6. Adhere them with glue to the paper according to your desired layout.
7. To make the pointer slide, you can make it a smooth surface. You can use a small glass planchette or a circular piece of cardboard with a small window cut out in the center.
8. Get your board prepped by clearing the surface of any debris or dirt.
9. Draw out your design on the board with a pencil or pen.
10. Use a soldering iron to go over your designs, burning them into the surface of the board.
11. Let the board cool and clean the surface to remove any debris left behind. Smooth down any rough edges with fine-grit sandpaper.
12. Apply a layer of varnish to the surface to protect the board and give it a glossy finish.
13. Add further details like embellishments or paint.
14. Choose your layout, font, embellishments and colors for the planchette.

Lastly, it's important to note that using a Ouija board can be a serious endeavor and should be approached with caution and respect. Always begin by grounding yourself, setting your intention, and creating a safe environment in which to use the board. And remember to always say goodbye at the end of each session to close the board and prevent any negative energies from lingering. Enjoy your new Ouija board and have fun communicating with the other side!

How to Malt Barley

Malted barley is a key ingredient in the beer-making process. In order to make malted barley, you first need to malt the barley itself. This involves a process of soaking the barley in water, allowing it to germinate, and then drying it out. If you're interested in malting your own barley, here's how to do it:

1. Go to a local feed store or farm. You can often find barley for sale at these kinds of places.

2. Buy smaller quantities in the beginning. Until you get the hang of the process, buy just enough barley to work with.

3. Choose barley that is low in protein. This will help ensure that your barley germinates properly.

4. Inspect the barley before you take it home. Look for any signs of mold or damage.

5. Purchase some food-grade buckets. You'll need these for the steeping process.

6. Fill a bucket with 1 to 10 lbs. of barley.

7. Fill the bucket with warm water. Make sure the water covers the barley completely.

8. Let the barley sit in the water for two hours.

9. Strain the barley with a fine strainer into another bucket.

10. Clean your first bucket.

11. Return the barley to the first bucket and repeat steps 6 through 10.

12. After you've done this twice, let the barley sit in the second bucket and allow it to dry for eight hours.

13. Repeat the steeping and drying cycle one more time.

14. Choose a clean, cool surface on which to germinate the grains.

15. Spread the barley out in a single layer.

16. Mist them occasionally with cool water.

17. Split a kernel with a razor blade or knife to see how the acrospires are growing within the grain. This will tell you when they are ready to be dried.

18. Choose your drying location. You can use an oven or a dehydrator.

19. Leave the malted barley in the oven or dehydrator for about 24 hours.

20. Dry the grain until it has low moisture content of about 10 percent.

21. Place the barley in a colander and shake the grains in small batches.

22. Bag your barley and use it within a few days to a week.

23. Consider toasting your barley at 275 degrees Fahrenheit (135 Celsius) for one hour to get a pale malt, or 350 degrees Fahrenheit (177 Celsius) for 15 to 30 minutes, to get medium crystal malt.

Now that you know how to malt barley, you can use your homemade malt in your own brewing projects. Happy brewing!

How to Set up Mobile Broadband in Ubuntu

Are you having trouble setting up your mobile broadband in Ubuntu? Look no further! With this step-by-step guide, you'll be connected to the internet in no time.

Step 1: Log into your Linux Mint KDE 9 Desktop
The first step to setting up your mobile broadband is to log into your Linux Mint KDE 9 desktop. Once you're logged in, you're ready to move on to the next step.

Step 2: Plug in your modem
Now that you're logged into your desktop, it's time to plug in your modem. Make sure it's properly connected to your computer before moving on to the next step.

Step 3: Notice the Cell Phone in the Task panel/Task bar
Once your modem is plugged in and recognized by your system, look for the Cell Phone icon in the taskbar or task panel.

Step 4: Click the Mobile Broadband Tab within the Network Connections window
Now that you've located the Cell Phone icon, click on it to open the Network Connections window. From there, click on the Mobile Broadband tab.

Step 5: Highlight that connection and click the Edit menu to the right
Find your mobile broadband connection in the list, and highlight it. Then, click on the Edit menu located to the right of the list.

Step 6: Type *99# in the Number box
Once you're in the edit window for your connection, type in *99# in the number box.

Step 7: Leave the Username box, the Password box, the PIN box and the PUK box empty unless your Modem doesn't require them
Unless your modem requires a username, password, PIN, or PUK, leave those boxes empty.

Step 8: Type your Internet Service Providers name in the APN box
Type your internet service provider's name in the APN box. This is usually provided by your mobile broadband service provider.

Step 9: In the Connection Name box, type in a name for your Modem
Type in a connection name for your modem. This name can be anything you want.

Step 10: Go to the PPP tab and ensure that all the Authentication Methods are checked
Next, go to the PPP tab and check all the Authentication Methods that are available.

Step 11: Leave the rest as it is and click OK
Leave everything else as it is, and click OK to save your settings.

Step 12: Check the Connect automatically check box
If you want your modem to automatically connect as soon as you plug it in, check the Connect automatically check box.

Step 13: Connect to the internet
Now that you've set up your mobile broadband connection, it's time to connect to the internet. Simply click on the Cell Phone icon in the taskbar or task panel, and click on the connection you just created.

Congratulations, you've successfully set up your mobile broadband in Ubuntu. Enjoy your new internet connection!

How much grass do you have to cut? Purchasing a lawn tractor is a good choice if you have a large size yard with more than a 1/2 acre (21,780 sq. ft, or 2,024 m�) of grass to mow.;
, Do you have a fairly level yard or does the terrain present a more challenging hilly landscape?

, Lawn tractors offer the option of choosing either a manual or automatic transmission. Select a manual transmission if you want to save money.

, If you will be mowing and doing general maintenance in low light conditions then consider a model with headlights.

, Added special features will make the task of maintaining your yard more enjoyable.

, Grass clippings can be used for compost or simply disposed of.

,, Some lawn tractor models offer additional safety features to prevent accidents such as automatic blade disengagement that stops the spinning blade.

, Expect to pay about $1,000 to $1,500 for a basic model. For additional features and attachments, the cost will be higher.
