, It should be located close to your AC compressor. This feeds the unit the voltage necessary for it to run. Often you will see a cable running from this breaker box directly to the AC compressor.

, There is a latch on the box (either on the side or bottom) that unlocks it. Once the door is in the open position, push it in slightly and it should lock in place.

, Set the breaker aside in a safe place.

,, Use the appropriate bit with a bit driver to remove the screws. Typically there are four 5/16" hex screws that attach the side panel to the rest of the unit. Place the screws in a secure location so as not to lose any.

, It will have a cylindrical, canister-like shape. It will have three terminals on top. There will be colored wires coming from the AC Unit to some if not all of the terminals on the capacitor.

, The top of the run capacitor should be perfectly flat. If you see that the top side is bulging upwards in any way, it needs to be replaced. If this is not the case, a faulty capacitor may not be the source of the issue. You may need to contact your local HVAC professional.





, The three terminals on the capacitor are labeled HERM, FAN, and C. Take a picture with your smart phone to record how everything is installed to make sure the new capacitor is connected in the same way. Jotting down these details on a piece of paper will also work.





, Inspect your insulated flat-head screwdriver. A properly insulated screw driver should have a entirely rubber handle. Ensure the handle has no exposed metal parts or cracks., To do this you are going to create a bridge between the C terminal and the HERM/FAN terminals, one at a time, using the metal part of the screwdriver. Ensure you are not touching anything metal. With the metal part of the screwdriver, touch a metal prong on the C terminal AND another from the HERM terminal at the same time, creating a bridge between the two. This may produce a shock. Do this two more times to ensure the terminals were completely discharged. Do the same to the C and FAN terminals.





, Use the appropriate bit and the bit driver to remove the screws. These are typically two 1/4" hex screws but this may vary. Set the screws and the bracket in a secure location.

, To be safe, always treat the capacitor terminals as live terminals. Remove the old capacitor., They can be purchased at your local HVAC Supply Store or they can also be purchased online through Amazon, eBay, etc... The simplest way to make sure you buy the correct one is to order the same model number from the same manufacturer. There are occasions where another brand might be more affordable. In this case you want to make sure to match the specifications on the old capacitor.

, Attach the appropriately colored wires according to how they were placed on the old capacitor.,, Plug the 220v breaker back in the ON position. Turn on the thermostat to cool. The AC unit should turn on within the next two to three minutes. Verify the unit is working properly. Do not attempt to reattached the AC unit's side panel while there is power running through it., Put the AC panel cover back on and secure it with the appropriate screws. Plug the 220v breaker back in and set the thermostat to cool. The repair is complete!

Learning and understanding the function of the various cells in the human body can be an overwhelming task, but with the right approach, it is achievable. The human body is home to a vast array of cells, each with specific functions crucial for the proper functioning of the body. In this article, we will delve into the various types of cells in the human body and their functions.

1. Understanding the Complexity of Human Cellular Structure

By virtue of its developed physiological structure compared to other animals, the human cellular structure is complex. The various cells in the body work together to ensure that the body functions properly. Understanding the structure and functions of these cells is essential in developing a comprehensive understanding of the human body.

2. Epithelial Cells: The Building Blocks of Tissues

Epithelial cells are essential cells in the body, making up the majority of the body's tissues. These cells are the building blocks of the body and provide the necessary support to other cells present in the body. Epithelial cells also play a role in the secretion of hormones, enzymes, and mucus.

3. Endocrine System Cells: The Messengers

The cells of the endocrine system are responsible for the secretion of hormones that play a crucial role in the regulation of various bodily functions. These cells work together in a complex network to ensure that hormones are secreted at the right time and in the right amounts.

4. Immune System Cells: The Fighters

Cells of the immune system play a crucial role in defending the body against invading pathogens. These cells work together to identify, isolate, and eliminate pathogens from the body. Differentiation of cells in this system plays a critical role in having the right defenses against different types of pathogens.

5. Blood Cells: The Transporters

Blood cells, including red and white blood cells, are responsible for the transportation of nutrients and oxygen throughout the body. The process of cellular differentiation ensures that enough blood cells are produced to enable this crucial function.

6. Liver Cells: The Detoxifiers

Liver cells are essential for normal body functioning as they play a crucial role in detoxification processes in the body. These cells help break down and eliminate toxins and other harmful substances from the body, ensuring that the body remains healthy.

7. Other Cells of the Body

Other cells in the human body include pancreatic cells, reproductive system cells, and many others. An understanding of these cells is essential for comprehensive knowledge of the human body.

In conclusion, the human body is home to various types of cells. An understanding of these cells and their functions is crucial in developing a comprehensive understanding of the human body. With the information provided in this article, you can start your journey to understanding the functions of the various cells in the body.

Middle school can be a challenging time, but with the right mindset and strategies, you can survive and thrive during this phase of your life. In this article, we will explore some key tips and tricks to help you navigate middle school successfully.

1. Know and obey your school's rules!

Following the rules is a crucial part of surviving middle school. Be familiar with school policies on behavior, dress code, bullying, and attendance. Make sure you understand consequences for breaking the rules, so you avoid getting into trouble.

2. Avoid drama and gossip.

Middle school can be rife with drama and gossip. Avoid getting caught up in negative situations. Refrain from spreading rumors or talking behind people's backs. Instead, focus on positive interactions and building healthy relationships.

3. Choose your friends wisely.

Your friends play a significant role in your middle school experience. Choose people who share your values and interests. Look for friends who are supportive, trustworthy, and positive.

4. Don’t let your friends get you in trouble.

While it's essential to have good friends, don't compromise your behavior and values to fit in. Refrain from participating in risky or illegal activities your friends may engage in. Stay true to yourself.

5. Don’t do anything that will harm your body.

Your health matters. Stay away from drugs, alcohol, and smoking. Make sure you're eating right and getting enough exercise and sleep.

6. Don't worry about relationships.

Relationships can be a distraction in middle school. Don't worry about finding a girlfriend or boyfriend. Focus on building friendships and enjoying your hobbies.

7. Don’t worry about gym.

Middle school gym class can be intimidating, but don't worry. Focus on having fun and getting some exercise. Also, remember to bring clean clothes and deodorant.

8. Learn how to solve problems.

Middle school is full of challenges, but learning how to solve problems is an essential skill. Use critical thinking and communication skills to resolve conflicts and overcome obstacles.

9. Always know that it gets better.

Middle school may seem challenging, but it's not forever. Remember that things will get better with time. Focus on the positive things in your life.

10. Find people you know.

If you're starting middle school in a new place, finding familiar faces can help ease the transition. Look for friends from elementary school or join clubs or teams.

11. Find people that live near you.

Finding friends who live nearby can make getting together for homework or other activities more convenient. Also, look for local community events to meet new people.

12. Be open to new friends.

While it's easy to stick with familiar faces, being open to making new friends can lead to exciting experiences and new perspectives. Engage with people who may have different backgrounds, interests or beliefs than you.

13. Join clubs.

Participating in clubs or groups that interest you is an excellent way to meet new people and explore new hobbies.

14. Show your interests.

Sharing your interests with people can help you connect and find common ground. Passionate about a topic or hobby? Let people know!

15. Act self-confident.

Confidence can make all the difference in middle school. Stand tall, make eye contact, and speak clearly. Believe in yourself.

16. Talk to people!

Initiate conversations with classmates, teachers, and other adults. Small talk can lead to meaningful connections.

17. Do fun things.

Make time for hobbies and activities you enjoy. Having fun can relieve stress and improve your mood.

18. Be nice!

Being kind and respectful to others will earn you respect in return. Help others when you can, and practice empathy.

19. Pay attention in class.

Being attentive during class is crucial for learning and performing well on assignments and tests.

20. Take notes.

Taking notes helps with retaining information. Find a note-taking style that works for you.

21. Do your homework.

Completing homework and assignments on time is critical for achieving academic success.

22. Stay organized.

Keeping track of assignments, deadlines, and schedules in a planner or digital tool will help you stay on top of your responsibilities.

23. Don’t procrastinate.

Procrastination leads to stress and can negatively affect your grades and relationships with others.

24. Ask questions!

Don't hesitate to ask for clarification on confusing topics. Asking questions shows a willingness to learn and improve.

25. Study as much as you can.

Study regularly to reinforce concepts and prepare for tests and exams.

26. Don’t stress over your grades.

While grades are important, stressing too much about them can negatively affect your mental health. Focus on doing your best; the grades will follow.

27. Explore yourself.

Middle school is a time of self-discovery. Explore your interests, strengths, and values.

28. Develop good hygiene habits.

Personal hygiene is important for your health and social interactions. Practice good hygiene, such as showering regularly and brushing your teeth.

29. Learn to balance responsibilities with fun.

Balancing responsibilities and fun is important for a fulfilling middle school experience. Prioritize tasks but also make time for self-care and enjoyment.

30. Get involved.

Participate in school events, such as dances, pep rallies, and fundraisers.

31. Exercise and eat healthy.

Maintaining a healthy lifestyle can improve your mood, energy, and academic performance. Exercise regularly and eat a balanced diet.

32. Work on your talents.

Nurture your talents and skills by practicing and seeking opportunities to develop them.

33. Don’t sweat the little things.

In the grand scheme of things, many of the little mishaps of middle school won't matter in the long run.

34. Believe that you are normal.

Many middle schoolers feel insecure about fitting in and being accepted. Remember that everyone has insecurities and that you are normal.

35. Get used to dealing with your period if you’re a girl.

If you're female, learn to manage your period by carrying pads or tampons and wearing appropriate clothing.

36. Learn to hide an erection if you’re a boy.

If you're male, learn to hide an erection by adjusting your posture or wearing loose-fitting clothing.

37. Work on your coordination.

Improving coordination can help in gym class and overall health. Practice sports or activities that require hand-eye coordination and balance.

38. Dress well.

Dressing appropriately and fashionably can boost confidence and positive social interactions.

39. Get a good bra, if you’re a girl.

If you're female, wearing a properly fitted bra can improve comfort and prevent discomfort.

40. Take good care of your body.

Overall health is essential for academic performance and well-being. Take care of yourself holistically, including physical, emotional, and social health.

41. Don’t stand for embarrassing pimples!

Acne can be embarrassing, but don't let it define you. Practice good hygiene and use acne creams or medication as necessary.

42. Stop bullying in its tracks.

Bullying is not acceptable. Speak up if you witness bullying and report incidents to trusted adults.

43. Get great study skills.

Developing study skills, such as note-taking, time-management, and test-taking strategies, is essential for academic success.

44. Learn to open your locker.

Opening a middle school locker can be tricky, but with practice, it will become second nature. Ask a teacher or friend for help if necessary.

In summary, surviving middle school requires a positive attitude, good decision-making skills, and the ability to balance responsibilities and fun. Use these tips to make the journey through middle school a successful one.

Creating a network documentation is a crucial part of managing a company's computer systems. A well-documented network can help administrators troubleshoot problems, optimize system performance, and save time and money. Here are some steps to create a comprehensive network documentation.

1. Ask for help.

If you are new to network documentation or simply unsure of where to start, don't hesitate to reach out to colleagues or online resources for guidance. They may be able to provide you with examples of other network documentation to use as a starting point or offer advice on how to structure your documentation.

2. Work out the format.

Decide on a format that works best for your company, whether it is a text document, spreadsheet, diagram, or specialized software. Make sure to choose a format that is easy to update and share.

3. Think about what information you need to collect in that documentation.

To get started, consider the following information:

- The number of physical locations where your company works, and how those locations are connected (wireless, wired).
- The number of routers, switches, firewalls, servers in each location, with passwords, types of operating systems, update management, types of hardware, types of RAID, period of guarantee offered by vendors for hardware and software, rules in firewall.
- A contact list with people responsible for administration and help desk.
- A contact list with companies that offer support for hardware and software.
- Documented and tested procedures for disaster/recovery scenarios.
- Places to store backups (on site and off site).
- Procedures for trial restores.

4. Make it as simple or as complex as you need.

Based on your company's needs, you may choose to include additional information such as IP addresses, software licenses, network bandwidth, and user access levels. But remember, simplicity is key: make sure the information is clear and easy to digest.

5. Analyze that drawing and see if you are satisfied with the details for each device.

Be sure to update the documentation regularly to reflect ongoing changes to the network.

6. Have a picture of how devices are connected, but also know exactly how to reach physically each device.

Include visual diagrams to help visualize the network architecture, as well as detailed notes that indicate where equipment is located and how it is configured.

7. Improve compliance.

Incorporate your network documentation into a larger compliance framework to ensure that your company is meeting regulatory requirements.

Overall, network documentation is a vital tool for monitoring and managing your company's computer systems. By following these steps, you can create a comprehensive, easy-to-use network documentation that will provide invaluable help as you monitor your network's performance.

Do you love chorizo but find yourself struggling to find the perfect one in the store? Why not try making it yourself at home? With a few tools and ingredients, you can create delicious homemade chorizo that you can enjoy anytime. Here's how to make your own chorizo at home:

1. Buy a sausage making/filling machine.

The first step in making chorizo is to get a sausage making/filling machine. You can buy one online or at a kitchenware store near you. This machine will help you fill the meat mixture into the sausage casings easily and quickly.

2. Clean and disinfect the area you plan to use to make sausage.

Before you start making chorizo, make sure you clean and disinfect the area where you plan to work. This will help you avoid any contamination that may affect the quality of your sausage.

3. Procure your pork.

To make chorizo, you will need to get half a kilo of pork shoulder and half a kilo of diced pork fat from your local butcher or grocery store. Make sure you get good quality meat that is fresh and free from any additives or preservatives.

4. Buy 7 oz.

You will also need to get seven ounces of sausage casings from your butcher or get them online. Choose casings that are fresh and pliable.

5. Mix 1.5 tbsp.

In a small bowl, mix together 1.5 tbsp of paprika, 1 tsp of salt, 1 tsp of ground black pepper, and 1 tsp of dried oregano. Stir the spices together with a fork or a whisk until they are well combined.

6. Crush 3 cloves of garlic.

Using a garlic press or a knife, crush three cloves of garlic and set them aside for later.

7. Place the minced pork shoulder and diced fat in a large bowl.

In a large mixing bowl, place the minced pork shoulder and diced fat and mix them together.

8. Sprinkle the minced meat with the spices and 3 crushed garlic cloves.

Sprinkle the spice mix and the crushed garlic cloves over the minced meat mixture. Use your hands or a spoon to mix the spices and garlic into the meat until they are evenly distributed.

9. Add 1/4 cup (59ml) of Spanish red wine.

Pour 1/4 cup (59ml) of Spanish red wine into the meat mixture and use your hands to knead the meat and spices together for several minutes until everything is well combined.

10. Put on some plastic food service gloves.

To avoid contamination and to protect your hands, put on some plastic food service gloves.

11. Knead the meat, spices and wine together for several minutes.

Continue to knead the meat mixture for several minutes until it becomes smooth and uniform.

12. Set up your sausage mincer machine.

Set up your sausage mincer machine according to its instructions. Make sure to get all the necessary attachments ready.

13. Place your first sausage skin below the nozzle on the sausage mincer.

Remove a sausage skin from the package and place it below the nozzle on the sausage mincer.

14. Load the meat mixture into the top of the mincer.

Load the meat mixture into the top of the mincer and start turning the crank or press down on the meat mixture until it feeds into the sausage casing.

15. Turn the crank or manually press the meat mixture downward until it feeds into the sausage casing.

Continue to turn the crank or manually press the meat mixture downward until it completely fills the sausage casing.

16. Twist the sausage skin several times every 12 inches (30cm) starting from the bottom up.

Once the sausage casing is filled, twist it several times every 12 inches (30cm), starting from the bottom up. This will create individual sausage links.

17. Continue to fill up the sausage skin and the mincer by hand.

Repeat steps 13 to 16 until you have used up all of your meat mixture and sausage casings.

18. Prick each sausage casing lightly with a toothpick to remove air pockets.

Prick each sausage casing lightly with a toothpick to remove any air pockets that may have formed during the filling process.

19. Tie a knot in the casing of the last sausage when you have used all of your mince.

Once you have filled all the sausage casings, tie a knot in the casing of the last sausage to seal it.

20. Identify a place in your house where it is cool, dry and sanitary.

Identify a place in your house where you can hang your chorizo for 1 to 3 months to cure. This should be a cool, dry and sanitary area.

21. Hang your chorizo from the ceiling for 1 to 3 months.

Using butcher's twine, tie each sausage link and hang them from the ceiling using a rod or a hanger. Let the chorizo hang for 1 to 3 months to cure and dry out.

22. Cook on a grill, in an oven or in a frying pan after it has cured.

Once your chorizo has cured for at least a month, it is ready to cook. You can cook it on a grill, in an oven or in a frying pan until it is browned and heated through.

23. Finished.

Enjoy your homemade chorizo with your favorite side dishes and a glass of red wine. Your friends and family will be impressed with your culinary skills and be begging for more!

These items will change over time, and they may depend on the season, too. Make sure to include a diaper or two, a small unopened package of baby wipes, some burp cloths, a pacifier or two (with an attached pacifier holder), baby bibs, diaper rash cream, diaper pins (if cloth-diapering), changing mats, a first aid kit, a bottle warmer (if needed), and 1-2 extra shirts, pants and socks.


The list of things may grow over time. As you shop for baby items, think about what you could place in the bin that won't go bad in six months. Remember, this drawer is for emergencies only.;
, Make it easy to reach, so that you can grab items from the drawer easily when you need them. Make sure the drawer isn't in the driver's way; most often, these drawers can be located underneath the passenger seat of the car or in back of the driver's side. Look for seatback pockets (if available); they can sometimes carry some of these necessary items. If nothing fits as to an emergency underseat drawer, you can pack a secondary "emergencies only" diaper bag or backpack with the necessary items.


Avoid keeping these supplies in the trunk. Items stored there will be too hard to reach in an emergency.

, The idea of this pack is that you won't take it in and out of the house like your regular diaper bag. All these emergency items should remain in the vehicle, so don't plan to include items that you need inside regularly.

, Is this summer or is this winter? Clothing can be a major consideration here for what will fit and what you will need. Will you need a pair of shorts, or will you need a pair of two of thermals? Is it so cold that you need to bring a blanket, or do you need to stick with light clothing because of the heat?

, Will you need daywear or is most of your travel doing to be done at night? Do you need any blankets, blanket sleepers, or onesies?

, Although a baby's emergency kit will probably include diapers, a toddler's might not, once they're potty trained. Change the contents as needed.

, Some of the items can be stored directly underneath the seat, but others are best kept in a bag or carrier. Try using a medium-sized backpack or a secondary diaper bag.

, Look for things that have no expiration date or won't expire for a long time. Formula and any food items, for example, expire at some point, so if you're going to keep them in your emergency kit, you'll need to restock these items once they go bad.

, This is just an emergency kit, not a lifetime's supply. Figure out how many of each item your kid might need on your average trip, and don't pack more than you need.


Change the set of diapers in the drawer to suit your baby's size. Children tend to outgrow diapers every few months (and some even sooner than that). Keep up with the times to ensure your child will still fit into the necessary clothes when they need them. Check the drawer regularly to ensure the items are still current and they will still fit in a pinch. Once items are used or outgrown, replace them immediately.

, A spit up rag made from a cloth diaper or from ripped fabric shirts or towels makes for a great cleanup option. Also, look to keep some baby wipes and baby bibs on hand, as well as one or two disposable absorbent mats (such as those made by Huggies Goodnites that are available at most grocery stores in the US).

, For example, you might want to include a small writing pad of paper and pen. If the child has a toy or second favorite teddy bear, it's okay to pack one of those into the bag.


You might also want an extra USB auto-adapter cord or an extra car charger in the drawer.

, Put them all in a pile. If you have a clean laundry basket, that can be a great temporary storage location as you assemble the kit.

, Place them on an orderly pile. You may end up placing the very thickest items aside to pack outside the drawer, but ideally you'll fit everything in your kit, with the bulkiest items on the bottom of your drawer.

, Your smallest items are likely to be the ones you'll use the most often, too (like diapers, pacifiers, baby bibs, changing mats, baby wipes, bottle warmer and first aid kit).

, Place it next to your piles of emergency items.

, Try to put the larger items in first and then the smallest things (such as the diapers) on the top of the bag.


Don't put away the pacifiers directly into the bag; you'll add them to your kit later.

, Stash the baby bottle warmer in the cup/bottle holder, if your bag has one. Put the first aid kit in an outside pocket. Baby bibs and changing mats can generally go into the side of the main part of the diaper bag.

, These keep the clothes and items seeming fresh.

,, Never just stuff the pacifiers into the bag itself, as loose items like this can get lost very easily.


Put a clean Ziploc bag over top of the pacifier to keep it fresh until it needs to be used. The Ziploc bag isn't going to completely close, but if left untouched it will close most of the way and keep the pacifier clean.

, Now you're ready for whatever your baby or toddler needs!

To beat drug addiction, you need to set a goal to quit. You may not be able to do it all at once, but setting the goal will help you chart your next steps., Writing down a specific list of the ways in which your addiction is negatively impacting your life can give you a jumpstart toward changing your behavior. Rather than framing the addictions effect in general terms ("It's destroying my life" or "I'm not reaching my potential"), write down the ways that your individual life has changed since your addiction began. Seeing it all written down on paper might be jarring, but having the list will help you through the tough steps to come., You know that you are addicted if you experience withdrawal symptoms when you try to stop using.Withdrawal symptoms are the opposite of how the drug makes you feel when you are under the influence. If you feel energized when you are high, then you feel extremely tired and groggy when you are in withdrawal. If you feel relaxed and happy when you are high, then you experience intense anxiety and agitation when you are in withdrawal. You may feel sick when you try to stop using, and you need to keep using to feel normal.


Keep a log of how you feel and how your addiction is affecting you physically. Depending on the drug you're using, it might be causing skin damage, organ damage, dental problems, and other physical issues. Even if the physical effects are subtle, like you've lost a lot of weight or your face is aging more quickly than it should, write them down.

, A drug addict may neglect responsibilities such as school attendance, work, family, and other duties like laundry, housework, car maintenance, paying bills, etc. When a person is addicted to a drug, their world revolves around using, recovering from the effects of using, and then getting more drugs.An addiction is not recreational or experimental use. It is a compulsion that requires intervention to bring it to an end.


Write down how often you’ve been at work or school lately. Reflect on how attentive you are to your responsibilities.
Think about whether the addiction has taken a financial toll. Write down how much money you spend to feed it every day, week, month, and year.

, Withdrawing from family members and friends because you are under the influence or experiencing withdrawal and you don’t feel like being around anyone. This behavior may baffle friends and family who wonder where you are or why you are acting strangely.


There may even be confrontations about the frequency of your drinking or drug use. These are all signs of an addiction. , Stealing and lying to others, especially those close to you like family and friends. It is not unusual for the addicted person to steal valuables or money to pay for more drugs. An addiction affects not only the body but it impairs thinking to the point that the addict can even consider stealing from others.


Lying goes hand in hand with the secretive nature of drug addiction as well as the shame the addict feels for their behavior.

, You may have given up hobbies and other interests because using drugs has been your primary focus. Imagine trying to give equal time to drug use as well as hobbies and personal interests (i.e., rock climbing, dance, stamp collecting, photography, playing an instrument, learning another language, and more).


Anyone who can still concentrate on their hobbies is not in the grips of an all-consuming chemical addiction.

, Continuing to use drugs even though it is creating problems with school, work, the legal system, family life and relationships, and health. For most people, being arrested would be so jarring that it would force you to reconsider the course of your life. But in a person who is dependent on drugs or alcohol, those consequences are forgotten or the memory fades as soon as the overpowering cravings to use return.


You may have been arrested for a DUI (driving under the influence) or for possession of a controlled substance.
Your relationships may be in trouble or they may have failed. If you have an addiction, friends and family may not want to be around you.

, Now that you've written down the negative stuff, focus on how much better your situation might be once you beat this. How will your life story change post-addiction? You'll minimize or eliminate many of those negatives, and you'll be able to make positive changes.

, Consult with a doctor who specializes in chemical addictions. This professional can give you guidance on treatment options for your particular drug addiction.


The doctor will then likely recommend that you check into a detox facility to begin the withdrawal process under medical supervision. This is especially important if you are withdrawing from alcohol, opiates or benzodiazepines. Withdrawal from these substances can have agonizing and sometimes life-threatening symptoms., , Barbiturates, methamphetamines, cocaine and crack, benzodiazepines, and alcohol withdrawal can all be life threatening, causing seizures, and in the case of cocaine and crack, respiratory failure, stroke, and convulsions.It’s important to detox under the care of a rehabilitation facility to help you deal with the physical effects of withdrawal.


Even if the substance does not have any life-threatening withdrawal symptoms, there are other side effects that can make withdrawal very uncomfortable, such as anxiousness and even hallucinations.Experiencing the withdrawal symptoms is part of what keeps you in the cycle of addiction. The best place to recover is in the hands of the professionals who can help you deal with all the effects of withdrawal from the drug.
If you’ve been arrested, your probation officer might allow you to attend treatment in lieu of jail time. Take advantage of this opportunity.

, As with many treatment programs that focus on chemical addiction, successful treatment includes individual and group counseling. Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) can help you identify the thought patterns that keep you stuck in the cycle of drug use.


A counselor might also use motivational interviewing to help you see where you are still ambivalent about making the commitment to change.To find a counselor that specializes in drug addiction counseling, get a recommendation from your doctor or rehabilitation facility.

, In order to beat drug addiction, you will need help in many different parts of your life. This is because drug addiction profoundly affects every aspect of your life. Be ready to seek help for your physical, mental, emotional, and spiritual wellbeing.You may also want to seriously consider working with a family therapist, a life coach, a job coach, a fitness coach, a financial advisor, or any other type of expert to help you navigate those areas you need help in that you want to turn into strengths.

, Evidence shows that addicts who have a strong network of support have much better success in recovery.12-Step Programs are the most popular types of self-help, peer support programs in the world.


Alcoholics Anonymous (AA) is a very well-known program. AA and other 12-step programs outline twelve specific steps “that are guidelines for nothing less than a total personality transformation.”Narcotics Anonymous (NA) is geared towards supporting individuals recovering from drug addiction.There are other peer-based groups that offer excellent support, such as SMART Recovery. This group is a 4-point program that addresses all types of addictions and compulsions.Don’t be afraid to try several options before you find what works best for you.
Search the Alcoholics Anonymous Narcotics Anonymous websites to find local support groups.
Recognize that your addiction is a disease. Addiction is a disease that alters brain structure and functioning.When you acknowledge that you’re suffering from a disease, you can address your addiction more readily.

, Many peer-based support groups assign sponsors to new members. The sponsor is a recovering addict who will help you through the steps of the recovery program., Support groups will help you realize that there are people who’ve been through similar experiences as you. They feel just as desperate and ashamed as you do. Giving and receiving support can be a useful way to heal and become responsible., In order to break old habits, you might have to plan out every hour of your day. This will help you develop new routines that do not include drugs. Establish routines that center around the goals you want to accomplish, such as finishing school, raising a family, or going to work. Eventually, you will develop healthy habits that not only distract you from using drugs, but also will help you achieve your life goals.

, This will help you see exactly what you will accomplish throughout the day. Create a simple daily planner. Keep track of daily things that you need to accomplish and then check them off.


If you get stuck, have a place for notes where you write down who can help you with this. Never give yourself a dead end.
If you don’t have any family or friends to help you with completing the things on your list, it’s perfectly acceptable to bring your list to your counseling session and work out your difficulties with your counselor or psychologist.

, Another part of breaking old habits is to practice uncompromising honesty with yourself about where you go and who you interact with. The pull to reconnect with those people and places that involved drug use will be strong. Good planning and brutal honesty is required to keep you on your path to success.


For example, don’t try to talk yourself into going to places where you used to hang out to test your own strength. Likewise, don’t think that it is okay to see a person you did drugs with all the time. These are just rationalizations, or ways for you to convince yourself to go back to using drugs again. Don’t fall victim to these rationalizations.

, Recognize that, beyond the physical cravings for the drug, you might have emotional connections and ties. You might yearn for the way things used to be. Know that it takes time to adjust, and you can and will adjust if you stick to your plan for recovery.

, Find people who will support you in your efforts to beat drug addiction. Caring family and friends likely want to help you get healthy.


You can also choose people who have been through a similar situation. They can help you stick to your goals.Choose people who do not drink or do drugs, so that you won’t have to put yourself in tempting situations.

, Getting regular exercise can be an excellent way of handling the stress of beating drug addiction.


It can be a good idea to join a gym or work with a personal trainer. This will help make you more accountable to improving your health.

, Find a nutrition program offered through your community. Some programs are offered through the county, and others through local hospitals. Getting your body back on track can also mean eating properly and taking care of your nutrition, which may have been damaged by your drug use.

, Yoga is a form of exercise and meditation that can benefit your body and mind. Carve out 15-30 minutes at least a few times a week to give yourself time to manage the stress of coping with urges to use alcohol or drugs.

, Meditation can be an excellent way to manage stress and focus on breathing and body awareness. Meditate to calm yourself as you confront urges to use alcohol or drugs.


Find a comfortable and quiet spot to sit for 10-15 minutes.
Focus on your breath, inhaling deeply and steadily.
As thoughts pass through your mind, release them without judgment. Turn your attention back to your breath.

, Acupuncture is an ancient Chinese healing method that places needles at certain pressure points on your body. This method will help you address long-term withdrawal symptoms and discomfort.


Check with your health insurance provider to determine if acupuncture treatment is covered by your policy.

, Continue counseling for as long as you need support. You may want to also bring your family to counseling sessions to work out problems.

, This plan will involve how to manage temptations and cravings when they occur, how to deal with boredom and discouragement, and learning how to meet responsibilities that have been neglected. Living without drugs is a lifestyle. It is part of every aspect of life (such as relationships, parenting, work, socializing, meeting obligations, interacting with others, etc.).


Think about how you will address each of these aspects of life in order to remove drugs from them.
Write down ideas of how you will handle situations, such as stressful conversations, social gatherings, and so on., Write down the goals that you’d like to accomplish. These can be small things, such as taking a shower every day or eating a proper meal every day. They may also be larger goals, such as getting a job or visiting the dentist.


Keep track of your progress on these goals every week. Even small accomplishments are worth noting. You will start to see improvement and progress, which will motivate you to keep going.

, If you start to feel like you’re going to start using again, try urge surfing. This is a mindfulness relapse prevention technique. When you suppress urges, you tend to make the urges worse. By recognizing and accepting urges, you will be able to ride them out, or “surf” them.Recognize the urges that you feel about your addiction. Be conscious about the feelings and thoughts that you experience.
Rate your urge from 1 to 10 (1 being hardly any urge to 10 being a pressing urge). Wait for 10 minutes. Busy yourself with an activity, such as cleaning the junk out of your car, or writing a list, or putting away laundry. Check with your urge again to gauge its level. If you still experience a high level of urge, continue busying yourself with another activity. , Don’t visit the places where you used to obtain or use drugs. Don’t associate with the people who were your drinking buddies.


The flip side here is to frequent places that you don’t associate with drugs or drink. You may develop new hobbies, such as rock climbing, knitting, hiking or gardening.

, Keep yourself busy by getting a job, even if it’s part time. This will also start building up your self-worth as you bring home a paycheck.


Deposit your paychecks in the bank and save the money.
You might also think about volunteering if you don’t want to get a job. Having commitments to other people will help you stay on track.

, Once the worst has passed, and your body and mind are no longer consumed by withdrawal, spend your time building the life you want to live. Nourish your relationships with the people you love, work hard at your job, and throw yourself into hobbies and past times that are meaningful to you.


During this time, you should continue going to meetings with your support group and continue meeting with your therapist. The process of beating a drug addiction is not a quick one, so don't declare yourself cured when things start to go well.

Most likely, your old water pump has failed and most of the coolant has already leaked out, so there might not be much coolant in the pump. Be sure to dispose of the old coolant properly, as it is poisonous and could be lethal to unsuspecting animals.
, On the manifold there is a heat cover along with an oxygen sensor. Remove both the heat cover and the oxygen sensor. There are 10 bolts connecting the exhaust manifold to the engine. 9 of the bolts you can reach from the top of the car that connects the manifold to the block. Remove these 9 bolts. We will get to the tenth in a couple more steps., You will need to jack up the car to do this. Once underneath the car, you will see two bolts connecting the pipe to the manifold. These bolts have springs on them (I think it's to ensure consistent tension). You will need to loosen one a couple of turns and then loosen the other a couple of turns, switching back and forth between the bolts until you get them out. By now, the pipe should be free from the manifold and you should be able to pull it back and down to disengage it from the manifold. There will be a little ring spacer between the manifold, and the pipe should fall free when you do this so watch your head., You will be able to see it from under the car: It will be directly under the spot where the pipe and manifold join., You're now to the easy part., The cover will have two bolts going into the block of the engine, two going down to the where the thermostat is mounted, and 5 going to where the water pump is mounted. These are all 10mm bolts. Just loosen them, and the housing should come free; the housing also has a hose coming out of the front of it that runs underneath the exhaust manifold to the other side of the engine. Disconnect the hose and take the whole housing out., It will have 4 bolts connecting it to the engine. Take these out and it should slide right out of there., For this you need to support the engine either from above with an engine lift or from below with a jack and block of wood. Once the engine is supported, it is time to take off the engine mounts., This is located on the left hand side of the engine. It is supported by locking slots and one bolt. Take the bolt out and slide the tank out of the slots. Disconnect the back two hoses on the tank and move it out of the way., WITH THE ENGINE SUPPORTED, start taking off the bolts. Once you have them off, save them and go to the store to get new ones. You have to get new bolts for engine mounts once they are removed because they stretch and lose their strength.,, carefully lower the car with the engine supported to raise the engine., (You might want to drain the oil out of the engine before you do this since it will probably run out.) Once the cover is off you should be able to see the tensioners. Remove these to release the chain from the gear of the water pump. Once this is done, it should slide right out., Take your time and make sure you get them clean., Also, while you have it all apart, consider replacing the thermostat., Apply bolt grease on all the bolts before reinserting them, so in case the water pump breaks again, you won't have to struggle w/ seized bolts., If everything checks out okay, then put the rest of it back together. If it sounds like too much work, feel free to visit your mechanic.

How to Ask Out a Scene Girl in Middle School

If you have a crush on a scene girl in your middle school, you might be nervous about asking her out. Maybe you're not sure if she's interested in you, or maybe you're not sure how to approach her. But with a few helpful tips, you can build a connection with her and hopefully start a relationship!

1. Actually know what "scene" is.

First things first: Do you know what "scene" means? Scene is a style trend that originated in the early 2000s, characterized by bright dyed hair, heavy makeup, and punk rock-inspired clothing. If your crush fits this description, she might identify as part of the scene subculture.

2. Decide if you want to still be with her.

It's important to consider why you're interested in this girl and whether you have a genuine connection beyond her outward appearance. Are you drawn to her personality, interests, and values? Do you enjoy talking to her and spending time with her? If so, great! If not, it might be better to move on.

3. If she is in your class, and sits next to you, say things like, "I don't get what the homework is about," or if you didn't do your home work (and if you know she won't mind about this) say "Did you do your homework?"

Starting a conversation is the first step to getting to know your crush better. If you sit next to her in class, find ways to make small talk. Ask her about the homework or classwork, or mention something you noticed about her outfit or accessories.

4. If she is in your class, but not sitting next to you, walk up to her (at an appropriate time) and ask for a pencil to borrow, or an eraser.

If you don't sit next to her in class, find a reason to approach her during a break or before/after class. Ask to borrow a writing utensil or a classroom supply, and strike up a conversation.

5. If she is not in any of your classes, then maybe find a friend of hers, and become friends with her.

If you don't have any classes or mutual activities with her, try to connect with a friend of hers. By becoming friends with someone in her social circle, you might have more opportunities to get to know her.

6. After you two have talked a little bit, begin to talk about normal things.

Once you've started a conversation, don't just talk about scene culture or your crush's appearance. Ask her about her interests outside of school or music preferences. By learning more about her, you might find common ground and shared passions.

7. If she sits away from you, then go up to her, ask for the eraser (or pencil) again, and then be like "Oh wait, I know this is random, but what's your favorite band?"

If you've already asked for a classroom supply from her before, don't repeat the same conversation. Instead, find a new reason to talk to her and pose a question that shows your interest in her. By asking about her favorite band or a related topic, you can show that you care about her passions and opinions.

8. By now, you guys should be at least friends, not best friends, but friends.

After several interactions, you might have developed a friendship with your crush. Don't rush or force the relationship - let it build naturally over time. As you continue to talk to her, show genuine interest and care for her as a person.

9. After the "date," you guys should be good friends, then you can start going at more often.

If you've hung out with her outside of school and had a good time, keep building the friendship. Over time, you might feel ready to ask her out on a "real" date - just make sure you're respecting her boundaries and feelings. If she's not interested, don't push the issue.

10. Once you guys seem to be best friends, ask her out!

If you've spent enough time getting to know your crush and it feels like the right moment, ask her out on a date! Be confident, sincere, and respectful - and remember that even if she says no, you can still maintain a friendship. With patience, kindness, and understanding, you can pursue a healthy and happy relationship with a scene girl in middle school.

Eliminate noises from telephones, computers, cell phones and other electronic devices during the period setting your kids up to go to sleep by telling people not to call after a certain time or silencing/unplugging your phone. Avoid using using electronic devices unless you are behind closed doors or far enough away that the child can't hear you.;
, Besides making them feel more comfortable at night, this can help signal to the child that bedtime is coming up. If you start by putting on their preferred pajamas, the child will learn to recognize that this part of the routine means it's time to relax and prepare for bed.


Plan ahead. If you have several children that need dressing assistance, you'll need to allot yourself enough time to get everyone ready for bed.
If the child still needs to wear a diaper, change it now, rather than having to disrupt the routine later.

, Make the room as mildly temperate as can be, whether that involves cooling it down or heating it up. If needed, you can add or subtract additional layers of clothes on the child, but adjust other factors in the room such as heaters and fans.

, Read books or sing them some calming songs; this will help to set the mood into "nighttime mode".

, Although both styles have their advantages, there's also an age factor that plays a part. The Sleep Separation Technique is meant for children who are younger than 3 years old, and the Stay in Bed Technique is usually aimed at older children.

Aim your methods towards getting the youngest children off first, before getting the others to sleep. Yes, at first the Stay In Bed technique can take a little bit of time to master, but with practice the child will learn and you can then move on to the older children with help from your significant other - or here you can split up the duties if you have someone else around at bedtime.

, Give the child the encouragement to fall asleep in their room. Give them kisses and hugs (cuddles, as Supernanny calls them).

, Most often times, this technique is used when the child sleeps in a crib and hasn't yet moved to their own bed.

, Try to remain quiet and move minimally, to avoid disrupting your child's rest.

, If you need to, you may end up sitting on the floor with your legs crossed Indian-style. Make sure that the child can see the side of your face, but refuse to give any direct eye contact to the child. It may be hard but this is essential to the method.


If you need to, use small strips of reflective tape to mark the place where you are sitting so you can return. (You may even need to do this prior to turning out the lights.)
Increase the distance between you and the child each night you use this technique. Make sure that you creep closer and closer to the door, as long as the child still feels secure in their crib.

, Make no communication with the child. This may be hard when they call for you, but remain consistent and firm in your approach.

, Avoid eye contact. Look away from the child, as you pick them up and place them back in their crib.

, Act as if you never left the spot; sit so that the child can still see the side of your face from the same spot.

, It'll be upsetting to hear them cry, but recognize that the child will be okay and isn't in pain; they just need to learn the new routine.

,, Each night following, move closer and closer to the door. Once you are outside the door, spend that night sitting there. After being outside the door, you can actively go about a normal routine., Talk to them and tell them that it's nighttime now when you place them into bed, and that you'd like them to calm down and relax.

, Whether you head back to your bedroom or stand just out of sight distance, they should not be able to see you. Wait for them to stir and get out of bed.

, Remind the child that it is nighttime in soft, mellow words as you walk them back into their room, before placing them back down into their bed. You might say, "It's nighttime/bedtime darling," for example.


Try not to feed into their activity. By this time, if they can walk, have them walk into bed from wherever they are. If they can't quite walk (or refuse to), carry them back into bed either in the shoulder hold or cradle hold (letting their legs dangle free).

,,, Make this interaction very brief, including a brief kiss and cuddle if needed. Use the Supernanny-approved "Bedtime, darling" response.

,, This is an easy step to forget, but it's important after a third attempt not to say anything.

, They will begin to realize that bedtime means "sleep". Don't kiss or cuddle them on these subsequent trips back to bed. Follow through with whether or not you'll be able to carry them.

Are you tired of dealing with greasy, oily straight hair? It can be frustrating to constantly feel like your hair needs washing, but there are steps you can take to manage it. Start by focusing on your diet: eating protein-rich foods, cutting out unhealthy options, and consuming fresh dark green leafy vegetables and fruits can all help regulate oil production. Drinking plenty of water and coconut water can also be beneficial.

Additionally, reducing stress, exercising regularly, and getting enough sleep can all impact your hair's oil levels. When it comes to washing, try to limit it to once or twice a week and use gentle shampoos and light conditioners. Opt for a wide-toothed comb and air-dry your hair whenever possible.

For a natural hair treatment, you can create a gooey paste from soaking certain ingredients overnight and applying it to your hair for several hours. One option is to mix fenugreek seeds with honey, while another involves a paste made from raw nuts and an iron utensil. These can be effective for nourishing your hair and reducing oiliness.

When it comes to styling, find a hairstyle that suits your face shape and keep your hair trimmed regularly. If you color your hair, visit a professional and don't skimp on post-color care. You can also use a blow dryer, teasing techniques, and dry shampoo to manage oil between washes.

Most importantly, be proud of your hair and of yourself. Don't let oily hair bring you down; with a little care and attention, you can manage it and feel confident and beautiful.

How to Get Music off of YouTube to Make a Mix CD

If you're a fan of creating mix CDs, you may want to include some of your favorite songs from YouTube. While YouTube does not allow downloads of music, there are a few ways to download songs from YouTube and transfer them to a CD. In this article, we'll show you how to do just that.

Method 1: Using youtube2mp3.cc

1. Open the YouTube website on a computer.

2. Open a song or music video you want to download.

3. Highlight the video's address in the address bar.

4. Copy the highlighted address.

5. Visit youtube2mp3.cc in your browser.

6. Paste the copied address into the text field.

7. Click Convert.

8. Wait while the video converts.

9. Click the Download button.

10. Wait while the file downloads.

11. Repeat for additional songs.

12. Open iTunes.

13. Insert a blank CD.

14. Click the Libraries menu in the upper-left.

15. Click Music.

16. Open the folder containing your downloaded music files.

17. Select all of the music files you want to burn to disc.

18. Drag the selected files into the iTunes window.

19. Click File → New → Playlist.

20. Type a name that you'll remember.

21. Click Edit Playlist.

22. Drag your newly-added music to the playlist sidebar.

23. Click Done in the playlist sidebar.

24. Click your new playlist in the left sidebar.

25. Click File → Burn Playlist to Disc.

26. Click Audio CD in the Format section.

27. Ensure Sound Check is enabled.

28. Click the Burn button.

29. Wait while your CD burns.

Method 2: Using Windows Media Player

1. Click the Start button.

2. Type windows media player.

3. Click Windows Media Player.

4. Insert a blank CD.

5. Click the Burn tab in Windows Media Player.

6. Open the folder containing your downloaded music.

7. Hold Ctrl and click each music file.

8. Drag the selected files into the Burn tab.

9. Click the Burn list heading.

10. Type the name for your disc and press ↵ Enter.

11. Click Start burn.

12. Wait while the disc is burned.

That's it! You now have your favorite songs downloaded from YouTube and transferred to a CD. Enjoy your mix CD in your car, on your stereo, or anywhere you listen to music.

Tsoureki, the sweet braided bread traditionally enjoyed in Greece during Easter, is a delicious treat that can also be enjoyed year-round. While the recipe may seem intimidating at first, with a little patience and attention to detail, you can easily bake a beautiful and flavorful loaf of Tsoureki. In this article, we'll walk you through the steps to make your own Tsoureki from scratch, including a unique step involving spice water and even a fun tradition involving a coin!

Ingredients:
- 1 stick of cinnamon
- 5 bay leaves
- 1 cup of water
- 1 package of yeast
- 1 teaspoon of sugar
- 1 can of milk
- 1 can's worth of water
- 1/2 cup of butter
- 7 eggs
- 2 cups of sugar
- 1 teaspoon of masticha
- 1 teaspoon of machlepi
- 8 cups of flour
- 1 coin wrapped in aluminum foil
- 1 tablespoon of sesame seeds

Instructions:

1. Make the spice water by boiling 1 stick of cinnamon and 5 bay leaves into a cup of water for 15 minutes.

2. Let the spice water cool for 10 minutes and add the yeast and 1 teaspoon of sugar to it. Stir gently and let sit for 10 minutes.

3. In a saucepan, heat up the can of milk on low flame. Once the milk is warm, add in one can's worth of water followed soon after by the butter. Stir until the butter melts and turn off the heat.

4. Beat 7 eggs and 2 cups of sugar in a separate, large bowl.

5. Add the milk mixture and the yeast mixture to the egg/sugar bowl. Stir to combine.

6. Grind masticha and machlepi in a mortar and pestle with a teaspoon of sugar until it forms a fine, sticky paste.

7. Mix the ground masticha and machlepi into the growing mixture.

8. Slowly add flour to the mixture, kneading with your hands until the dough is soft and slightly sticky.

9. Wrap the coin in aluminum foil and knead it into the dough.

10. Place the dough into a pie tin or on a baking sheet, cover it with a towel and let it rise until it doubles in size (usually 2 to 3 hours).

11. Preheat your oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.

12. Mix the remaining two eggs with sesame seeds and then lightly brush the mixture onto the top of the dough once it has risen.

13. Bake the Tsoureki at 350 degrees for 5 minutes.

14. Lower the oven temperature to 250 degrees Fahrenheit and let the Tsoureki bake for 45 minutes or until it turns golden brown.

15. Once it's finished baking, place the cooled Tsoureki onto a large plate.

16. Gather everyone around the table and have the primary caregiver in the house place $20-40 dollars onto the center of the table near the Tsoureki.

17. Carve a cross shape into the top of the Tsoureki (the ritual begins with a remembrance of Christ).

18. Spin the Tsoureki three times (also a religious reference, this time to the Trinity).

19. Cut the Tsoureki into equal sections, in this order: Christou (For the Church), Spitiou (For the House), Thoulia (For the Work), Nikoukidi (For the 'Man' of the House), Nikoukida (For the 'Woman' of the House), Bethiou (One slice for each of the children starting with the oldest).

20. Search each slice for the coin.

21. Whoever finds the coin in their piece of Tsoureki gets to keep it and spend the prize money on something frivolous that makes them happy! 

22. The only situation in which the money is used seriously is if the Church wins.

23. Keep the coin in a safe place for the next year and DO NOT SPEND IT!

Enjoy your homemade Tsoureki and the fun traditions that come along with it. You can also share this delicious bread with your family and friends as a special treat during holidays or any time of the year.

Learning a new language can seem intimidating at first, but with perseverance and dedication, you can easily learn Botswana's official language, Setswana. Observing pronunciation is key, so it's important to learn how Batswana naturally pronounce and speak words. Here's a breakdown of Setswana pronunciation:

- 'a' - pronounced like 'a' in father (as in aga)
- 'b' - pronounced like 'b' in bat (as in bana)
- 'd' - pronounced like 'd' in do (as in dira)
- 'e' - pronounced like 'e' in exit (as in ema)
- 'f' - pronounced like 'f' in farm (as in fofa)
- 'g' - pronounced like 'ch' in loch (as in goga)
- 'h' - pronounced like 'h' in ham (as in huduga)
- 'i' - pronounced like 'ea' in eat (as in bina)
- 'j' - pronounced like 'j' in job (as in ja)
- 'k' - pronounced like 'c' in clerical (as in kopa)
- 'l' - pronounced like 'l' in list (as in lema)
- 'm' - pronounced like 'm' in mum (as in mae)
- 'n' - pronounced like 'n' in now (as in nna)
- 'ng' - pronounced like 'ng' in linger (as in ngaka)
- 'o' - pronounced like 'o' in order (as in bona)
- 'p' - pronounced like 'p' in pull (as in pitsa)
- 'r' - pronounced like 'r' in rope (as in reka)
- 's' - pronounced like 's' in say (as in sala)
- 'sh' - pronounced like 'tion' in station (as in seteišene)
- 'sw' - pronounced like 'sw' in swift (as in mašwi)
- 't' - pronounced like 't' in pot (as in tau)
- 'tsh' - pronounced like 'ch' in church (as in ntšha)
- 'u' - pronounced like 'oo' in book (as in bula)
- 'w' - pronounced like 'w' in well (as in wena)
- 'y' - pronounced like 'y' in yearn (as in yola)

Now that you have an idea of how to pronounce words in Setswana, it's time to dive into learning the language. Start with commonly used greetings like "Dumela" (hello) and "Leina la gago ke mang?" (what's your name?). Make sure to practice these phrases as much as possible until you feel comfortable using them in everyday conversation.

Next, learn common expressions like "Ke a leboga" (thank you) and "E ka bophara" (excuse me). These phrases can come in handy when out and about in Botswana.

Finally, it's important to know how to ask questions and form sentences. A useful question to know is "O tla tsenya (dilo / kredikadi / chekeso ya motho yo o tsholang nako ya botlhe)?" which means "Do you accept (money/credit cards/traveler's cheques)?"

Learning a new language may take time, but with patience and dedication, you'll soon be speaking Setswana with ease.

Bay Area Rapid Transit (BART) is a public transportation system that serves the San Francisco Bay Area. As a visitor or newcomer to the area, it might take some getting used to before you become comfortable with using it. This article will guide you on how to ride BART.

Review the route map and find the stations nearest where you wish to go.

The first step to getting around on BART is to study the system map. This map is available online on the BART website and at all BART stations. It shows all of the lines and stations in the BART system. Review it and locate the stations nearest to where you wish to go.

Find the stations nearest your starting point and your destination, and determine the trains to use to get from the former to the latter.

Once you have identified the stations nearest to your starting point and your destination, you can use the system map to determine the trains to use to travel from one station to another. The trains are color-coded, and their destinations are displayed on electronic signs at the station.

Find out the fare required for the trip you will take.

BART uses a fare system based on distance traveled. Fares are posted at each station, and you can also estimate your fare using the BART website's fare calculator.

Alternatively, use route-planning tools such as Google Maps or competing services, on the web or an appropriate app on your phone.

Many of the popular route-planning tools like Google Maps can provide options on how to get from one point to another using BART. They could tell you which train to take, when to transfer, etc.

Go to the BART station where you start your trip.

Once you have identified the station you would like to start your trip, go to the station nearest you.

Buy a ticket.

Buying a ticket from the ticket vending machines located at all BART stations. Fare gates require your ticket or Clipper Card to access the train platform.

Check for announcements about system-wide delays before entering the station.

Occasionally, BART could run delayed due to weather issues, protests, strikes, and other activities that affect train operations. Before you enter the station, check the BART website for system-wide scheduled work and current information about service interruptions.

Place your ticket into the turnstile in the direction shown to enter the station.

The turnstiles are located near the fare gates and will let you enter the train platform area once your ticket has been accepted.

Board the correct train and ride BART to your destination.

Once inside the train platform area, check the electronic signs to see which train is going your way. Board the correct train.

Practice good train etiquette.

Avoid loud conversations, turn off your cellphone ringer, and refrain from eating, smoking or drinking on board. Keep the space neat and clear your trash.

Exit the train.

At your destination, exit the station using the fare gate, and keep in mind that you will be unable to re-enter the station using the same fare gate once you leave.

Insert the ticket into the turnstile (or touch your card) on your way out.

When exiting, make sure you insert your ticket into the turnstile in the direction shown on the electronic sign or touch your Clipper Card to pay the fare.

BART trains are more likely to be a little late than a little early.

If you're traveling during peak hours, be on the lookout for crowded train rides, and plan accordingly based on BART's Quick Planner. Trains are usually late by a few minutes, so anticipate that.

If you are boarding the station at a time when trains are crowded, and wish to use the front or rear car to maximize the chances of seating space, budget an extra minute to walk to the end of the platform.

The train cars are connected, so if you walk towards the end of the platform, you'll have a better shot of getting a seat.

Check BART's Quick Planner to get their estimates of the crowdedness of trains.

BART's Quick Planner can offer you information on the ideal times when trains are less occupied, an essential item to know, especially if you are traveling during rush hour.

If you are really interested in getting seating space and don't mind your commute being longer (for instance, if you'd like to sit and work on your laptop), and your direction of travel is crowded, consider riding in the opposite direction and then boarding a less crowded train in the direction you're going.

This tip is useful if you're not in a hurry and want to have more seating space for a more comfortable ride.

A scheduled transfer is a transfer between trains where both trains arrive at about the same time at a station, but the train being transferred to will not wait for the other train if it is delayed.

To avoid misinformation or confusion, follow the schedule of the trains you're taking. A transfer is confirmed only when trains come in at the estimated time, otherwise best wait for the next train.

Occasionally, the BART system encounters delays due to reasons ranging from medical emergencies on trains, equipment issues on trains and tracks, and police activity at stations.

If you find yourself waiting for more than ten minutes for the next train, listen for announcements or check the BART website for updates about the situation.

Are you interested in understanding Messianic prophecies? As a follower of Judaism or Christianity, it's important to have an understanding of the promised Messiah and what to expect from His arrival. In this article, we will discuss how to understand Messianic prophecies.

Wait for the Coming of Messiah

According to the Jewish faith, the Messiah is a leader and savior who will restore the former glory of the Kingdom of Israel. So, it's important to wait for the coming of the Messiah. The Jewish people have been waiting for the Messiah for centuries, and they continue to wait for His arrival.

Know When to Expect Him

In the biblical book of Daniel, the prophet Daniel prophesied the exact year of the Messiah's arrival. Although there is some disagreement as to when this prophecy was fulfilled, Daniel's prophecy is a good starting point for understanding the timeline of the Messiah's expected arrival.

Know What to Expect

The Jewish people have been taught that the Messiah will restore the kingdom at Jerusalem. They are expecting the Messiah to be a powerful and victorious leader who will restore the former glory of the Kingdom of Israel. Understanding what the Jewish people expect from the Messiah is an important step in understanding Messianic prophecies.

Decide Whether the Messiah Would Be Like Gideon

In the past, some Jewish people have believed that the Messiah would be like Gideon, taking a small force with the backing of God and His angels to confound and defeat the Roman oppressors and make Israel the rightful hub of the world. If this is what you believe, it's important to consider all of the relevant prophetic passages from the Bible to confirm your beliefs.

Know Who to Expect

Isaiah, another ancient prophet, goes into great detail about the coming of the Messiah. The Bible provides many proofs and scriptures for you to investigate, which will help you gain a better understanding of the Messiah and His mission.

Another Biblical Prophecy Concerning the Suffering of the Messiah

According to Wisdom chapter 2 in the Bible, there is another prophecy concerning the suffering of the Messiah. To get a complete understanding of Messianic prophecies, it's important to investigate and understand all pertinent passages.

Accept Jesus Christ as the Biblical Messiah

Today, there are many who have accepted Jesus as the Messiah who has already come in the flesh and won the victory over sin. These people call themselves Christians and Messianic Jews, and there are also Arabic Christians who profess Yasu (Yeshua) as the Messiah. Accepting Jesus as the Messiah is a critical step in understanding Messianic prophecies.

In conclusion, understanding Messianic prophecies can be a complex task. It's important to wait for the coming of the Messiah, know when to expect Him, know what to expect, decide whether the Messiah would be like Gideon, know who to expect, know about the Biblical prophecy concerning the suffering of the Messiah, and accept Jesus Christ as the Biblical Messiah. By doing so, you can gain a deeper understanding of Messianic prophecies and God's plan for humanity.

How to Report a Stolen Social Security Card

A stolen Social Security card can be a serious issue and can lead to identity theft. It's important to act quickly and take the necessary steps to protect yourself. Here's how to report a stolen Social Security card:

1. Contact the Social Security Administration (SSA).

The first step is to contact the SSA and report your stolen Social Security card. You can do this by calling 1-800-772-1213 or visiting your local SSA office. They will guide you through the steps you need to take to protect your Social Security account.

2. File a report with the Federal Trade Commission (FTC).

The next step is to file a report with the FTC. The FTC collects information that can lead to an investigation or prosecution of identity thieves, but they do not resolve individual cases relating to a stolen Social Security card. You can complete the FTC’s online complaint form to file your report.

3. File a police report with your local police.

It's important to file a police report with your local police department as soon as possible. This helps to establish a record of the theft and can assist in any future investigations. Make sure to obtain a copy of the police report for your own records.

4. File a complaint with the Internet Crime Complaint Center (IC3).

The IC3 is a partnership between the FBI and the National White Collar Crime Center. They investigate international organized crime and can assist in identity theft cases. You can file a complaint with the IC3 online.

5. Get an application.

To apply for a replacement Social Security card, you will need to get an application. You can find the application on the SSA's website or request one in person at your local SSA office.

6. Fill out the form.

Make sure to fill out the application form completely and accurately. You will need to provide personal information and explain the circumstances of the theft.

7. Collect necessary identity documents.

You will need to provide identification documents with your application. These can include a driver's license, passport, or birth certificate.

8. Collect the necessary citizenship or immigration documents.

If you are not a U.S. citizen, you will need to provide additional documentation to prove your lawful presence in the United States.

9. Apply for a replacement card.

Submit your completed application and all necessary documents to the SSA. You can do this in person at your local SSA office or by mail. You should receive your replacement Social Security card by mail within a few weeks.

10. Request a fraud alert with Equifax, Trans Union, or Experian.

Contact one of the three main credit reporting agencies and request a fraud alert be placed on your credit report. This will help to prevent anyone from opening new accounts in your name.

11. Consider requesting Extended Fraud Alerts.

If you believe you are at a high risk of identity theft, you may want to consider requesting Extended Fraud Alerts. These alerts last for seven years and require any potential creditors to contact you before opening any new accounts.

12. Request a free copy of your credit report from each of the credit reporting agencies.

You are entitled to a free copy of your credit report from each of the three credit reporting agencies. Request your reports and review them carefully for any fraudulent or inaccurate information.

13. Have any fraudulent or inaccurate information removed from the credit report.

If you find any fraudulent or inaccurate information on your credit report, take steps to have it removed. This may involve filing a dispute with the credit reporting agency or contacting the creditor directly.

14. Continue to check your credit reports at least every 3 months.

It's important to continue monitoring your credit reports for any suspicious activity. Set reminders to check your reports at least every 3 months to ensure you catch any potential issues early on.
