How to Become a Fan of Nickelodeon's Double Dare TV Game Show

If you're looking to become a fan of one of the most popular TV game shows from Nickelodeon, Double Dare is the perfect show to indulge your interests. With its exciting challenges and energetic hosts, Double Dare kept viewers glued to their screens for years. In this article, we'll explore the key components that make up Double Dare and help you understand how you too can become a fan of this iconic show.

1. Understand the general make-up of what happened during the introductory scene (toss-up game).

The toss-up game is where the two teams would face off in a head-to-head competition. The first team to ring the buzzer would be asked a question, and if they answered correctly, they would get to advance to the next round. This round was primarily used as an icebreaker and to establish the teams' order for the rest of the game.

2. Understand the makeup of the game.

Double Dare comprised of several rounds, which included: the toss-up game, question rounds, physical challenges, and the obstacle course. Each of these rounds brought its own unique challenges and excitement.

3. Recognize what happened in the Introductions.

The host would introduce the contestants, teams and the prizes that were up for grabs in each obstacle course. This introduction was accompanied by the main game music from the introductory credits.

4. Recognize what happened in the Question rounds: Generally, a question was asked to the team (it could be on any subject that the kids could know about or could have studied).

In this round, one team would be asked a question, if they didn't know the answer they would Dare the other team to answer the question for double the points. If the opposing team couldn't answer, they could Double Dare the first team to answer for quadruple the points.

5. Understand what happened during a Physical challenge.

In this round, one of the teams would move to the physical challenge area. The challenge could be anything, from wrapping your teammate in toilet paper to finding hidden objects in an oversized bowl of cereal.

6. Be able to understand the Obstacle Course round.

The Obstacle Course was the final round of the game, and it was where the teams could win the grand prize by completing as many obstacles as possible within a set amount of time. The obstacles ranged from an oversized sundae slide to running through a human car wash.

7. Recognize what the player would win for winning the game.

The grand prize was usually a trip, a new car, or a chance to be a VIP on another Nickelodeon game show.

8. Recognize what the players wore during the show.

The teams would wear matching jerseys or jackets with their team name on them.

9. Watch as many shows of each type as you can.

To become a fan of Double Dare, watching past episodes can be a great way to learn about the game's structure and grow to love the show's characters.

10. Understand the parts of the stages where the events were taking place.

Knowing the different parts of the stage and the different games played in each section can help you keep up with the action and understand what's happening.

11. Recognize the hosts for this show.

Double Dare had several hosts over the years, including Marc Summers, Jason Harris, and Jeff Sutphen. Each host brought their own style and personality to the show.

12. Recognize the TV network that brought the viewer these games.

Double Dare was aired on Nickelodeon.

13. Recognize the stage hands that helped clean the stage.

In between rounds, stage hands would come out and clean the stage of any leftover slime or other debris from previous challenges.

14. Recognize the narrator of the show.

Each episode of Double Dare was narrated by Johnny Gilbert, who would describe each physical challenge as it happened.

15. Recognize where else this name of the show was aired.

Double Dare was so successful that it was later adapted and aired in other countries, including Canada and the UK.

16. Get used to the main game music from the introductory credits as well as the theme music from when each obstacle course's prize was introduced after the host would introduce the obstacle).

The music played an essential role in the show's atmosphere, from the initial game music to the obstacle course theme music. Listening to these pieces can help to transport you back to the game show's glory days.

By understanding the key components of Double Dare, you too can become a fan of this iconic TV game show. From the toss-up game to the obstacle course, there's never a dull moment with Double Dare.

Growing a garden is a fun, rewarding activity that can provide you with fresh produce, beautiful flowers, and an opportunity to connect with nature. But with so many plants to choose from, how do you know which ones are right for you and your garden? In this article, we'll give you some tips on how to choose plants for a garden that will thrive in your environment and suit your interests.

1. Choose the type of gardening you are interested in.

Before you start selecting plants, you should consider what type of gardening you want to do. Do you want a vegetable garden, a flower garden, or both? Will your garden be in a raised bed, containers, or directly in the ground? Do you want to grow annuals that bloom for one season or perennials that come back year after year? Answering these questions will help you narrow down your choices.

2. Talk to people in your neighborhood who garden successfully.

Speaking to experienced gardeners can give you some great ideas for what plants grow well in your particular area. You can also learn about any challenges they faced and how they overcame them.

3. Check the phone book under government services.

If you're in the US, your local government may have a gardening hotline or website that can provide useful information on what plants grow well in your area. Some areas even offer free soil testing to help you determine what nutrients your soil needs.

4. Go for what you like.

Ultimately, you should choose plants that you find aesthetically pleasing or have a practical use for. It's your garden, and you want to feel happy and satisfied with what you've grown.

5. Check for different sizes.

Consider the mature size of the plants you're interested in - both their height and width. Make sure you have enough space in your garden to accommodate them and that they won't obstruct walkways or other plants.

6. Give some thought for the location of your garden.

Think about the amount of sunlight your garden will receive each day. Most vegetables and flowers need at least six hours of direct sunlight daily to thrive. You should also consider the type of soil you have and any potential threats like the wind, frost, or pests.

7. Go to the outdoor or garden section of the local big box store or home improvement center, they usually have a pretty good selection appropriate for your area and the season you find yourself in.

When you're ready to start shopping, visit your local garden center or nursery. They'll have a wide variety of plants to choose from, and their staff can help you identify which ones will do well in your garden.

8. Browse around, looking at the labels of plants you may be interested in trying.

Read the labels on the plants you're interested in to learn about their care requirements, like watering needs, ideal soil pH, and how much sunlight they require.

9. Pick a few "container started" plants that you want to try, looking for healthy plants with a good plant size to container proportion.

If you're new to gardening or don't want to start from seed, buying plants in containers can be a good option. Look for plants with healthy foliage and no signs of disease or pests.

10. Look for seeds, too, if the idea of planting "from scratch" interests you.

Growing plants from seed can be a fun and rewarding experience. Look for seed packets that are suited to your growing conditions and follow the instructions carefully.

11. Plan to start on a small scale, don't fill up a shopping cart the first trip down to the store.

It's important to start small, especially if you're new to gardening. Planting too much too soon can be overwhelming and lead to unnecessary stress.

12. Browse the fertilizer area of the store, and check out the soil amendments available.

Your plants will need nutrients to grow, so visit the fertilizer section to find products that will help keep them healthy.

13. Pick the spot for your garden, keeping it out of the way of frequently used areas of the yard.

Find a spot in your yard that's convenient to tend to and won't get trampled by foot traffic or pets.

14. Using a shovel or garden spade, turn the soil by digging up shovel fulls and breaking the clumps, then dumping it back in the hole.

Before planting, you'll need to prepare the soil by turning it over and breaking up any large clumps.

15. Remove any vegetative matter like roots, branches, leaves, clumps of grass and such that the soil turning has dislodged.

Clear any debris that may interfere with the healthy growth of your plants.

16. Add any soil amendments (compost, etc) and rake them into the top 4 or 5 inches (10.2 or 12.7 cm) of soil.

Add organic matter like compost or aged manure to enrich the soil.

17. Make rows, usually about 3 feet (0.9 m) apart in your new "garden", and if you are using container-grown seedlings, dig holes for each one.

Plant in rows, leaving enough space between each plant to allow for growth.

18. Place your plant in the hole, keeping the bottom of the stem level or slightly lower than the soil around it.

Make sure the plant is level with the soil and has room to grow.

19. Dig rows for seeds if you have bought them.

If planting seeds, create rows and plant them at the recommended depth and spacing.

20. Drop the seeds into this furrow at the spacing recommended on the seed package.

Plant seeds as directed on the seed packet.

21. Water your new plants or seed beds lightly, and fairly often, depending on existing soil moisture for the first week or two.

Newly planted plants and seeds will need frequent watering until they establish themselves.

22. Watch your new plants for healthy appearance.

Check your plants regularly for signs of disease or pest infestation.

23. Keep an eye out for predators.

Keep an eye out for pests like aphids or caterpillars, and take action if there's a problem.

24. Keep the ground around your plants clean and free of weeds as much as you are able.

Weeds can compete with your plants for nutrients and water, so keep them under control.

25. Keep some kind of records of your efforts.

Take notes on what you plant, how you care for your plants, and what kind of pests or diseases you encounter. This will help you learn from your experiences and improve your gardening skills over time.

In conclusion, choosing plants for a garden requires careful thought and planning. By considering your interests, your environment, and the needs of your plants, you can create a beautiful and productive garden that you'll enjoy for years to come.

Sewing an Amish Puzzle Ball is a fun and challenging sewing project that results in a unique and playful toy. It is made up of 12 wedges that are sewn together to create a ball shape. The wedges are arranged in such a way that they can be taken apart and reassembled like a puzzle. This toy is great for children and adults alike, as it can be both entertaining and educational. In this article, we will go through the step-by-step process of sewing an Amish Puzzle Ball.

Obtain about a quarter yard (22.8cm) of closely woven fabric.

The first step in sewing an Amish Puzzle Ball is to obtain the necessary materials. You will need a quarter yard of closely woven fabric. Choose a fabric that is sturdy and can withstand wear and tear. You will also need stuffing material and a sewing machine or needle and thread.

Cut 36 football shapes (American football, not soccer!)

Using a football-shaped template or a freehand drawing, cut out 36 football shapes from your fabric. Be sure to cut them precisely for uniform wedges later on.

With "right sides", or patterned side, of the fabric together, stitch two pieces together along one edge.

Take two of the football shapes that you have cut and place them together with the patterned side facing inwards. Sew the two pieces together along one of the edges. Repeat this step with all 36 shapes.

Fold the current pair open as shown.

Once you have stitched two pieces together, open them up and fold the current pair open. This will allow you to stitch the third piece onto the opening.

Place the third piece over the opening, with edges aligned.

Take the third football shape and place it over the opening created by the first two shapes. Align the edges of the pieces and pin them together.

Unfold the remaining edge and sew half a side of it to the third piece (leaving an opening for stuffing).

Unfold the remaining edge of the first two pieces and sew half of that side to the third piece. Leave an opening for stuffing.

Sew all 12 wedges in a similar manner.

Repeat this process with all 36 shapes. You should end up with 12 wedges in total.

Clip the points of the wedges away before turning to prevent bunching.

Before turning the wedges inside out, clip the points away to prevent bunching.

Turn the seams to the middle and the "right" or patterned side out.

Turn the wedges inside out, so that the seams are in the middle and the patterned side is facing outwards.

Stuff firmly with batting and stitch closed carefully.

Stuff each wedge firmly with batting and stitch the opening closed carefully.

Attach the wedges to each other by their end points.

Take two of the wedges and attach them to each other by their end points. Repeat this process with all 12 wedges.

Form a triangle from each group of three wedges by sewing their points together end to end.

Form a triangle from each group of three wedges by sewing their points together end-to-end. Repeat this process with all four triangles.

Join each wedge triangle to another group at the points, forming a cross or "X" shape.

Join each wedge triangle to another group at the points, forming a cross or "X" shape. This should create a ball shape.

When all points are sewn together, the wedges will resemble a ball in shape.

When you have sewn all the wedges together, you should have a ball shape.

Note that the ball can "change" colors, by turning the wedges so that a different cloth faces out.

One of the unique features of the Amish Puzzle Ball is that it can "change" colors by turning the wedges so that a different cloth faces out. This creates a new and fun look for the toy.

In conclusion, sewing an Amish Puzzle Ball is a fun and engaging project that can result in a unique and entertaining toy. With careful attention to detail and precision sewing, anyone can create a beautiful and functional Amish Puzzle Ball.

For best results, it's ideal to start at least two weeks before the concert you want to attend is scheduled, and the longer in advance you prepare, the better.;
, Write down (or print out) where the concert is taking place, how much it costs to go, when it is happening, and who is performing.

, The more they know about it, the more comfortable they will feel about you being there.

, However, discuss the concert with your folks before you buy the ticket, because if they say no after you've bought one, then you might be stuck with an expensive ticket you can't even use!

, Get clear written directions if you have never been there before. The important thing is who will be driving you there and back. , (Make sure the driver knows this too!) It may seem dorky, but consider asking your parents to drive you. Riding a bus or other public transportation would work well too.

, You're safer together than you would be alone.

, The concert is your time to have fun, but consider choosing an outfit that is reasonably modest and low-key. Your parents don't want you to attract "the wrong kind of attention." Remember, the music will be just as good no matter what you're wearing.

Using a sharp knife, decide what kind of caulk is already in your tub or shower area. When you cut into the caulk, if it is soft and rubbery it is most likely silicone based; however, if the caulk is hard and crumbly then it is most likely water based, latex or PVA.
Determine the best manner of removal based on caulk type. Silicone based caulk may be removed using a razor blade scraper with a plastic handle, and the other types are removed best when warmed using an ever-moving hair dryer on a low heat temperature and then scraped using the tool.

, This will help to get rid of any remaining bits and pieces of caulk left after the scraping.

,, Let this sit and take effect against the mold and mildew for at least 5 minutes before scrubbing down with fresh, clean water.

, You do not want the dampness that crept into the joints when the previous caulk was loose, or the dampness that resulted from the cleaning you have just done, to impact the ability of your new caulk to adhere and stay in place.

, PVA and acrylic latex are best with ceramic fixtures that meet tile because the caulk is hard and easily removed in the long run. Silicone based caulk is best with fiberglass fixtures meeting tile or other fiberglass surrounds because the caulk is relatively soft when it dries.

, Make sure not to make the hole in the tip too large because that will control how much caulk comes out at once, and a smaller tip will help your caulking to be a success.

Others argue that this may just add potential moisture in the area that can effect the caulking and so the tub should be empty. A properly installed tub moves very little so which ever method you are comfortable with will work.

How to Come in Contact With the Father You Have Never Met

It is never too late to begin searching for the father you have never met. Finding your father can be a difficult and emotional journey, but it can also bring closure and a sense of connection to your roots. Here are some helpful steps to take when searching for your father.

Search for your father. 

The first step in finding your father is to gather as much information about him as possible. Start by talking to your mother, grandparents, or anyone who may have known your father. Try to gather any details you can, like his name, place of birth, and any other pertinent information.

Research relevant state or national laws.

Depending on where you live, there may be laws that restrict access to information about your father. Research state or national laws to see what your options are.

Search adoption or reunion registries.

If your father was adopted, you may be able to find him through an adoption registry. Some states also have reunion registries that can help you find your biological family.

Talk to relatives in order to assemble information about your father.

Relatives can be a great source of information about your father, so be sure to reach out to them. Ask about any stories or information they may have about your father, and use this to guide your search.

Hire a professional or volunteer searcher.

Finding your father can be a daunting task, but there are professionals and volunteers who can help. Consider enlisting the help of a private investigator or utilizing services from organizations that specialize in helping people locate lost family members.

Decide whether you want to meet your father.

It is important to take some time to think about whether or not you want to meet your father. This can be an emotional experience, so take some time to prepare yourself.

Prepare yourself emotionally.

Meeting your father for the first time can be an emotional experience. It is important to prepare yourself and be prepared for any potential outcomes.

Think about what you hope to get out of meeting your father.

It’s important to have realistic expectations about what you hope to achieve by meeting your father. Avoid fantasizing about your “ideal” father, and instead focus on what you hope to gain from the experience.

Don’t share too much too soon.

It’s important to take things slow when meeting your father. Don’t divulge too much information about yourself too soon, as this may make things overwhelming for both of you.

Arrange a meeting with your father.

When you feel ready to meet your father, arrange a meeting in a comfortable and neutral location. This could be a park, coffee shop, or other public place.

Ask questions.

When you meet your father, ask questions about his life and his experiences. This can help you understand him better and build a connection.

Don’t make grand plans for the future.

It’s important to take things slow and avoid making grand plans for the future. This can cause stress and anxiety, so take things one step at a time.

Set up a support system for yourself.

Meeting your father can be an emotional experience, so be sure to set up a support system for yourself. This could include friends, family, or a therapist.

Don’t let a disappointing first meeting define the relationship.

If your first meeting with your father doesn’t go as planned, try not to give up hope. It takes time to build a relationship, so give it time.

Recognize that there may be a “honeymoon” phase.

After meeting your father, there may be a honeymoon phase where everything feels perfect. Recognize that this may not last, and that relationships take work.

Establish limits about each other’s lives.

It’s important to establish limits about each other’s lives. This can help both of you feel more comfortable and avoid any potential conflicts.

Beginning with minimal expectations can help you both to build a stronger, more enduring relationship.

It’s important to take things one step at a time and avoid making grand plans for the future. By beginning with minimal expectations, you can build a stronger and more enduring relationship with your father.

Allow time for the relationship to develop.

Building a relationship takes time and effort, so allow yourself and your father time for the relationship to develop.

Accept the relationship may not deepen or may not last.

Although reconnection often carries great benefits, some people find that they do not want a lasting relationship with their father after all. It’s important to accept this possibility and be prepared for it.

Don’t neglect your childhood family.

While it’s important to seek out your father, it’s also important to maintain your relationships with your childhood family. Keep in touch and continue to build these relationships as well.

How to Be a Good Girl

Being a good girl means different things to different people. However, some general rules apply: be kind, considerate, and respectful to everyone. Being good is not just about being nice to people, but also about taking good care of yourself and embodying qualities that reflect your values. Here is how to be a good girl:

1. Become your own best friend.

Being your own best friend means knowing what you want and treating yourself with respect and love. Take care of yourself, eat well, exercise, and get enough rest. Love yourself and take time to do things that make you happy.

2. Wear what makes you comfortable.

Wear what makes you feel good and confident, whether it's jeans and a t-shirt or a fancy dress. Don't try to fit in with others; wear what you like.

3. Take good care of your body.

Eat well, exercise, and get enough rest. Your body is your temple, and it's important to take good care of it. Drink plenty of water and avoid unhealthy habits like smoking and drinking alcohol.

4. Make the most out of your free time.

Use your free time wisely. Find something you enjoy doing, like reading, playing sports, or writing. Learn a new skill.

5. Celebrate your mind.

Embrace your intelligence and keep learning. Take advantage of every opportunity to learn new things, whether it's in school or through independent study.

6. Be mindful of your media exposure.

Limit your exposure to negative media, like violent movies or TV shows. Focus on positive messages that inspire, motivate, and uplift you.

7. Let yourself struggle sometimes.

Life is full of challenges, and it's okay to struggle. Learn from your mistakes and keep moving forward.

8. Find inspiration for being good.

Find people who embody the qualities you admire and try to emulate them. Seek out good role models and learn from their actions and words.

9. Respect and listen to your parent(s).

Show your parents respect and listen to them. They have your best interests at heart.

10. Offer help at home.

Help out around the house by doing chores and lending a hand when needed.

11. Learn helpful skills.

Learn new skills that can help you in your everyday life, like cooking, budgeting, or time management.

12. Keep your space clean.

Take pride in your living space and keep it clean and organized.

13. Be kind to your sibling(s).

Be generous with your siblings and show them love and respect.

14. Be nice to your classmates and to your teacher.

Treat others with kindness and respect, especially in social settings like school.

15. Get good grades.

Try your best in school and aim for good grades.

16. Do your homework every night.

Stay organized and manage your time well by doing your homework every evening.

17. Break up your studying time.

Study in short, focused sessions and take breaks in between to avoid burnout.

18. Be respectful during class.

Be attentive and respectful during class and participate when asked to.

19. Ask for help if you're overwhelmed.

Reach out to teachers, counselors, or parents when you're feeling overwhelmed.

20. Be kind to other people.

Show kindness to others, even if they are different from you.

21. Be clear when you want something.

Be clear about your wants and needs and communicate them respectfully.

22. Learn to be a great listener.

Be present and attentive when others are talking to you and show you're listening by responding appropriately.

23. Practice random acts of kindness.

Do something kind for someone else, without any expectation of receiving anything in return.

24. When you think a kind thought, say it out loud.

Speak positively about others and let them know you appreciate them.

25. Look for the best in everyone.

Focus on the good in others and avoid negative thoughts or judgments.

26. Treat everyone with respect, including those who are different from you.

Embrace diversity and show respect to people from all walks of life.

27. Be polite.

Show good manners, especially in social settings like school or in public.

28. Practice patience when you don't understand.

Be patient with yourself and others when you're trying to learn something new.

29. If you get angry, defuse the situation or leave.

Don't let anger consume you. Find a way to defuse the situation or remove yourself from it if necessary.

30. Surround yourself with people who make you feel happy.

Choose friends and relationships that make you feel good about yourself.

31. Be mindful about your romantic relationships.

Make sure you're in a healthy and respectful relationship, and avoid relationships that are harmful to yourself or others.

32. Show people how much you appreciate them.

Thank people for their kindness and show gratitude for the good things in your life.

Don't tug desperately at the reins; this will just scare him/her even more. Firmly pull back, then release, being careful not to lean backwards yourself. Keep repeating the pull and release. , This will make it harder for you to fall. Do this by putting your legs forward and leaning back slightly. Falling from a gallop can be very dangerous, so you should avoid it at all costs. Keep talking to your horse and asking them to stop while you do this.

,,, Just because the horse is acting up doesn't mean you want them hurt. Jump like you normally would, but as soon as you've landed, steer away from the jump. ,, If the horse continues then repeat until he stops.

How to Find out if You Are Too Clingy

If you have a tendency to cling to your loved ones, it can be tough to recognize when you're crossing a line. However, being too clingy can be a turn-off to your friends or partner and put a strain on your relationships. In this article, we will discuss the signs that you may be too clingy and what you can do to create a healthier dynamic in your relationships.

1. Recognize when you reveal too much too soon.

If you’re clingy, you might want to get everything about your feelings or your life off your chest right away because you fear that the person you feel very clingy toward might abandon you at any moment. However, oversharing can put a strain on your relationships and make you feel more anxious about the situation.

2. Identify an inability to make decisions.

Clingy people will want to make the “right” decision – that is, the decision which they believe will please and win the affections of the person they are clinging to. If you find yourself unable to make decisions without your friend or partner's input, it may be a sign that you're relying too heavily on them.

3. Search your feelings for fear of separation from someone.

People who are too clingy may fear being separated from their loved ones, leading them to become overly attached. This fear can cause you to act irrationally or impulsively and put a strain on your relationships.

4. Look for highs and lows in your relationships.

In other words, look for cycles of emotional boom and bust, in which you and your friend or partner get along really well for a long stretch, and nothing can go wrong. But then things seem to take a nasty turn for the worse, for days on end. This can be a sign that you're too clingy and your partner needs some space.

5. Ask your friend if you are too clingy.

The best way to know for sure if you are being too clingy is to ask your friend or partner if they feel that you are being too clingy. It may be difficult to hear their answer, but it's important to listen to what they have to say.

6. Listen to what your friend has to say.

A friend or partner who asks to limit your time together and wants to institute more stringent boundaries is, in a way, telling you that you’re being a bit overbearing. If they ask for some space, take this as a sign that you need to tone down your clinginess.

7. Identify behavior in your friend or partner which indicates they are unable to develop deep bonds.

If your friend or partner seems to have trouble forming deep connections with people, it may be a sign that they are not interested in a deep emotional connection. As hard as it may be, it's important to respect their boundaries.

8. Familiarize yourself with stories in which characters support and care for each other.

Sometimes, in childhood, we fail to form secure attachments. Reading or watching stories about healthy relationships can help you identify what a healthy relationship looks like and aspire towards it.

9. Spend time on your own hobbies.

In order to break yourself of clinging to one individual, distract yourself with some healthy hobbies. By finding activities that you enjoy, you can take some of your focus off of your relationship and onto something that brings you joy.

10. Seek therapeutic treatment.

Psychotherapy is the best option to tackle your dependent behavior. A therapist can help you identify the underlying causes of your clinginess and work with you to develop better coping mechanisms and strategies.

11. Try medication.

Your therapist can prescribe medication for specific symptoms in cases where your clingy behavior constitutes an actual dependent personality disorder. While medication is not a cure, it can help alleviate some of the symptoms associated with overdependence.

12. Acknowledge your feelings but don’t react negatively.

Feeling clingy is a normal response to feeling insecure or anxious about a relationship. However, acting out on these feelings, such as by becoming jealous or possessive, can be harmful and damaging to your relationships.

13. Understand why you’re clingy.

By understanding your motivations for being clingy, you can work on addressing the underlying issues and improving your behavior in your relationships.

14. Be patient with yourself and with your friend or partner.

Breaking ingrained patterns of behavior takes time and effort. Similarly, your friend or partner may need some time to adjust to the changes you're making to the dynamic of the relationship.

15. Spend time with other friends.

Being too clingy with one person means you've neglected other people in your life who care about you. By spending time with other friends and family members, you can regain a sense of balance in your life and feel less dependent on one person.

16. Accept the boundaries your friend or partner draws between you.

Your friend or partner may need to set boundaries in the relationship to maintain their own mental health and well-being. It's important to respect these boundaries and work on finding a balance that works for both of you.

17. Use imagery to envision a healthy relationship.

Thinking about a secure interpersonal relationship can help both you and the other party become more comfortable with and trusting of each other. By visualizing a healthy, balanced relationship, you can work towards realizing that vision in your own life.

This does not mean being unrealistic, or saying you're going to go professional within a year. Instead, you should take a look at what you are, and figure out where you think you would like to be. Give yourself a suitable amount of time to achieve that goal, and break it down into smaller parts if the goal is especially big.;
, Being good at sports means more than physical strength and speed. In order to be truly great, you need to apply that positive attitude to the way you treat other players, even if they're on the other team. If you lose a match, accept the loss and show respect to the other player for their victory.If you set an example of good sportsmanship, you'll receive the same respect in turn when you win.

, No matter what you do, skills are often slow to build. There is always going to be someone who can do it better than you, at least at first. Impatient athletes tend to make poor ones. If you're not patient, you will try to leap into more advanced techniques long before you're ready. Your motivation will sap away when you don't see improvement immediately. Keep the long-term goal in mind, and hang tight in the meantime while you make it a reality.

, When you play sports, you will inevitably receive criticism. Whether from a coach, other players, or spectators, you need to take most of it with a grain of salt. Are they angry because you missed a pass, or do they sincerely want to help you improve? Learn to distinguish the constructive criticism from the hurtful remarks. In a lot of cases, you can use criticism as motivation to get better in whatever area they bring up.


Don't let yourself get defensive.Your thinking becomes more limited if you allow yourself to become emotional against criticism.

, One of the biggest reasons people join sports teams in the first place is to make friends with new people.If you join a team, you're bound to run into a lot of people. Chances are you'll develop friendships with at least some of them. Making these friendships a priority is a good move if you want to become great at sports. You can practice together on your own time. The morale boost of playing with friends helps as well.


Team sports like soccer can have some skills practiced in solitude, but for others (like goalkeeping and passing) another person is needed. It's helpful to have this other person as a friend. It'll make the practice process more enjoyable in the long term.

, It's possible to get so obsessed with being good at something that you lose track of why you want to be good in the first place. Not taking the time to enjoy the sport you play results in a fast burnout. Whether you're practicing or playing a competition, try to remember the other reasons why you play sports. This may include the natural satisfaction you get from exercise, or quality time spent with friends., If you want to become a sports superstar, joining a team is a good way to start. Even if your skills are currently low, joining an amateur league will sharpen up your skills. Sports teams are everywhere, and most of them are free to join. If you go to school, you should go for team tryouts when they're happening. Recreation centres are often host to sports teams as well.If you're not in school, you can find or start a sports club online., Great coaches come in all shapes and sizes.Certain coaching personalities will suit you better than others. The best scenario is having a coach who genuinely wants to see you succeed. In the early stages, enthusiasm is often more helpful than scientific know-how.


Across the board, communication skills are the most important trait a coach can have.There are different levels of sports coach. Most after-school coaches are volunteers with a working knowledge of the sport and enthusiasm for the game. You can hire a sports coach with full training and education, although it will set you back a lot of money., If you want to be truly great at sports, it's not enough to focus on a couple of sports alone. It is important to set your sights wide with your exercises. Don't specialize in skills unless you're already a great athlete. Find ways to train every part of your body. Whether this means playing multiple sports or doing an all-encompassing exercise routine, training your entire body will boost your sports performance.


There's a lot of evidence to suggest that athletes are better if they play more than one sport., it's a common misgiving for young athletes to want to jump into the advanced stuff quickly. This isn't an effective way to spend training time. You need a solid bedrock foundation before you move on to bigger things.If you're new to a sport, take as much time as you need to get the basic movements down properly. It will make advanced skills a lot easier to grasp in the long run.


Some argue that fundamental "movement skills" (such as jumping and kicking) should be mastered properly before moving on to applied sports skills., In an actual competition, things aren't usually going to play exactly by the books. While you're practicing, you'll usually be playing under optimal circumstances. In order to make sure you'll be up for the unforeseen challenges, you need to predict the conditions of the competition.Ask yourself if you are learning a skill, or if you're learning to actually use that skill in the competitions you'll be in.


Remember this motto: "Train the way you want to play."There's no way to perfectly replicate a competition in practice, but playing with other people can get you used to the scenarios you'll need to beware of.

, It is common for bodies to adapt to a level of strain. Progress will slow down if you don't keep upping the stakes as you go along. Bodybuilders and strength-oriented athletes do this by increasing their reps or the weights they work with. As a competition athlete, the best thing you can do to keep progress going is to practice the skills while under fatigue.Studies have shown that skill execution goes out the window when you get tired, so it's a good idea to practice under those conditions.


Building up your speed is important too. Speed usually comes with practice, but you shouldn't try to rush into speed until you have the basics down pat., If you're wondering at what point a skill becomes mastered, it is when you're able to do it automatically and without thinking. This is called the autonomous phase, and it's where you want to be at if you want to play sports competitively.Given enough time and repetition, a skill will eventually fall under this category. When you're in the fray of a game, you won't have time to think everything through, so practice until it's all automatic, so you'll know you're ready.


When you're playing a sport like football in a competition, you're going to potentially need to throw a ball while getting chased by several competitors. You can't replicate this stress in private practice, so you have to make sure your tosses perfected beforehand.
Keep practicing. Practice is never something that should stop. even if you become great at sports, you can always become better. If you stop practicing, chances are that someone more determined will beat you.

, The best athletes know that training doesn't stop at sports skills. You want your body to be as sharp as it can be for whatever sport you're playing. On the downtime away from sports, keep your body in general shape by going to the gym. Although it may seem daunting or expensive, it is absolutely worth the investment if you want to be good at sports. There are lots of benefits to going to a gym. As you'll know from playing in sports teams, exercising with others is very motivating.Do some research into a prospective gym before you pay for a membership. Go for a tour, and ask for details. Make sure the gym fits the circumstances of your life before you make your first payment., This is especially true during times of intense practice or training, where things get so busy it becomes hard to cram everything into a regular day. Nonetheless, your body needs its full rest. At least 6 hours is good, but 8 will ensure your body's had its full time to regenerate.

, Everyone should make a point of eating a diet that benefits their body. This is especially true if you want to be great at sports. Eating junk food will work against whatever efforts you make n the gym. Base your diet on leafy greens and legumes.Cut back on "empty calories" (like soda) and replace them with things that improve your health.


If you're lactose intolerant, try going without dairy. It may sound hard, but you'll see benefits within a month.

, As a general rule, the more you drink it, the better you'll feel. Water regulates everything, and you should expect to lose some of your natural hydration via sweat while you exercise. The common "8 glasses a day" guideline isn't mandatory, but you should try to keep water next to you whenever you can. It's especially important to stock up on water while you're exercising.


Keep a refillable bottle of water around with you. Refill it when it goes empty. You'll find your water consumption goes up a lot if you simply keep it near you.

, Drugs and alcohol aren't recommended if you want to be great at sports. Alcohol is a diuretic, which means it drains your body of hydration.Your body will expend resources trying to get rid of the alcohol, and it can have a negative impact on your sports performance for days after the fact.


In the long run, the amount of calories you digest from alcohol like cider and beer can have a negative impact on your gut., The competition will be stiff enough, so it's not worth it losing your edge just because you weren't willing to sleep at least 8 hours.

, While it wouldn't be regularly recommended in a diet, athletes should load up on their carbohydrate intake.Carbs essentially give your body energy, and you'll need lots of energy if you're playing a sports competition.


Stay away from sugar for a few hours before a big competition. Sugar and starches dehydrate your body. That is something you want to avoid in the midst of competing.Keep yourself up to pace with snacks.Longer matches will be a test of endurance, and something as simple as an energy bar can mean a big difference.

, Warm ups are important for any physically strenuous activity. They can be fairly light, but a proper warm up will help prevent early exhaustion and injury. Aim to warm up around half an hour before the start of the game.Stretch out your arms and legs. Run in place. Work up a bit of a sweat. This will get your body in the right mode for the competition.


Warm ups also help fight off anxiety. Pre-game anxiety can be an issue for some players, so you should keep that in mind if it's something you suffer from.

, Having a good idea what to expect with the opposing side is important, regardless whether you're going one on one or playing as a team. If you want to know which techniques you should be using in the midst of certain competitors, it's a good idea to study their methods in the days and weeks before a big game. If there is any footage of those players during a game, give it a shot.


The science of analytics turns the respective skills of your teammates and competitors into a sharp formula.Matching each player to their best strengths is integral to a team's success. Professional sports analysts make it their specific business to analyze the sporting behaviour of athletes.

, Life is always complicated, and there may be things in your personal life that are bothering you. However, at least for the course of the game, you can't let any of that stuff get to you. It may be easier said than done, but if winning the game means enough to you, it should be a straightforward matter to keep your eyes on the prize.

, A lot of the best athletes may have had a natural aptitude for it, but the reason they ultimately succeed is because they want victory more than any of the competition. That's a tricky thing to develop inside yourself, but if the desire is intense enough, you will do just about anything to make your dreams a reality. This broadly applies to the mindset of training, but it's incredibly important during a competition. How much you want to win will have an effect on how far you end up pushing yourself. Sometimes, the distance between winning and losing is very small. A world of difference is made from that added bit of effort. Passion is the key to most kinds of success, and the same applies to sports.
